0x00000000.name: ENABLED
0x00000000.desc: clock generator is enabled

0x00000000.name: NUDGE
0x00000000.desc:
An edge on this signal shifts the phase of the output by 1 cycle of the input clock 
                            This can be done at any time

0x00000000.name: PHASE
0x00000000.desc:
This delays the enable signal by up to 3 cycles of the input clock 
                            This must be set before the clock is enabled to have any effect

0x00000000.name: DC50
0x00000000.desc: Enables duty cycle correction for odd divisors, can be changed on-the-fly

0x00000000.name: ENABLE
0x00000000.desc: Starts and stops the clock generator cleanly

0x00000000.name: KILL
0x00000000.desc: Asynchronously kills the clock generator, enable must be set low before deasserting kill

0x00000000.name: AUXSRC
0x00000000.desc: Selects the auxiliary clock source, will glitch when switching

0x00000004.name: INT
0x00000004.desc: Integer part of clock divisor, 0 -> max+1, can be changed on-the-fly

0x00000004.name: FRAC
0x00000004.desc: Fractional component of the divisor, can be changed on-the-fly

0x00000008.name: CLK_GPOUT0_SELECTED
0x00000008.desc: This slice does not have a glitchless mux (only the AUX_SRC field is present, not SRC) so this register is hardwired to 0x1.

0x00000034.name: INT

0x00000038.name: CLK_REF_SELECTED
0x00000038.desc: The glitchless multiplexer does not switch instantaneously (to avoid glitches), so software should poll this register to wait for the switch to complete. This register contains one decoded bit for each of the clock sources enumerated in the CTRL SRC field. At most one of these bits will be set at any time, indicating that clock is currently present at the output of the glitchless mux. Whilst switching is in progress, this register may briefly show all-0s.

0x0000003c.name: SRC
0x0000003c.desc: Selects the clock source glitchlessly, can be changed on-the-fly

0x00000084.name: CLEAR
0x00000084.desc: For clearing the resus after the fault that triggered it has been corrected

0x00000084.name: FRCE
0x00000084.desc: Force a resus, for test purposes only

0x00000084.name: ENABLE
0x00000084.desc: Enable resus

0x00000084.name: TIMEOUT
0x00000084.desc:
This is expressed as a number of clk_ref cycles 
                            and must be >= 2x clk_ref_freq/min_clk_tst_freq

0x00000088.name: RESUSSED
0x00000088.desc: Clock has been resuscitated, correct the error then send ctrl_clear=1

0x000000a4.name: DIED
0x000000a4.desc: Test clock stopped during test

0x000000a4.name: FAST
0x000000a4.desc: Test clock faster than expected, only valid when status_done=1

0x000000a4.name: SLOW
0x000000a4.desc: Test clock slower than expected, only valid when status_done=1

0x000000a4.name: FAIL
0x000000a4.desc: Test failed

0x000000a4.name: WAITING
0x000000a4.desc: Waiting for test clock to start

0x000000a4.name: RUNNING
0x000000a4.desc: Test running

0x000000a4.name: DONE
0x000000a4.desc: Test complete

0x000000a4.name: PASS
0x000000a4.desc: Test passed

0x00000000.name: BSWAP
0x00000000.desc:
Enable byte swapping of 32-bit values at the point they are committed to the SHA message scheduler.

                            This block's bus interface assembles byte/halfword data into message words in little-endian order, so that DMAing the same buffer with different transfer sizes always gives the same result on a little-endian system like RP2350.

                            However, when marshalling bytes into blocks, SHA expects that the first byte is the *most significant* in each message word. To resolve this, once the bus interface has accumulated 32 bits of data (either a word write, two halfword writes in little-endian order, or four byte writes in little-endian order) the final value can be byte-swapped before passing to the actual SHA core.

                            This feature is enabled by default because using the SHA core to checksum byte buffers is expected to be more common than having preformatted SHA message words lying around.

0x00000000.name: DMA_SIZE
0x00000000.desc:
Configure DREQ logic for the correct DMA data size. Must be configured before the DMA channel is triggered.

                            The SHA-256 core's DREQ logic requests one entire block of data at once, since there is no FIFO, and data goes straight into the core's message schedule and digest hardware. Therefore, when transferring data with DMA, CSR_DMA_SIZE must be configured in advance so that the correct number of transfers can be requested per block.

0x00000000.name: ERR_WDATA_NOT_RDY
0x00000000.desc: Set when a write occurs whilst the SHA-256 core is not ready for data (WDATA_RDY is low). Write one to clear.

0x00000000.name: SUM_VLD
0x00000000.desc:
If 1, the SHA-256 checksum presented in registers SUM0 through SUM7 is currently valid.

                            Goes low when WDATA is first written, then returns high once 16 words have been written and the digest of the current 512-bit block has subsequently completed.

0x00000000.name: WDATA_RDY
0x00000000.desc:
If 1, the SHA-256 core is ready to accept more data through the WDATA register.

                            After writing 16 words, this flag will go low for 57 cycles whilst the core completes its digest.

0x00000000.name: START
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to prepare the SHA-256 core for a new checksum.

                            The SUMx registers are initialised to the proper values (fractional bits of square roots of first 8 primes) and internal counters are cleared. This immediately forces WDATA_RDY and SUM_VLD high.

                            START must be written before initiating a DMA transfer to the SHA-256 core, because the core will always request 16 transfers at a time (1 512-bit block). Additionally, the DMA channel should be configured for a multiple of 16 32-bit transfers.

0x00000004.name: WDATA
0x00000004.desc:
After pulsing START and writing 16 words of data to this register, WDATA_RDY will go low and the SHA-256 core will complete the digest of the current 512-bit block.

                            Software is responsible for ensuring the data is correctly padded and terminated to a whole number of 512-bit blocks.

                            After this, WDATA_RDY will return high, and more data can be written (if any).

                            This register supports word, halfword and byte writes, so that DMA from non-word-aligned buffers can be supported. The total amount of data per block remains the same (16 words, 32 halfwords or 64 bytes) and byte/halfword transfers must not be mixed within a block.

0x00000000.name: WOF
0x00000000.desc: FIFO was written when full. Write 1 to clear.

0x00000000.name: CLKDIV
0x00000000.desc:
Clock period of the generated clock, measured in HSTX clock cycles. Can be odd or even. The generated clock advances only on cycles where the shift register shifts.

                            For example, a clkdiv of 5 would generate a complete output clock period for every 5 HSTX clocks (or every 10 half-clocks).

                            A CLKDIV value of 0 is mapped to a period of 16 HSTX clock cycles.

0x00000000.name: CLKPHASE
0x00000000.desc:
Set the initial phase of the generated clock.

                            A CLKPHASE of 0 means the clock is initially low, and the first rising edge occurs after one half period of the generated clock (i.e. CLKDIV/2 cycles of clk_hstx). Incrementing CLKPHASE by 1 will advance the initial clock phase by one half clk_hstx period. For example, if CLKDIV=2 and CLKPHASE=1:

                            * The clock will be initially low

                            * The first rising edge will be 0.5 clk_hstx cycles after asserting first data

                            * The first falling edge will be 1.5 clk_hstx cycles after asserting first data

                            This configuration would be suitable for serialising at a bit rate of clk_hstx with a centre-aligned DDR clock.

                            When the HSTX is halted by clearing CSR_EN, the clock generator will return to its initial phase as configured by the CLKPHASE field.

                            Note CLKPHASE must be strictly less than double the value of CLKDIV (one full period), else its operation is undefined.

0x00000000.name: N_SHIFTS
0x00000000.desc:
Number of times to shift the shift register before refilling it from the FIFO. (A count of how many times it has been shifted, *not* the total shift distance.)

                            A register value of 0 means shift 32 times.

0x00000000.name: SHIFT
0x00000000.desc:
How many bits to right-rotate the shift register by each cycle.

                            The use of a rotate rather than a shift allows left shifts to be emulated, by subtracting the left-shift amount from 32. It also allows data to be repeated, when the product of SHIFT and N_SHIFTS is greater than 32.

0x00000000.name: COUPLED_SEL
0x00000000.desc: Select which PIO to use for coupled mode operation.

0x00000000.name: COUPLED_MODE
0x00000000.desc:
Enable the PIO-to-HSTX 1:1 connection. The HSTX must be clocked *directly* from the system clock (not just from some other clock source of the same frequency) for this synchronous interface to function correctly.

                            When COUPLED_MODE is set, BITx_SEL_P and SEL_N indices 24 through 31 will select bits from the 8-bit PIO-to-HSTX path, rather than shifter bits. Indices of 0 through 23 will still index the shift register as normal.

                            The PIO outputs connected to the PIO-to-HSTX bus are those same outputs that would appear on the HSTX-capable pins if those pins' FUNCSELs were set to PIO instead of HSTX.

                            For example, if HSTX is on GPIOs 12 through 19, then PIO outputs 12 through 19 are connected to the HSTX when coupled mode is engaged.

0x00000000.name: EXPAND_EN
0x00000000.desc:
Enable the command expander. When 0, raw FIFO data is passed directly to the output shift register. When 1, the command expander can perform simple operations such as run length decoding on data between the FIFO and the shift register.

                            Do not change CXPD_EN whilst EN is set. It's safe to set CXPD_EN simultaneously with setting EN.

0x00000000.name: EN
0x00000000.desc:
When EN is 1, the HSTX will shift out data as it appears in the FIFO. As long as there is data, the HSTX shift register will shift once per clock cycle, and the frequency of popping from the FIFO is determined by the ratio of SHIFT and SHIFT_THRESH.

                            When EN is 0, the FIFO is not popped. The shift counter and clock generator are also reset to their initial state for as long as EN is low. Note the initial phase of the clock generator can be configured by the CLKPHASE field.

                            Once the HSTX is enabled again, and data is pushed to the FIFO, the generated clock's first rising edge will be one half-period after the first data is launched.

0x00000004.name: CLK
0x00000004.desc: Connect this output to the generated clock, rather than the data shift register. SEL_P and SEL_N are ignored if this bit is set, but INV can still be set to generate an antiphase clock.

0x00000004.name: INV
0x00000004.desc: Invert this data output (logical NOT)

0x00000004.name: SEL_N
0x00000004.desc: Shift register data bit select for the second half of the HSTX clock cycle

0x00000004.name: SEL_P
0x00000004.desc: Shift register data bit select for the first half of the HSTX clock cycle

0x00000024.name: ENC_N_SHIFTS
0x00000024.desc: Number of times to consume from the shift register before refilling it from the FIFO, when the current command is an encoded data command (e.g. TMDS). A register value of 0 means shift 32 times.

0x00000024.name: ENC_SHIFT
0x00000024.desc: How many bits to right-rotate the shift register by each time data is pushed to the output shifter, when the current command is an encoded data command (e.g. TMDS).

0x00000024.name: RAW_N_SHIFTS
0x00000024.desc: Number of times to consume from the shift register before refilling it from the FIFO, when the current command is a raw data command. A register value of 0 means shift 32 times.

0x00000024.name: RAW_SHIFT
0x00000024.desc: How many bits to right-rotate the shift register by each time data is pushed to the output shifter, when the current command is a raw data command.

0x00000028.name: L2_NBITS
0x00000028.desc: Number of valid data bits for the lane 2 TMDS encoder, starting from bit 7 of the rotated data. Field values of 0 -> 7 encode counts of 1 -> 8 bits.

0x00000028.name: L2_ROT
0x00000028.desc: Right-rotate applied to the current shifter data before the lane 2 TMDS encoder.

0x00000028.name: L1_NBITS
0x00000028.desc: Number of valid data bits for the lane 1 TMDS encoder, starting from bit 7 of the rotated data. Field values of 0 -> 7 encode counts of 1 -> 8 bits.

0x00000028.name: L1_ROT
0x00000028.desc: Right-rotate applied to the current shifter data before the lane 1 TMDS encoder.

0x00000028.name: L0_NBITS
0x00000028.desc: Number of valid data bits for the lane 0 TMDS encoder, starting from bit 7 of the rotated data. Field values of 0 -> 7 encode counts of 1 -> 8 bits.

0x00000028.name: L0_ROT
0x00000028.desc: Right-rotate applied to the current shifter data before the lane 0 TMDS encoder.

0x00000008.name: WICENACK
0x00000008.desc: Status signal from the processor's interrupt controller. Changes to WICENREQ are eventually reflected in WICENACK.

0x00000008.name: WICENREQ
0x00000008.desc: Request that the next processor deep sleep is a WIC sleep. After setting this bit, before sleeping, poll WICENACK to ensure the processor interrupt controller has acknowledged the change.

0x00000008.name: LIGHT_SLEEP
0x00000008.desc:
By default, any processor sleep will deassert the system-level clock request. Reenabling the clocks incurs 5 cycles of additional latency on wakeup.

                            Setting LIGHT_SLEEP to 1 keeps the clock request asserted during a normal sleep (Arm SCR.SLEEPDEEP = 0), for faster wakeup. Processor deep sleep (Arm SCR.SLEEPDEEP = 1) is not affected, and will always deassert the system-level clock request.

0x00000e00.name: STIMENA
0x00000e00.desc: For STIMENA[m] in ITM_TER*n, controls whether ITM_STIM(32*n + m) is enabled

0x00000e40.name: PRIVMASK
0x00000e40.desc: Bit mask to enable tracing on ITM stimulus ports

0x00000e80.name: BUSY
0x00000e80.desc: Indicates whether the ITM is currently processing events

0x00000e80.name: TRACEBUSID
0x00000e80.desc: Identifier for multi-source trace stream formatting. If multi-source trace is in use, the debugger must write a unique non-zero trace ID value to this field

0x00000e80.name: GTSFREQ
0x00000e80.desc: Defines how often the ITM generates a global timestamp, based on the global timestamp clock frequency, or disables generation of global timestamps

0x00000e80.name: TSPRESCALE
0x00000e80.desc: Local timestamp prescaler, used with the trace packet reference clock

0x00000e80.name: STALLENA
0x00000e80.desc: Stall the PE to guarantee delivery of Data Trace packets.

0x00000e80.name: SWOENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables asynchronous clocking of the timestamp counter

0x00000e80.name: TXENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables forwarding of hardware event packet from the DWT unit to the ITM for output to the TPIU

0x00000e80.name: SYNCENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables Synchronization packet transmission for a synchronous TPIU

0x00000e80.name: TSENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables Local timestamp generation

0x00000e80.name: ITMENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables the ITM

0x00000ef0.name: AFVALID
0x00000ef0.desc: A read of this bit returns the value of AFVALID

0x00000ef0.name: ATREADY
0x00000ef0.desc: A read of this bit returns the value of ATREADY

0x00000ef8.name: AFREADY
0x00000ef8.desc: A write to this bit gives the value of AFREADY

0x00000ef8.name: ATREADY
0x00000ef8.desc: A write to this bit gives the value of ATVALID

0x00000f00.name: IME
0x00000f00.desc: Integration mode enable bit - The possible values are:  0 - The trace unit is not in integration mode. 1 - The trace unit is in integration mode. This mode enables: A debug agent to perform topology detection. SoC test software to perform integration testing.

0x00000fbc.name: ARCHITECT
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines the architect of the component. Bits [31:28] are the JEP106 continuation code (JEP106 bank ID, minus 1) and bits [27:21] are the JEP106 ID code.

0x00000fbc.name: PRESENT
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines that the DEVARCH register is present

0x00000fbc.name: REVISION
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines the architecture revision of the component

0x00000fbc.name: ARCHVER
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines the architecture version of the component

0x00000fbc.name: ARCHPART
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines the architecture of the component

0x00001000.name: NUMCOMP
0x00001000.desc: Number of DWT comparators implemented

0x00001000.name: NOTRCPKT
0x00001000.desc: Indicates whether the implementation does not support trace

0x00001000.name: NOEXTTRIG
0x00001000.desc: Reserved, RAZ

0x00001000.name: NOCYCCNT
0x00001000.desc: Indicates whether the implementation does not include a cycle counter

0x00001000.name: NOPRFCNT
0x00001000.desc: Indicates whether the implementation does not include the profiling counters

0x00001000.name: CYCDISS
0x00001000.desc: Controls whether the cycle counter is disabled in Secure state

0x00001000.name: CYCEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables Event Counter packet generation on POSTCNT underflow

0x00001000.name: FOLDEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables DWT_FOLDCNT counter

0x00001000.name: LSUEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables DWT_LSUCNT counter

0x00001000.name: SLEEPEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enable DWT_SLEEPCNT counter

0x00001000.name: EXCEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables DWT_EXCCNT counter

0x00001000.name: CPIEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables DWT_CPICNT counter

0x00001000.name: EXTTRCENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables generation of Exception Trace packets

0x00001000.name: PCSAMPLENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables use of POSTCNT counter as a timer for Periodic PC Sample packet generation

0x00001000.name: SYNCTAP
0x00001000.desc: Selects the position of the synchronization packet counter tap on the CYCCNT counter. This determines the Synchronization packet rate

0x00001000.name: CYCTAP
0x00001000.desc: Selects the position of the POSTCNT tap on the CYCCNT counter

0x00001000.name: POSTINIT
0x00001000.desc: Initial value for the POSTCNT counter

0x00001000.name: POSTPRESET
0x00001000.desc: Reload value for the POSTCNT counter

0x00001000.name: CYCCNTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables CYCCNT

0x00001004.name: CYCCNT
0x00001004.desc: Increments one on each processor clock cycle when DWT_CTRL.CYCCNTENA == 1 and DEMCR.TRCENA == 1. On overflow, CYCCNT wraps to zero

0x0000100c.name: EXCCNT
0x0000100c.desc: Counts one on each cycle when all of the following are true: - DWT_CTRL.EXCEVTENA == 1 and DEMCR.TRCENA == 1. - No instruction is executed, see DWT_CPICNT. - An exception-entry or exception-exit related operation is in progress. - Either SecureNoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE, or NS-Req for the operation is set to Non-secure and NoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE.

0x00001014.name: LSUCNT
0x00001014.desc: Counts one on each cycle when all of the following are true: - DWT_CTRL.LSUEVTENA == 1 and DEMCR.TRCENA == 1. - No instruction is executed, see DWT_CPICNT. - No exception-entry or exception-exit operation is in progress, see DWT_EXCCNT. - A load-store operation is in progress. - Either SecureNoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE, or NS-Req for the operation is set to Non-secure and NoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE.

0x00001018.name: FOLDCNT
0x00001018.desc: Counts on each cycle when all of the following are true: - DWT_CTRL.FOLDEVTENA == 1 and DEMCR.TRCENA == 1. - At least two instructions are executed, see DWT_CPICNT. - Either SecureNoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE, or the PE is in Non-secure state and NoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE. The counter is incremented by the number of instructions executed, minus one

0x00001028.name: ID
0x00001028.desc: Identifies the capabilities for MATCH for comparator *n

0x00001028.name: MATCHED
0x00001028.desc: Set to 1 when the comparator matches

0x00001028.name: DATAVSIZE
0x00001028.desc: Defines the size of the object being watched for by Data Value and Data Address comparators

0x00001028.name: ACTION
0x00001028.desc: Defines the action on a match. This field is ignored and the comparator generates no actions if it is disabled by MATCH

0x00001028.name: MATCH
0x00001028.desc: Controls the type of match generated by this comparator

0x00002000.name: NUM_CODE_14_12_
0x00002000.desc: Indicates the number of implemented instruction address comparators. Zero indicates no Instruction Address comparators are implemented. The Instruction Address comparators are numbered from 0 to NUM_CODE - 1

0x00002000.name: NUM_LIT
0x00002000.desc: Indicates the number of implemented literal address comparators. The Literal Address comparators are numbered from NUM_CODE to NUM_CODE + NUM_LIT - 1

0x00002000.name: NUM_CODE_7_4_
0x00002000.desc: Indicates the number of implemented instruction address comparators. Zero indicates no Instruction Address comparators are implemented. The Instruction Address comparators are numbered from 0 to NUM_CODE - 1

0x00002000.name: KEY
0x00002000.desc: Writes to the FP_CTRL are ignored unless KEY is concurrently written to one

0x0000e004.name: INTLINESNUM
0x0000e004.desc: Indicates the number of the highest implemented register in each of the NVIC control register sets, or in the case of NVIC_IPR*n, 4×INTLINESNUM

0x0000e008.name: EXTEXCLALL
0x0000e008.desc: External Exclusives Allowed with no MPU

0x0000e010.name: COUNTFLAG
0x0000e010.desc: Returns 1 if timer counted to 0 since last time this was read. Clears on read by application or debugger.

0x0000e010.name: CLKSOURCE
0x0000e010.desc:
SysTick clock source. Always reads as one if SYST_CALIB reports NOREF. 
                            Selects the SysTick timer clock source: 
                            0 = External reference clock. 
                            1 = Processor clock.

0x0000e010.name: TICKINT
0x0000e010.desc:
Enables SysTick exception request: 
                            0 = Counting down to zero does not assert the SysTick exception request. 
                            1 = Counting down to zero to asserts the SysTick exception request.

0x0000e010.name: ENABLE
0x0000e010.desc:
Enable SysTick counter: 
                            0 = Counter disabled. 
                            1 = Counter enabled.

0x0000e014.name: RELOAD
0x0000e014.desc: Value to load into the SysTick Current Value Register when the counter reaches 0.

0x0000e018.name: CURRENT
0x0000e018.desc: Reads return the current value of the SysTick counter. This register is write-clear. Writing to it with any value clears the register to 0. Clearing this register also clears the COUNTFLAG bit of the SysTick Control and Status Register.

0x0000e01c.name: NOREF
0x0000e01c.desc: If reads as 1, the Reference clock is not provided - the CLKSOURCE bit of the SysTick Control and Status register will be forced to 1 and cannot be cleared to 0.

0x0000e01c.name: SKEW
0x0000e01c.desc: If reads as 1, the calibration value for 10ms is inexact (due to clock frequency).

0x0000e01c.name: TENMS
0x0000e01c.desc: An optional Reload value to be used for 10ms (100Hz) timing, subject to system clock skew errors. If the value reads as 0, the calibration value is not known.

0x0000ed04.desc: Allows the NMI exception pend state to be cleared

0x0000ed04.name: PENDSVSET
0x0000ed04.desc: Indicates whether the PendSV `FTSSS exception is pending

0x0000ed04.name: PENDSVCLR
0x0000ed04.desc: Allows the PendSV exception pend state to be cleared `FTSSS

0x0000ed04.name: PENDSTSET
0x0000ed04.desc: Indicates whether the SysTick `FTSSS exception is pending

0x0000ed04.name: PENDSTCLR
0x0000ed04.desc: Allows the SysTick exception pend state to be cleared `FTSSS

0x0000ed04.name: STTNS
0x0000ed04.desc: Controls whether in a single SysTick implementation, the SysTick is Secure or Non-secure

0x0000ed04.name: ISRPREEMPT
0x0000ed04.desc: Indicates whether a pending exception will be serviced on exit from debug halt state

0x0000ed04.name: ISRPENDING
0x0000ed04.desc: Indicates whether an external interrupt, generated by the NVIC, is pending

0x0000ed04.name: VECTPENDING
0x0000ed04.desc: The exception number of the highest priority pending and enabled interrupt

0x0000ed04.name: RETTOBASE
0x0000ed04.desc: In Handler mode, indicates whether there is more than one active exception

0x0000ed04.name: VECTACTIVE
0x0000ed04.desc: The exception number of the current executing exception

0x0000ed08.name: TBLOFF
0x0000ed08.desc: Vector table base offset field. It contains bits[31:7] of the offset of the table base from the bottom of the memory map.

0x0000ed0c.name: VECTKEY
0x0000ed0c.desc:
Register key: 
                            Reads as Unknown 
                            On writes, write 0x05FA to VECTKEY, otherwise the write is ignored.

0x0000ed0c.name: PRIS
0x0000ed0c.desc:
Prioritize Secure exceptions. The value of this bit defines whether Secure exception priority boosting is enabled. 
                            0	Priority ranges of Secure and Non-secure exceptions are identical. 
                            1	Non-secure exceptions are de-prioritized.

0x0000ed0c.name: BFHFNMINS
0x0000ed0c.desc:
BusFault, HardFault, and NMI Non-secure enable. 
                            0	BusFault, HardFault, and NMI are Secure. 
                            1	BusFault and NMI are Non-secure and exceptions can target Non-secure HardFault.

0x0000ed0c.name: PRIGROUP
0x0000ed0c.desc:
Interrupt priority grouping field. This field determines the split of group priority from subpriority. 
                            See https://developer.arm.com/documentation/100235/0004/the-cortex-m33-peripherals/system-control-block/application-interrupt-and-reset-control-register?lang=en

0x0000ed0c.name: SYSRESETREQS
0x0000ed0c.desc:
System reset request, Secure state only. 
                            0	SYSRESETREQ functionality is available to both Security states. 
                            1 SYSRESETREQ functionality is only available to Secure state.

0x0000ed0c.name: SYSRESETREQ
0x0000ed0c.desc: Writing 1 to this bit causes the SYSRESETREQ signal to the outer system to be asserted to request a reset. The intention is to force a large system reset of all major components except for debug. The C_HALT bit in the DHCSR is cleared as a result of the system reset requested. The debugger does not lose contact with the device.

0x0000ed0c.name: VECTCLRACTIVE
0x0000ed0c.desc: Clears all active state information for fixed and configurable exceptions. This bit: is self-clearing, can only be set by the DAP when the core is halted.  When set: clears all active exception status of the processor, forces a return to Thread mode, forces an IPSR of 0. A debugger must re-initialize the stack.

0x0000ed10.name: SEVONPEND
0x0000ed10.desc:
Send Event on Pending bit: 
                            0 = Only enabled interrupts or events can wakeup the processor, disabled interrupts are excluded. 
                            1 = Enabled events and all interrupts, including disabled interrupts, can wakeup the processor. 
                            When an event or interrupt becomes pending, the event signal wakes up the processor from WFE. If the 
                            processor is not waiting for an event, the event is registered and affects the next WFE. 
                            The processor also wakes up on execution of an SEV instruction or an external event.

0x0000ed10.name: SLEEPDEEPS
0x0000ed10.desc:
0 SLEEPDEEP is available to both security states 
                            1 SLEEPDEEP is only available to Secure state

0x0000ed10.name: SLEEPDEEP
0x0000ed10.desc:
Controls whether the processor uses sleep or deep sleep as its low power mode: 
                            0 = Sleep. 
                            1 = Deep sleep.

0x0000ed10.name: SLEEPONEXIT
0x0000ed10.desc:
Indicates sleep-on-exit when returning from Handler mode to Thread mode: 
                            0 = Do not sleep when returning to Thread mode. 
                            1 = Enter sleep, or deep sleep, on return from an ISR to Thread mode. 
                            Setting this bit to 1 enables an interrupt driven application to avoid returning to an empty main application.

0x0000ed14.name: BP
0x0000ed14.desc: Enables program flow prediction `FTSSS

0x0000ed14.name: IC
0x0000ed14.desc: This is a global enable bit for instruction caches in the selected Security state

0x0000ed14.name: DC
0x0000ed14.desc: Enables data caching of all data accesses to Normal memory `FTSSS

0x0000ed14.name: STKOFHFNMIGN
0x0000ed14.desc: Controls the effect of a stack limit violation while executing at a requested priority less than 0

0x0000ed14.name: RES1
0x0000ed14.desc: Reserved, RES1

0x0000ed14.name: BFHFNMIGN
0x0000ed14.desc: Determines the effect of precise BusFaults on handlers running at a requested priority less than 0

0x0000ed14.name: DIV_0_TRP
0x0000ed14.desc: Controls the generation of a DIVBYZERO UsageFault when attempting to perform integer division by zero

0x0000ed14.name: UNALIGN_TRP
0x0000ed14.desc: Controls the trapping of unaligned word or halfword accesses

0x0000ed14.name: USERSETMPEND
0x0000ed14.desc: Determines whether unprivileged accesses are permitted to pend interrupts via the STIR

0x0000ed14.name: RES1_1
0x0000ed14.desc: Reserved, RES1

0x0000ed24.name: HARDFAULTPENDED
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the pending state of the HardFault exception `CTTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: SECUREFAULTPENDED
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the pending state of the SecureFault exception

0x0000ed24.name: SECUREFAULTENA
0x0000ed24.desc: `DW the SecureFault exception is enabled

0x0000ed24.name: USGFAULTENA
0x0000ed24.desc: `DW the UsageFault exception is enabled `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: BUSFAULTENA
0x0000ed24.desc: `DW the BusFault exception is enabled

0x0000ed24.name: MEMFAULTENA
0x0000ed24.desc: `DW the MemManage exception is enabled `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: SVCALLPENDED
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the pending state of the SVCall exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: BUSFAULTPENDED
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the pending state of the BusFault exception

0x0000ed24.name: MEMFAULTPENDED
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the pending state of the MemManage exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: USGFAULTPENDED
0x0000ed24.desc: The UsageFault exception is banked between Security states, `IAAMO the pending state of the UsageFault exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: SYSTICKACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the SysTick exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: PENDSVACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the PendSV exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: MONITORACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the DebugMonitor exception

0x0000ed24.name: SVCALLACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the SVCall exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: NMIACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the NMI exception

0x0000ed24.name: SECUREFAULTACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the SecureFault exception

0x0000ed24.name: USGFAULTACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the UsageFault exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: HARDFAULTACT
0x0000ed24.desc: Indicates and allows limited modification of the active state of the HardFault exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed24.name: BUSFAULTACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the BusFault exception

0x0000ed24.name: MEMFAULTACT
0x0000ed24.desc: `IAAMO the active state of the MemManage exception `FTSSS

0x0000ed28.name: UFSR_DIVBYZERO
0x0000ed28.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether an integer division by zero error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: UFSR_UNALIGNED
0x0000ed28.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether an unaligned access error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: UFSR_STKOF
0x0000ed28.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether a stack overflow error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: UFSR_NOCP
0x0000ed28.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether a coprocessor disabled or not present error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: UFSR_INVPC
0x0000ed28.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether an integrity check error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: UFSR_INVSTATE
0x0000ed28.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether an EPSR.T or EPSR.IT validity error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: UFSR_UNDEFINSTR
0x0000ed28.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether an undefined instruction error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: BFSR_BFARVALID
0x0000ed28.desc: Indicates validity of the contents of the BFAR register

0x0000ed28.name: BFSR_LSPERR
0x0000ed28.desc: Records whether a BusFault occurred during FP lazy state preservation

0x0000ed28.name: BFSR_STKERR
0x0000ed28.desc: Records whether a derived BusFault occurred during exception entry stacking

0x0000ed28.name: BFSR_UNSTKERR
0x0000ed28.desc: Records whether a derived BusFault occurred during exception return unstacking

0x0000ed28.name: BFSR_IMPRECISERR
0x0000ed28.desc: Records whether an imprecise data access error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: BFSR_PRECISERR
0x0000ed28.desc: Records whether a precise data access error has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: BFSR_IBUSERR
0x0000ed28.desc: Records whether a BusFault on an instruction prefetch has occurred

0x0000ed28.name: MMFSR
0x0000ed28.desc: Provides information on MemManage exceptions

0x0000ed2c.name: DEBUGEVT
0x0000ed2c.desc: Indicates when a Debug event has occurred

0x0000ed2c.name: FORCED
0x0000ed2c.desc: Indicates that a fault with configurable priority has been escalated to a HardFault exception, because it could not be made active, because of priority, or because it was disabled

0x0000ed2c.name: VECTTBL
0x0000ed2c.desc: Indicates when a fault has occurred because of a vector table read error on exception processing

0x0000ed30.name: EXTERNAL
0x0000ed30.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether an External debug request debug event has occurred

0x0000ed30.name: VCATCH
0x0000ed30.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether a Vector catch debug event has occurred

0x0000ed30.name: DWTTRAP
0x0000ed30.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether a Watchpoint debug event has occurred

0x0000ed30.name: BKPT
0x0000ed30.desc: Sticky flag indicating whether a Breakpoint debug event has occurred

0x0000ed30.name: HALTED
0x0000ed30.desc: Sticky flag indicating that a Halt request debug event or Step debug event has occurred

0x0000ed34.name: ADDRESS
0x0000ed34.desc: This register is updated with the address of a location that produced a MemManage fault. The MMFSR shows the cause of the fault, and whether this field is valid. This field is valid only when MMFSR.MMARVALID is set, otherwise it is UNKNOWN

0x0000ed38.name: ADDRESS
0x0000ed38.desc: This register is updated with the address of a location that produced a BusFault. The BFSR shows the reason for the fault. This field is valid only when BFSR.BFARVALID is set, otherwise it is UNKNOWN

0x0000ed44.name: MPROGMOD
0x0000ed44.desc: Identifies support for the M-Profile programmers' model support

0x0000ed44.name: SECURITY
0x0000ed44.desc: Identifies whether the Security Extension is implemented

0x0000ed48.name: MPROFDBG
0x0000ed48.desc: Indicates the supported M-profile debug architecture

0x0000ed50.name: AUXREG
0x0000ed50.desc: Indicates support for Auxiliary Control Registers

0x0000ed50.name: TCM
0x0000ed50.desc: Indicates support for tightly coupled memories (TCMs)

0x0000ed50.name: SHARELVL
0x0000ed50.desc: Indicates the number of shareability levels implemented

0x0000ed50.name: OUTERSHR
0x0000ed50.desc: Indicates the outermost shareability domain implemented

0x0000ed50.name: PMSA
0x0000ed50.desc: Indicates support for the protected memory system architecture (PMSA)

0x0000ed58.name: WFISTALL
0x0000ed58.desc: Indicates the support for Wait For Interrupt (WFI) stalling

0x0000ed5c.name: BPMAINT
0x0000ed5c.desc: Indicates the supported branch predictor maintenance

0x0000ed5c.name: CMAINTSW
0x0000ed5c.desc: Indicates the supported cache maintenance operations by set/way

0x0000ed5c.name: CMAINTVA
0x0000ed5c.desc: Indicates the supported cache maintenance operations by address

0x0000ed60.name: CMPBRANCH
0x0000ed60.desc: Indicates the supported combined Compare and Branch instructions

0x0000ed60.name: BITFIELD
0x0000ed60.desc: Indicates the supported bit field instructions

0x0000ed60.name: BITCOUNT
0x0000ed60.desc: Indicates the supported bit count instructions

0x0000ed64.name: IMMEDIATE
0x0000ed64.desc: Indicates the implemented for data-processing instructions with long immediates

0x0000ed68.name: MULTU
0x0000ed68.desc: Indicates the implemented advanced unsigned Multiply instructions

0x0000ed68.name: MULTS
0x0000ed68.desc: Indicates the implemented advanced signed Multiply instructions

0x0000ed68.name: MULT
0x0000ed68.desc: Indicates the implemented additional Multiply instructions

0x0000ed68.name: MULTIACCESSINT
0x0000ed68.desc: Indicates the support for interruptible multi-access instructions

0x0000ed68.name: MEMHINT
0x0000ed68.desc: Indicates the implemented Memory Hint instructions

0x0000ed68.name: LOADSTORE
0x0000ed68.desc: Indicates the implemented additional load/store instructions

0x0000ed6c.name: TRUENOP
0x0000ed6c.desc: Indicates the implemented true NOP instructions

0x0000ed6c.name: T32COPY
0x0000ed6c.desc: Indicates the support for T32 non flag-setting MOV instructions

0x0000ed6c.name: TABBRANCH
0x0000ed6c.desc: Indicates the implemented Table Branch instructions

0x0000ed6c.name: SYNCHPRIM
0x0000ed6c.desc: Used in conjunction with ID_ISAR4.SynchPrim_frac to indicate the implemented Synchronization Primitive instructions

0x0000ed70.name: PSR_M
0x0000ed70.desc: Indicates the implemented M profile instructions to modify the PSRs

0x0000ed70.name: SYNCPRIM_FRAC
0x0000ed70.desc: Used in conjunction with ID_ISAR3.SynchPrim to indicate the implemented Synchronization Primitive instructions

0x0000ed70.name: WRITEBACK
0x0000ed70.desc: Indicates the support for writeback addressing modes

0x0000ed70.name: WITHSHIFTS
0x0000ed70.desc: Indicates the support for writeback addressing modes

0x0000ed7c.name: CWG
0x0000ed7c.desc: Log2 of the number of words of the maximum size of memory that can be overwritten as a result of the eviction of a cache entry that has had a memory location in it modified

0x0000ed7c.name: ERG
0x0000ed7c.desc: Log2 of the number of words of the maximum size of the reservation granule that has been implemented for the Load-Exclusive and Store-Exclusive instructions

0x0000ed7c.name: DMINLINE
0x0000ed7c.desc: Log2 of the number of words in the smallest cache line of all the data caches and unified caches that are controlled by the PE

0x0000ed7c.name: IMINLINE
0x0000ed7c.desc: Log2 of the number of words in the smallest cache line of all the instruction caches that are controlled by the PE

0x0000ed88.name: CP11
0x0000ed88.desc: The value in this field is ignored. If the implementation does not include the FP Extension, this field is RAZ/WI. If the value of this bit is not programmed to the same value as the CP10 field, then the value is UNKNOWN

0x0000ed88.name: CP10
0x0000ed88.desc: Defines the access rights for the floating-point functionality

0x0000ed8c.name: CP11
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to the Floating-point Extension

0x0000ed8c.name: CP10
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to the Floating-point Extension

0x0000ed8c.name: CP7
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to coprocessor CP7

0x0000ed8c.name: CP6
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to coprocessor CP6

0x0000ed8c.name: CP5
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to coprocessor CP5

0x0000ed8c.name: CP4
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to coprocessor CP4

0x0000ed8c.name: CP3
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to coprocessor CP3

0x0000ed8c.name: CP2
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to coprocessor CP2

0x0000ed8c.name: CP1
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to coprocessor CP1

0x0000ed8c.name: CP0
0x0000ed8c.desc: Enables Non-secure access to coprocessor CP0

0x0000ed90.name: DREGION
0x0000ed90.desc: Number of regions supported by the MPU

0x0000ed90.name: SEPARATE
0x0000ed90.desc: Indicates support for separate instructions and data address regions

0x0000ed94.name: PRIVDEFENA
0x0000ed94.desc: Controls whether the default memory map is enabled for privileged software

0x0000ed94.name: HFNMIENA
0x0000ed94.desc: Controls whether handlers executing with priority less than 0 access memory with the MPU enabled or disabled. This applies to HardFaults, NMIs, and exception handlers when FAULTMASK is set to 1

0x0000ed98.name: REGION
0x0000ed98.desc: Indicates the memory region accessed by MPU_RBAR and MPU_RLAR

0x0000ed9c.name: BASE
0x0000ed9c.desc: Contains bits [31:5] of the lower inclusive limit of the selected MPU memory region. This value is zero extended to provide the base address to be checked against

0x0000ed9c.name: SH
0x0000ed9c.desc: Defines the Shareability domain of this region for Normal memory

0x0000ed9c.name: AP
0x0000ed9c.desc: Defines the access permissions for this region

0x0000ed9c.name: XN
0x0000ed9c.desc: Defines whether code can be executed from this region

0x0000eda0.name: LIMIT
0x0000eda0.desc: Contains bits [31:5] of the upper inclusive limit of the selected MPU memory region. This value is postfixed with 0x1F to provide the limit address to be checked against

0x0000eda0.name: ATTRINDX
0x0000eda0.desc: Associates a set of attributes in the MPU_MAIR0 and MPU_MAIR1 fields

0x0000eda4.name: BASE
0x0000eda4.desc: Contains bits [31:5] of the lower inclusive limit of the selected MPU memory region. This value is zero extended to provide the base address to be checked against

0x0000eda4.name: SH
0x0000eda4.desc: Defines the Shareability domain of this region for Normal memory

0x0000eda4.name: AP
0x0000eda4.desc: Defines the access permissions for this region

0x0000eda4.name: XN
0x0000eda4.desc: Defines whether code can be executed from this region

0x0000eda8.name: LIMIT
0x0000eda8.desc: Contains bits [31:5] of the upper inclusive limit of the selected MPU memory region. This value is postfixed with 0x1F to provide the limit address to be checked against

0x0000eda8.name: ATTRINDX
0x0000eda8.desc: Associates a set of attributes in the MPU_MAIR0 and MPU_MAIR1 fields

0x0000edac.name: BASE
0x0000edac.desc: Contains bits [31:5] of the lower inclusive limit of the selected MPU memory region. This value is zero extended to provide the base address to be checked against

0x0000edac.name: SH
0x0000edac.desc: Defines the Shareability domain of this region for Normal memory

0x0000edac.name: AP
0x0000edac.desc: Defines the access permissions for this region

0x0000edac.name: XN
0x0000edac.desc: Defines whether code can be executed from this region

0x0000edb0.name: LIMIT
0x0000edb0.desc: Contains bits [31:5] of the upper inclusive limit of the selected MPU memory region. This value is postfixed with 0x1F to provide the limit address to be checked against

0x0000edb0.name: ATTRINDX
0x0000edb0.desc: Associates a set of attributes in the MPU_MAIR0 and MPU_MAIR1 fields

0x0000edb4.name: BASE
0x0000edb4.desc: Contains bits [31:5] of the lower inclusive limit of the selected MPU memory region. This value is zero extended to provide the base address to be checked against

0x0000edb4.name: SH
0x0000edb4.desc: Defines the Shareability domain of this region for Normal memory

0x0000edb4.name: AP
0x0000edb4.desc: Defines the access permissions for this region

0x0000edb4.name: XN
0x0000edb4.desc: Defines whether code can be executed from this region

0x0000edb8.name: LIMIT
0x0000edb8.desc: Contains bits [31:5] of the upper inclusive limit of the selected MPU memory region. This value is postfixed with 0x1F to provide the limit address to be checked against

0x0000edb8.name: ATTRINDX
0x0000edb8.desc: Associates a set of attributes in the MPU_MAIR0 and MPU_MAIR1 fields

0x0000edc0.name: ATTR3
0x0000edc0.desc: Memory attribute encoding for MPU regions with an AttrIndex of 3

0x0000edc0.name: ATTR2
0x0000edc0.desc: Memory attribute encoding for MPU regions with an AttrIndex of 2

0x0000edc0.name: ATTR1
0x0000edc0.desc: Memory attribute encoding for MPU regions with an AttrIndex of 1

0x0000edc0.name: ATTR0
0x0000edc0.desc: Memory attribute encoding for MPU regions with an AttrIndex of 0

0x0000edc4.name: ATTR7
0x0000edc4.desc: Memory attribute encoding for MPU regions with an AttrIndex of 7

0x0000edc4.name: ATTR6
0x0000edc4.desc: Memory attribute encoding for MPU regions with an AttrIndex of 6

0x0000edc4.name: ATTR5
0x0000edc4.desc: Memory attribute encoding for MPU regions with an AttrIndex of 5

0x0000edc4.name: ATTR4
0x0000edc4.desc: Memory attribute encoding for MPU regions with an AttrIndex of 4

0x0000edd0.name: ALLNS
0x0000edd0.desc: When SAU_CTRL.ENABLE is 0 this bit controls if the memory is marked as Non-secure or Secure

0x0000edd4.name: SREGION
0x0000edd4.desc: The number of implemented SAU regions

0x0000edd8.name: REGION
0x0000edd8.desc: Indicates the SAU region accessed by SAU_RBAR and SAU_RLAR

0x0000eddc.name: BADDR
0x0000eddc.desc: Holds bits [31:5] of the base address for the selected SAU region

0x0000ede0.name: LADDR
0x0000ede0.desc: Holds bits [31:5] of the limit address for the selected SAU region

0x0000ede0.name: NSC
0x0000ede0.desc: Controls whether Non-secure state is permitted to execute an SG instruction from this region

0x0000ede4.name: LSERR
0x0000ede4.desc: Sticky flag indicating that an error occurred during lazy state activation or deactivation

0x0000ede4.name: SFARVALID
0x0000ede4.desc: This bit is set when the SFAR register contains a valid value. As with similar fields, such as BFSR.BFARVALID and MMFSR.MMARVALID, this bit can be cleared by other exceptions, such as BusFault

0x0000ede4.name: LSPERR
0x0000ede4.desc: Stick flag indicating that an SAU or IDAU violation occurred during the lazy preservation of floating-point state

0x0000ede4.name: INVTRAN
0x0000ede4.desc: Sticky flag indicating that an exception was raised due to a branch that was not flagged as being domain crossing causing a transition from Secure to Non-secure memory

0x0000ede4.name: AUVIOL
0x0000ede4.desc: Sticky flag indicating that an attempt was made to access parts of the address space that are marked as Secure with NS-Req for the transaction set to Non-secure. This bit is not set if the violation occurred during lazy state preservation. See LSPERR

0x0000ede4.name: INVER
0x0000ede4.desc: This can be caused by EXC_RETURN.DCRS being set to 0 when returning from an exception in the Non-secure state, or by EXC_RETURN.ES being set to 1 when returning from an exception in the Non-secure state

0x0000ede4.name: INVIS
0x0000ede4.desc: This bit is set if the integrity signature in an exception stack frame is found to be invalid during the unstacking operation

0x0000ede4.name: INVEP
0x0000ede4.desc: This bit is set if a function call from the Non-secure state or exception targets a non-SG instruction in the Secure state. This bit is also set if the target address is a SG instruction, but there is no matching SAU/IDAU region with the NSC flag set

0x0000ede8.name: ADDRESS
0x0000ede8.desc: The address of an access that caused a attribution unit violation. This field is only valid when SFSR.SFARVALID is set. This allows the actual flip flops associated with this register to be shared with other fault address registers. If an implementation chooses to share the storage in this way, care must be taken to not leak Secure address information to the Non-secure state. One way of achieving this is to share the SFAR register with the MMFAR_S register, which is not accessible to the Non-secure state

0x0000edf0.name: S_RESTART_ST
0x0000edf0.desc: Indicates the PE has processed a request to clear DHCSR.C_HALT to 0. That is, either a write to DHCSR that clears DHCSR.C_HALT from 1 to 0, or an External Restart Request

0x0000edf0.name: S_RESET_ST
0x0000edf0.desc: Indicates whether the PE has been reset since the last read of the DHCSR

0x0000edf0.name: S_RETIRE_ST
0x0000edf0.desc: Set to 1 every time the PE retires one of more instructions

0x0000edf0.name: S_SDE
0x0000edf0.desc: Indicates whether Secure invasive debug is allowed

0x0000edf0.name: S_LOCKUP
0x0000edf0.desc: Indicates whether the PE is in Lockup state

0x0000edf0.name: S_SLEEP
0x0000edf0.desc: Indicates whether the PE is sleeping

0x0000edf0.name: S_HALT
0x0000edf0.desc: Indicates whether the PE is in Debug state

0x0000edf0.name: S_REGRDY
0x0000edf0.desc: Handshake flag to transfers through the DCRDR

0x0000edf0.name: C_SNAPSTALL
0x0000edf0.desc: Allow imprecise entry to Debug state

0x0000edf0.name: C_MASKINTS
0x0000edf0.desc: When debug is enabled, the debugger can write to this bit to mask PendSV, SysTick and external configurable interrupts

0x0000edf0.name: C_STEP
0x0000edf0.desc: Enable single instruction step

0x0000edf0.name: C_HALT
0x0000edf0.desc: PE enter Debug state halt request

0x0000edf0.name: C_DEBUGEN
0x0000edf0.desc: Enable Halting debug

0x0000edf4.name: REGWNR
0x0000edf4.desc: Specifies the access type for the transfer

0x0000edf4.name: REGSEL
0x0000edf4.desc: Specifies the general-purpose register, special-purpose register, or FP register to transfer

0x0000edf8.name: DBGTMP
0x0000edf8.desc: Provides debug access for reading and writing the general-purpose registers, special-purpose registers, and Floating-point Extension registers

0x0000edfc.name: TRCENA
0x0000edfc.desc: Global enable for all DWT and ITM features

0x0000edfc.name: SDME
0x0000edfc.desc: Indicates whether the DebugMonitor targets the Secure or the Non-secure state and whether debug events are allowed in Secure state

0x0000edfc.name: MON_REQ
0x0000edfc.desc: DebugMonitor semaphore bit

0x0000edfc.name: MON_STEP
0x0000edfc.desc: Enable DebugMonitor stepping

0x0000edfc.name: MON_PEND
0x0000edfc.desc: Sets or clears the pending state of the DebugMonitor exception

0x0000edfc.name: MON_EN
0x0000edfc.desc: Enable the DebugMonitor exception

0x0000edfc.name: VC_SFERR
0x0000edfc.desc: SecureFault exception halting debug vector catch enable

0x0000edfc.name: VC_HARDERR
0x0000edfc.desc: HardFault exception halting debug vector catch enable

0x0000edfc.name: VC_INTERR
0x0000edfc.desc: Enable halting debug vector catch for faults during exception entry and return

0x0000edfc.name: VC_BUSERR
0x0000edfc.desc: BusFault exception halting debug vector catch enable

0x0000edfc.name: VC_STATERR
0x0000edfc.desc: Enable halting debug trap on a UsageFault exception caused by a state information error, for example an Undefined Instruction exception

0x0000edfc.name: VC_CHKERR
0x0000edfc.desc: Enable halting debug trap on a UsageFault exception caused by a checking error, for example an alignment check error

0x0000edfc.name: VC_NOCPERR
0x0000edfc.desc: Enable halting debug trap on a UsageFault caused by an access to a coprocessor

0x0000edfc.name: VC_MMERR
0x0000edfc.desc: Enable halting debug trap on a MemManage exception

0x0000edfc.name: VC_CORERESET
0x0000edfc.desc: Enable Reset Vector Catch. This causes a warm reset to halt a running system

0x0000ee08.name: CDSKEY
0x0000ee08.desc: Writes to the CDS bit are ignored unless CDSKEY is concurrently written to zero

0x0000ee08.name: CDS
0x0000ee08.desc: This field indicates the current Security state of the processor

0x0000ee08.name: SBRSEL
0x0000ee08.desc: If SBRSELEN is 1 this bit selects whether the Non-secure or the Secure version of the memory-mapped Banked registers are accessible to the debugger

0x0000ee08.name: SBRSELEN
0x0000ee08.desc: Controls whether the SBRSEL field or the current Security state of the processor selects which version of the memory-mapped Banked registers are accessed to the debugger

0x0000ef00.name: INTID
0x0000ef00.desc: Indicates the interrupt to be pended. The value written is (ExceptionNumber - 16)

0x0000ef34.name: ASPEN
0x0000ef34.desc: When this bit is set to 1, execution of a floating-point instruction sets the CONTROL.FPCA bit to 1

0x0000ef34.name: LSPEN
0x0000ef34.desc: Enables lazy context save of floating-point state

0x0000ef34.name: LSPENS
0x0000ef34.desc: This bit controls whether the LSPEN bit is writeable from the Non-secure state

0x0000ef34.name: CLRONRET
0x0000ef34.desc: Clear floating-point caller saved registers on exception return

0x0000ef34.name: CLRONRETS
0x0000ef34.desc: This bit controls whether the CLRONRET bit is writeable from the Non-secure state

0x0000ef34.name: TS
0x0000ef34.desc: Treat floating-point registers as Secure enable

0x0000ef34.name: UFRDY
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates whether the software executing when the PE allocated the floating-point stack frame was able to set the UsageFault exception to pending

0x0000ef34.name: SPLIMVIOL
0x0000ef34.desc: This bit is banked between the Security states and indicates whether the floating-point context violates the stack pointer limit that was active when lazy state preservation was activated. SPLIMVIOL modifies the lazy floating-point state preservation behavior

0x0000ef34.name: MONRDY
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates whether the software executing when the PE allocated the floating-point stack frame was able to set the DebugMonitor exception to pending

0x0000ef34.name: SFRDY
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates whether the software executing when the PE allocated the floating-point stack frame was able to set the SecureFault exception to pending. This bit is only present in the Secure version of the register, and behaves as RAZ/WI when accessed from the Non-secure state

0x0000ef34.name: BFRDY
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates whether the software executing when the PE allocated the floating-point stack frame was able to set the BusFault exception to pending

0x0000ef34.name: MMRDY
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates whether the software executing when the PE allocated the floating-point stack frame was able to set the MemManage exception to pending

0x0000ef34.name: HFRDY
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates whether the software executing when the PE allocated the floating-point stack frame was able to set the HardFault exception to pending

0x0000ef34.name: THREAD
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates the PE mode when it allocated the floating-point stack frame

0x0000ef34.name: S
0x0000ef34.desc: Security status of the floating-point context. This bit is only present in the Secure version of the register, and behaves as RAZ/WI when accessed from the Non-secure state. This bit is updated whenever lazy state preservation is activated, or when a floating-point instruction is executed

0x0000ef34.name: USER
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates the privilege level of the software executing when the PE allocated the floating-point stack frame

0x0000ef34.name: LSPACT
0x0000ef34.desc: Indicates whether lazy preservation of the floating-point state is active

0x0000ef38.name: ADDRESS
0x0000ef38.desc: The location of the unpopulated floating-point register space allocated on an exception stack frame

0x0000ef40.name: FPROUND
0x0000ef40.desc: Indicates the rounding modes supported by the FP Extension

0x0000ef40.name: FPSQRT
0x0000ef40.desc: Indicates the support for FP square root operations

0x0000ef40.name: FPDIVIDE
0x0000ef40.desc: Indicates the support for FP divide operations

0x0000ef40.name: FPDP
0x0000ef40.desc: Indicates support for FP double-precision operations

0x0000ef40.name: FPSP
0x0000ef40.desc: Indicates support for FP single-precision operations

0x0000ef40.name: SIMDREG
0x0000ef40.desc: Indicates size of FP register file

0x0000ef44.name: FMAC
0x0000ef44.desc: Indicates whether the FP Extension implements the fused multiply accumulate instructions

0x0000ef44.name: FPHP
0x0000ef44.desc: Indicates whether the FP Extension implements half-precision FP conversion instructions

0x0000ef44.name: FPDNAN
0x0000ef44.desc: Indicates whether the FP hardware implementation supports NaN propagation

0x0000ef44.name: FPFTZ
0x0000ef44.desc: Indicates whether subnormals are always flushed-to-zero

0x0000ef48.name: FPMISC
0x0000ef48.desc: Indicates support for miscellaneous FP features

0x0000efbc.name: ARCHITECT
0x0000efbc.desc: Defines the architect of the component. Bits [31:28] are the JEP106 continuation code (JEP106 bank ID, minus 1) and bits [27:21] are the JEP106 ID code.

0x0000efbc.name: PRESENT
0x0000efbc.desc: Defines that the DEVARCH register is present

0x0000efbc.name: REVISION
0x0000efbc.desc: Defines the architecture revision of the component

0x0000efbc.name: ARCHVER
0x0000efbc.desc: Defines the architecture version of the component

0x0000efbc.name: ARCHPART
0x0000efbc.desc: Defines the architecture of the component

0x0004100c.name: PMSTABLE
0x0004100c.desc: Indicates whether the ETM-Teal registers are stable and can be read

0x0004100c.name: IDLE
0x0004100c.desc: Indicates that the trace unit is inactive

0x00041020.name: TYPE1
0x00041020.desc: Selects the resource type for event 1

0x00041020.name: SEL1
0x00041020.desc: Selects the resource number, based on the value of TYPE1: When TYPE1 is 0, selects a single selected resource from 0-15 defined by SEL1[2:0].  When TYPE1 is 1, selects a Boolean combined resource pair from 0-7 defined by SEL1[2:0]

0x00041020.name: TYPE0
0x00041020.desc: Selects the resource type for event 0

0x00041020.name: SEL0
0x00041020.desc: Selects the resource number, based on the value of TYPE0: When TYPE1 is 0, selects a single selected resource from 0-15 defined by SEL0[2:0].  When TYPE1 is 1, selects a Boolean combined resource pair from 0-7 defined by SEL0[2:0]

0x00041024.name: INSTEN1
0x00041024.desc: One bit per event, to enable generation of an event element in the instruction trace stream when the selected event occurs

0x00041024.name: INSTEN0
0x00041024.desc: One bit per event, to enable generation of an event element in the instruction trace stream when the selected event occurs

0x0004102c.name: ISTALL
0x0004102c.desc: Stall processor based on instruction trace buffer space

0x0004102c.name: LEVEL
0x0004102c.desc: Threshold at which stalling becomes active. This provides four levels. This level can be varied to optimize the level of invasion caused by stalling, balanced against the risk of a FIFO overflow

0x00041030.name: TYPE0
0x00041030.desc: Selects the resource type for event 0

0x00041030.name: SEL0
0x00041030.desc: Selects the resource number, based on the value of TYPE0: When TYPE1 is 0, selects a single selected resource from 0-15 defined by SEL0[2:0].  When TYPE1 is 1, selects a Boolean combined resource pair from 0-7 defined by SEL0[2:0]

0x00041034.name: PERIOD
0x00041034.desc: Defines the number of bytes of trace between trace synchronization requests as a total of the number of bytes generated by the instruction stream. The number of bytes is 2N where N is the value of this field: - A value of zero disables these periodic trace synchronization requests, but does not disable other trace synchronization requests.  - The minimum value that can be programmed, other than zero, is 8, providing a minimum trace synchronization period of 256 bytes.  - The maximum value is 20, providing a maximum trace synchronization period of 2^20 bytes

0x00041080.name: EXLEVEL_S3
0x00041080.desc: In Secure state, each bit controls whether instruction tracing is enabled for the corresponding exception level

0x00041080.name: EXLEVEL_S0
0x00041080.desc: In Secure state, each bit controls whether instruction tracing is enabled for the corresponding exception level

0x00041080.name: TRCERR
0x00041080.desc: Selects whether a system error exception must always be traced

0x00041080.name: TRCRESET
0x00041080.desc: Selects whether a reset exception must always be traced

0x00041080.name: SSSTATUS
0x00041080.desc: Indicates the current status of the start/stop logic

0x00041080.name: TYPE0
0x00041080.desc: Selects the resource type for event 0

0x00041080.name: SEL0
0x00041080.desc: Selects the resource number, based on the value of TYPE0: When TYPE1 is 0, selects a single selected resource from 0-15 defined by SEL0[2:0].  When TYPE1 is 1, selects a Boolean combined resource pair from 0-7 defined by SEL0[2:0]

0x00041140.name: VALUE
0x00041140.desc: Defines the reload value for the counter. This value is loaded into the counter each time the reload event occurs

0x00041208.name: PAIRINV
0x00041208.desc: Inverts the result of a combined pair of resources.  This bit is only implemented on the lower register for a pair of resource selectors

0x00041208.name: SELECT
0x00041208.desc: Selects one or more resources from the wanted group. One bit is provided per resource from the group

0x0004120c.name: PAIRINV
0x0004120c.desc: Inverts the result of a combined pair of resources.  This bit is only implemented on the lower register for a pair of resource selectors

0x0004120c.name: SELECT
0x0004120c.desc: Selects one or more resources from the wanted group. One bit is provided per resource from the group

0x000412a0.name: STATUS
0x000412a0.desc: Single-shot status bit. Indicates if any of the comparators, that TRCSSCCRn.SAC or TRCSSCCRn.ARC selects, have matched

0x000412c0.name: PC
0x000412c0.desc: Selects one or more PE comparator inputs for Single-shot control.  TRCIDR4.NUMPC defines the size of the PC field.  1 bit is provided for each implemented PE comparator input.  For example, if bit[1] == 1 this selects PE comparator input 1 for Single-shot control

0x00041314.name: STICKYPD
0x00041314.desc: Sticky powerdown status bit. Indicates whether the trace register state is valid:

0x00041fa0.desc: When a write to one of these bits occurs, with the value:

0x00041fa0.desc: When a write to one of these bits occurs, with the value:

0x00041fa4.name: CLR0

0x00041fb8.name: SNID
0x00041fb8.desc: Indicates whether the system enables the trace unit to support Secure non-invasive debug:

0x00041fb8.name: SID
0x00041fb8.desc: Indicates whether the trace unit supports Secure invasive debug:

0x00041fb8.name: NSNID
0x00041fb8.desc: Indicates whether the system enables the trace unit to support Non-secure non-invasive debug:

0x00041fb8.name: NSID
0x00041fb8.desc: Indicates whether the trace unit supports Non-secure invasive debug:

0x00042010.name: INTACK
0x00042010.desc: Acknowledges the corresponding ctitrigout output. There is one bit of the register for each ctitrigout output. When a 1 is written to a bit in this register, the corresponding ctitrigout is acknowledged, causing it to be cleared.

0x00042014.name: APPSET
0x00042014.desc: Setting a bit HIGH generates a channel event for the selected channel. There is one bit of the register for each channel

0x00042018.name: APPCLEAR
0x00042018.desc: Sets the corresponding bits in the CTIAPPSET to 0. There is one bit of the register for each channel.

0x0004201c.name: APPULSE
0x0004201c.desc: Setting a bit HIGH generates a channel event pulse for the selected channel. There is one bit of the register for each channel.

0x00042020.name: TRIGINEN
0x00042020.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to the corresponding channel when a ctitrigin input is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels

0x00042024.name: TRIGINEN
0x00042024.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to the corresponding channel when a ctitrigin input is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels

0x00042028.name: TRIGINEN
0x00042028.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to the corresponding channel when a ctitrigin input is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels

0x0004202c.name: TRIGINEN
0x0004202c.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to the corresponding channel when a ctitrigin input is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels

0x00042030.name: TRIGINEN
0x00042030.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to the corresponding channel when a ctitrigin input is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels

0x00042034.name: TRIGINEN
0x00042034.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to the corresponding channel when a ctitrigin input is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels

0x00042038.name: TRIGINEN
0x00042038.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to the corresponding channel when a ctitrigin input is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels

0x0004203c.name: TRIGINEN
0x0004203c.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to the corresponding channel when a ctitrigin input is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels

0x000420a0.name: TRIGOUTEN
0x000420a0.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to ctitrigout when the corresponding channel is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels.

0x000420a4.name: TRIGOUTEN
0x000420a4.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to ctitrigout when the corresponding channel is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels.

0x000420a8.name: TRIGOUTEN
0x000420a8.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to ctitrigout when the corresponding channel is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels.

0x000420ac.name: TRIGOUTEN
0x000420ac.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to ctitrigout when the corresponding channel is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels.

0x000420b0.name: TRIGOUTEN
0x000420b0.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to ctitrigout when the corresponding channel is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels.

0x000420b4.name: TRIGOUTEN
0x000420b4.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to ctitrigout when the corresponding channel is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels.

0x000420b8.name: TRIGOUTEN
0x000420b8.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to ctitrigout when the corresponding channel is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels.

0x000420bc.name: TRIGOUTEN
0x000420bc.desc: Enables a cross trigger event to ctitrigout when the corresponding channel is activated. There is one bit of the field for each of the four channels.

0x00042130.name: TRIGINSTATUS
0x00042130.desc: Shows the status of the ctitrigin inputs. There is one bit of the field for each trigger input.Because the register provides a view of the raw ctitrigin inputs, the reset value is UNKNOWN.

0x00042134.name: TRIGOUTSTATUS
0x00042134.desc: Shows the status of the ctitrigout outputs. There is one bit of the field for each trigger output.

0x00042138.name: CTICHOUTSTATUS
0x00042138.desc: Shows the status of the ctichout outputs. There is one bit of the field for each channel output

0x00042140.name: CTIGATEEN3
0x00042140.desc: Enable ctichout3. Set to 0 to disable channel propagation.

0x00042140.name: CTIGATEEN2
0x00042140.desc: Enable ctichout2. Set to 0 to disable channel propagation.

0x00042140.name: CTIGATEEN1
0x00042140.desc: Enable ctichout1. Set to 0 to disable channel propagation.

0x00042140.name: CTIGATEEN0
0x00042140.desc: Enable ctichout0. Set to 0 to disable channel propagation.

0x00042ee4.name: CTCHOUT
0x00042ee4.desc: Sets the value of the ctichout outputs

0x00042ee8.name: CTTRIGOUT
0x00042ee8.desc: Sets the value of the ctitrigout outputs

0x00042ef4.name: CTCHIN
0x00042ef4.desc: Reads the value of the ctichin inputs.

0x00042fbc.name: PRESENT
0x00042fbc.desc: Indicates whether the DEVARCH register is present

0x00042fc8.name: EXTMUXNUM
0x00042fc8.desc: Indicates the number of multiplexers available on Trigger Inputs and Trigger Outputs that are using asicctl. The default value of 0b00000 indicates that no multiplexing is present. This value of this bit depends on the Verilog define EXTMUXNUM that you must change accordingly.

0x00042fcc.name: SUB
0x00042fcc.desc: Sub-classification of the type of the debug component as specified in the ARM Architecture Specification within the major classification as specified in the MAJOR field.

0x00042fcc.name: MAJOR
0x00042fcc.desc: Major classification of the type of the debug component as specified in the ARM Architecture Specification for this debug and trace component.

0x00042fd0.name: SIZE
0x00042fd0.desc: Always 0b0000. Indicates that the device only occupies 4KB of memory

0x00042fd0.name: DES_2
0x00042fd0.desc: Together, PIDR1.DES_0, PIDR2.DES_1, and PIDR4.DES_2 identify the designer of the component.

0x00042fe0.name: PART_0
0x00042fe0.desc: Bits[7:0] of the 12-bit part number of the component. The designer of the component assigns this part number.

0x00042fe4.name: DES_0
0x00042fe4.desc: Together, PIDR1.DES_0, PIDR2.DES_1, and PIDR4.DES_2 identify the designer of the component.

0x00042fe4.name: PART_1
0x00042fe4.desc: Bits[11:8] of the 12-bit part number of the component. The designer of the component assigns this part number.

0x00042fe8.name: JEDEC
0x00042fe8.desc: Always 1. Indicates that the JEDEC-assigned designer ID is used.

0x00042fe8.name: DES_1
0x00042fe8.desc: Together, PIDR1.DES_0, PIDR2.DES_1, and PIDR4.DES_2 identify the designer of the component.

0x00042fec.name: REVAND
0x00042fec.desc: Indicates minor errata fixes specific to the revision of the component being used, for example metal fixes after implementation. In most cases, this field is 0b0000. ARM recommends that the component designers ensure that a metal fix can change this field if required, for example, by driving it from registers that reset to 0b0000.

0x00042fec.name: CMOD
0x00042fec.desc: Customer Modified. Indicates whether the customer has modified the behavior of the component. In most cases, this field is 0b0000. Customers change this value when they make authorized modifications to this component.

0x00042ff0.name: PRMBL_0
0x00042ff0.desc: Preamble[0]. Contains bits[7:0] of the component identification code

0x00042ff4.name: CLASS
0x00042ff4.desc: Class of the component, for example, whether the component is a ROM table or a generic CoreSight component. Contains bits[15:12] of the component identification code.

0x00042ff4.name: PRMBL_1
0x00042ff4.desc: Preamble[1]. Contains bits[11:8] of the component identification code.

0x00042ff8.name: PRMBL_2
0x00042ff8.desc: Preamble[2]. Contains bits[23:16] of the component identification code.

0x00042ffc.name: PRMBL_3
0x00042ffc.desc: Preamble[3]. Contains bits[31:24] of the component identification code.

0x00000e00.name: STIMENA
0x00000e00.desc: For STIMENA[m] in ITM_TER*n, controls whether ITM_STIM(32*n + m) is enabled

0x00000e40.name: PRIVMASK
0x00000e40.desc: Bit mask to enable tracing on ITM stimulus ports

0x00000e80.name: BUSY
0x00000e80.desc: Indicates whether the ITM is currently processing events

0x00000e80.name: TRACEBUSID
0x00000e80.desc: Identifier for multi-source trace stream formatting. If multi-source trace is in use, the debugger must write a unique non-zero trace ID value to this field

0x00000e80.name: GTSFREQ
0x00000e80.desc: Defines how often the ITM generates a global timestamp, based on the global timestamp clock frequency, or disables generation of global timestamps

0x00000e80.name: TSPRESCALE
0x00000e80.desc: Local timestamp prescaler, used with the trace packet reference clock

0x00000e80.name: STALLENA
0x00000e80.desc: Stall the PE to guarantee delivery of Data Trace packets.

0x00000e80.name: SWOENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables asynchronous clocking of the timestamp counter

0x00000e80.name: TXENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables forwarding of hardware event packet from the DWT unit to the ITM for output to the TPIU

0x00000e80.name: SYNCENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables Synchronization packet transmission for a synchronous TPIU

0x00000e80.name: TSENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables Local timestamp generation

0x00000e80.name: ITMENA
0x00000e80.desc: Enables the ITM

0x00000ef0.name: AFVALID
0x00000ef0.desc: A read of this bit returns the value of AFVALID

0x00000ef0.name: ATREADY
0x00000ef0.desc: A read of this bit returns the value of ATREADY

0x00000ef8.name: AFREADY
0x00000ef8.desc: A write to this bit gives the value of AFREADY

0x00000ef8.name: ATREADY
0x00000ef8.desc: A write to this bit gives the value of ATVALID

0x00000f00.name: IME
0x00000f00.desc: Integration mode enable bit - The possible values are:  0 - The trace unit is not in integration mode. 1 - The trace unit is in integration mode. This mode enables: A debug agent to perform topology detection. SoC test software to perform integration testing.

0x00000fbc.name: ARCHITECT
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines the architect of the component. Bits [31:28] are the JEP106 continuation code (JEP106 bank ID, minus 1) and bits [27:21] are the JEP106 ID code.

0x00000fbc.name: PRESENT
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines that the DEVARCH register is present

0x00000fbc.name: REVISION
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines the architecture revision of the component

0x00000fbc.name: ARCHVER
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines the architecture version of the component

0x00000fbc.name: ARCHPART
0x00000fbc.desc: Defines the architecture of the component

0x00001000.name: NUMCOMP
0x00001000.desc: Number of DWT comparators implemented

0x00001000.name: NOTRCPKT
0x00001000.desc: Indicates whether the implementation does not support trace

0x00001000.name: NOEXTTRIG
0x00001000.desc: Reserved, RAZ

0x00001000.name: NOCYCCNT
0x00001000.desc: Indicates whether the implementation does not include a cycle counter

0x00001000.name: NOPRFCNT
0x00001000.desc: Indicates whether the implementation does not include the profiling counters

0x00001000.name: CYCDISS
0x00001000.desc: Controls whether the cycle counter is disabled in Secure state

0x00001000.name: CYCEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables Event Counter packet generation on POSTCNT underflow

0x00001000.name: FOLDEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables DWT_FOLDCNT counter

0x00001000.name: LSUEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables DWT_LSUCNT counter

0x00001000.name: SLEEPEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enable DWT_SLEEPCNT counter

0x00001000.name: EXCEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables DWT_EXCCNT counter

0x00001000.name: CPIEVTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables DWT_CPICNT counter

0x00001000.name: EXTTRCENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables generation of Exception Trace packets

0x00001000.name: PCSAMPLENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables use of POSTCNT counter as a timer for Periodic PC Sample packet generation

0x00001000.name: SYNCTAP
0x00001000.desc: Selects the position of the synchronization packet counter tap on the CYCCNT counter. This determines the Synchronization packet rate

0x00001000.name: CYCTAP
0x00001000.desc: Selects the position of the POSTCNT tap on the CYCCNT counter

0x00001000.name: POSTINIT
0x00001000.desc: Initial value for the POSTCNT counter

0x00001000.name: POSTPRESET
0x00001000.desc: Reload value for the POSTCNT counter

0x00001000.name: CYCCNTENA
0x00001000.desc: Enables CYCCNT

0x00001004.name: CYCCNT
0x00001004.desc: Increments one on each processor clock cycle when DWT_CTRL.CYCCNTENA == 1 and DEMCR.TRCENA == 1. On overflow, CYCCNT wraps to zero

0x0000100c.name: EXCCNT
0x0000100c.desc: Counts one on each cycle when all of the following are true: - DWT_CTRL.EXCEVTENA == 1 and DEMCR.TRCENA == 1. - No instruction is executed, see DWT_CPICNT. - An exception-entry or exception-exit related operation is in progress. - Either SecureNoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE, or NS-Req for the operation is set to Non-secure and NoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE.

0x00001014.name: LSUCNT
0x00001014.desc: Counts one on each cycle when all of the following are true: - DWT_CTRL.LSUEVTENA == 1 and DEMCR.TRCENA == 1. - No instruction is executed, see DWT_CPICNT. - No exception-entry or exception-exit operation is in progress, see DWT_EXCCNT. - A load-store operation is in progress. - Either SecureNoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE, or NS-Req for the operation is set to Non-secure and NoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE.

0x00001018.name: FOLDCNT
0x00001018.desc: Counts on each cycle when all of the following are true: - DWT_CTRL.FOLDEVTENA == 1 and DEMCR.TRCENA == 1. - At least two instructions are executed, see DWT_CPICNT. - Either SecureNoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE, or the PE is in Non-secure state and NoninvasiveDebugAllowed() == TRUE. The counter is incremented by the number of instructions executed, minus one

0x00001028.name: ID
0x00001028.desc: Identifies the capabilities for MATCH for comparator *n

0x00001028.name: MATCHED
0x00001028.desc: Set to 1 when the comparator matches

0x00001028.name: DATAVSIZE
0x00001028.desc: Defines the size of the object being watched for by Data Value and Data Address comparators

0x00001028.name: ACTION
0x00001028.desc: Defines the action on a match. This field is ignored and the comparator generates no actions if it is disabled by MATCH

0x00001028.name: MATCH
0x00001028.desc: Controls the type of match generated by this comparator

0x00002000.desc: Indicates the number of implemented instruction address comparators. Zero indicates no Instruction Address comparators are implemented. The Instruction Address comparators are numbered from 0 to NUM_CODE - 1

0x00002000.name: NUM_LIT
0x00002000.desc: Indicates the number of implemented literal address comparators. The Literal Address comparators are numbered from NUM_CODE to NUM_CODE + NUM_LIT - 1

0x00002000.name: NUM_CODE_7_4_
0x00002000.desc: Indicates the number of implemented instruction address comparators. Zero indicates no Instruction Address comparators are implemented. The Instruction Address comparators are numbered from 0 to NUM_CODE - 1

0x00002000.name: KEY
0x00002000.desc: Writes to the FP_CTRL are ignored unless KEY is concurrently written to one

0x0000e004.name: INTLINESNUM
0x0000e004.desc: Indicates the number of the highest implemented register in each of the NVIC control register sets, or in the case of NVIC_IPR*n, 4×INTLINESNUM

0x0000e008.name: EXTEXCLALL
0x0000e008.desc: External Exclusives Allowed with no MPU

0x0000e010.name: COUNTFLAG
0x0000e010.desc: Returns 1 if timer counted to 0 since last time this was read. Clears on read by application or debugger.

0x0000e010.name: CLKSOURCE
0x0000e010.desc:
SysTick clock source. Always reads as one if SYST_CALIB reports NOREF. 
                            Selects the SysTick timer clock source: 
                            0 = External reference clock. 
                            1 = Processor clock.

0x0000e010.name: TICKINT
0x0000e010.desc:
Enables SysTick exception request: 
                            0 = Counting down to zero does not assert the SysTick exception request. 
                            1 = Counting down to zero to asserts the SysTick exception request.

0x0000e010.name: ENABLE
0x0000e010.desc:
Enable SysTick counter: 
                            0 = Counter disabled. 
                            1 = Counter enabled.

0x0000e014.name: RELOAD
0x0000e014.desc: Value to load into the SysTick Current Value Register when the counter reaches 0.

0x0000e018.name: CURRENT
0x0000e018.desc: Reads return the current value of the SysTick counter. This register is write-clear. Writing to it with any value clears the register to 0. Clearing this register also clears the COUNTFLAG bit of the SysTick Control and Status Register.

0x0000e01c.name: NOREF
0x0000e01c.desc: If reads as 1, the Reference clock is not provided - the CLKSOURCE bit of the SysTick Control and Status register will be forced to 1 and cannot be cleared to 0.

0x0000e01c.name: SKEW
0x0000e01c.desc: If reads as 1, the calibration value for 10ms is inexact (due to clock frequency).

0x0000e01c.name: TENMS
0x0000e01c.desc: An optional Reload value to be used for 10ms (100Hz) timing, subject to system clock skew errors. If the value reads as 0, the calibration value is not known.
