0x00000000.name: OE
0x00000000.desc: Overrun error. This bit is set to 1 if data is received and the receive FIFO is already full. This is cleared to 0 once there is an empty space in the FIFO and a new character can be written to it.

0x00000000.name: BE
0x00000000.desc: Break error. This bit is set to 1 if a break condition was detected, indicating that the received data input was held LOW for longer than a full-word transmission time (defined as start, data, parity and stop bits). In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO. When a break occurs, only one 0 character is loaded into the FIFO. The next character is only enabled after the receive data input goes to a 1 (marking state), and the next valid start bit is received.

0x00000000.name: PE
0x00000000.desc: Parity error. When set to 1, it indicates that the parity of the received data character does not match the parity that the EPS and SPS bits in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

0x00000000.name: FE
0x00000000.desc: Framing error. When set to 1, it indicates that the received character did not have a valid stop bit (a valid stop bit is 1). In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

0x00000000.name: DATA
0x00000000.desc: Receive (read) data character. Transmit (write) data character.

0x00000004.name: OE
0x00000004.desc: Overrun error. This bit is set to 1 if data is received and the FIFO is already full. This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. The FIFO contents remain valid because no more data is written when the FIFO is full, only the contents of the shift register are overwritten. The CPU must now read the data, to empty the FIFO.

0x00000004.name: BE
0x00000004.desc: Break error. This bit is set to 1 if a break condition was detected, indicating that the received data input was held LOW for longer than a full-word transmission time (defined as start, data, parity, and stop bits). This bit is cleared to 0 after a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO. When a break occurs, only one 0 character is loaded into the FIFO. The next character is only enabled after the receive data input goes to a 1 (marking state) and the next valid start bit is received.

0x00000004.name: PE
0x00000004.desc: Parity error. When set to 1, it indicates that the parity of the received data character does not match the parity that the EPS and SPS bits in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

0x00000004.name: FE
0x00000004.desc: Framing error. When set to 1, it indicates that the received character did not have a valid stop bit (a valid stop bit is 1). This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

0x00000018.name: RI
0x00000018.desc: Ring indicator. This bit is the complement of the UART ring indicator, nUARTRI, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTRI is LOW.

0x00000018.name: TXFE
0x00000018.desc: Transmit FIFO empty. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the transmit holding register is empty. If the FIFO is enabled, the TXFE bit is set when the transmit FIFO is empty. This bit does not indicate if there is data in the transmit shift register.

0x00000018.name: RXFF
0x00000018.desc: Receive FIFO full. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the receive holding register is full. If the FIFO is enabled, the RXFF bit is set when the receive FIFO is full.

0x00000018.name: TXFF
0x00000018.desc: Transmit FIFO full. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the transmit holding register is full. If the FIFO is enabled, the TXFF bit is set when the transmit FIFO is full.

0x00000018.name: RXFE
0x00000018.desc: Receive FIFO empty. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the receive holding register is empty. If the FIFO is enabled, the RXFE bit is set when the receive FIFO is empty.

0x00000018.name: BUSY
0x00000018.desc: UART busy. If this bit is set to 1, the UART is busy transmitting data. This bit remains set until the complete byte, including all the stop bits, has been sent from the shift register. This bit is set as soon as the transmit FIFO becomes non-empty, regardless of whether the UART is enabled or not.

0x00000018.name: DCD
0x00000018.desc: Data carrier detect. This bit is the complement of the UART data carrier detect, nUARTDCD, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTDCD is LOW.

0x00000018.name: DSR
0x00000018.desc: Data set ready. This bit is the complement of the UART data set ready, nUARTDSR, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTDSR is LOW.

0x00000018.name: CTS
0x00000018.desc: Clear to send. This bit is the complement of the UART clear to send, nUARTCTS, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTCTS is LOW.

0x00000020.name: ILPDVSR
0x00000020.desc: 8-bit low-power divisor value. These bits are cleared to 0 at reset.

0x00000024.name: BAUD_DIVINT
0x00000024.desc: The integer baud rate divisor. These bits are cleared to 0 on reset.

0x00000028.name: BAUD_DIVFRAC
0x00000028.desc: The fractional baud rate divisor. These bits are cleared to 0 on reset.

0x0000002c.name: SPS
0x0000002c.desc: Stick parity select. 0 = stick parity is disabled 1 = either: * if the EPS bit is 0 then the parity bit is transmitted and checked as a 1 * if the EPS bit is 1 then the parity bit is transmitted and checked as a 0. This bit has no effect when the PEN bit disables parity checking and generation.

0x0000002c.name: WLEN
0x0000002c.desc: Word length. These bits indicate the number of data bits transmitted or received in a frame as follows: b11 = 8 bits b10 = 7 bits b01 = 6 bits b00 = 5 bits.

0x0000002c.name: FEN
0x0000002c.desc: Enable FIFOs: 0 = FIFOs are disabled (character mode) that is, the FIFOs become 1-byte-deep holding registers 1 = transmit and receive FIFO buffers are enabled (FIFO mode).

0x0000002c.name: STP2
0x0000002c.desc: Two stop bits select. If this bit is set to 1, two stop bits are transmitted at the end of the frame. The receive logic does not check for two stop bits being received.

0x0000002c.name: EPS
0x0000002c.desc: Even parity select. Controls the type of parity the UART uses during transmission and reception: 0 = odd parity. The UART generates or checks for an odd number of 1s in the data and parity bits. 1 = even parity. The UART generates or checks for an even number of 1s in the data and parity bits. This bit has no effect when the PEN bit disables parity checking and generation.

0x0000002c.name: PEN
0x0000002c.desc: Parity enable: 0 = parity is disabled and no parity bit added to the data frame 1 = parity checking and generation is enabled.

0x0000002c.name: BRK
0x0000002c.desc: Send break. If this bit is set to 1, a low-level is continually output on the UARTTXD output, after completing transmission of the current character. For the proper execution of the break command, the software must set this bit for at least two complete frames. For normal use, this bit must be cleared to 0.

0x00000030.name: CTSEN
0x00000030.desc: CTS hardware flow control enable. If this bit is set to 1, CTS hardware flow control is enabled. Data is only transmitted when the nUARTCTS signal is asserted.

0x00000030.name: RTSEN
0x00000030.desc: RTS hardware flow control enable. If this bit is set to 1, RTS hardware flow control is enabled. Data is only requested when there is space in the receive FIFO for it to be received.

0x00000030.name: OUT2
0x00000030.desc: This bit is the complement of the UART Out2 (nUARTOut2) modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1, the output is 0. For DTE this can be used as Ring Indicator (RI).

0x00000030.name: OUT1
0x00000030.desc: This bit is the complement of the UART Out1 (nUARTOut1) modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 the output is 0. For DTE this can be used as Data Carrier Detect (DCD).

0x00000030.name: RTS
0x00000030.desc: Request to send. This bit is the complement of the UART request to send, nUARTRTS, modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 then nUARTRTS is LOW.

0x00000030.name: DTR
0x00000030.desc: Data transmit ready. This bit is the complement of the UART data transmit ready, nUARTDTR, modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 then nUARTDTR is LOW.

0x00000030.name: RXE
0x00000030.desc: Receive enable. If this bit is set to 1, the receive section of the UART is enabled. Data reception occurs for either UART signals or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit. When the UART is disabled in the middle of reception, it completes the current character before stopping.

0x00000030.name: TXE
0x00000030.desc: Transmit enable. If this bit is set to 1, the transmit section of the UART is enabled. Data transmission occurs for either UART signals, or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit. When the UART is disabled in the middle of transmission, it completes the current character before stopping.

0x00000030.name: LBE
0x00000030.desc: Loopback enable. If this bit is set to 1 and the SIREN bit is set to 1 and the SIRTEST bit in the Test Control Register, UARTTCR is set to 1, then the nSIROUT path is inverted, and fed through to the SIRIN path. The SIRTEST bit in the test register must be set to 1 to override the normal half-duplex SIR operation. This must be the requirement for accessing the test registers during normal operation, and SIRTEST must be cleared to 0 when loopback testing is finished. This feature reduces the amount of external coupling required during system test. If this bit is set to 1, and the SIRTEST bit is set to 0, the UARTTXD path is fed through to the UARTRXD path. In either SIR mode or UART mode, when this bit is set, the modem outputs are also fed through to the modem inputs. This bit is cleared to 0 on reset, to disable loopback.

0x00000030.name: SIRLP
0x00000030.desc: SIR low-power IrDA mode. This bit selects the IrDA encoding mode. If this bit is cleared to 0, low-level bits are transmitted as an active high pulse with a width of 3 / 16th of the bit period. If this bit is set to 1, low-level bits are transmitted with a pulse width that is 3 times the period of the IrLPBaud16 input signal, regardless of the selected bit rate. Setting this bit uses less power, but might reduce transmission distances.

0x00000030.name: SIREN
0x00000030.desc: SIR enable: 0 = IrDA SIR ENDEC is disabled. nSIROUT remains LOW (no light pulse generated), and signal transitions on SIRIN have no effect. 1 = IrDA SIR ENDEC is enabled. Data is transmitted and received on nSIROUT and SIRIN. UARTTXD remains HIGH, in the marking state. Signal transitions on UARTRXD or modem status inputs have no effect. This bit has no effect if the UARTEN bit disables the UART.

0x00000030.name: UARTEN
0x00000030.desc: UART enable: 0 = UART is disabled. If the UART is disabled in the middle of transmission or reception, it completes the current character before stopping. 1 = the UART is enabled. Data transmission and reception occurs for either UART signals or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit.

0x00000034.name: RXIFLSEL
0x00000034.desc: Receive interrupt FIFO level select. The trigger points for the receive interrupt are as follows: b000 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 8 full b001 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 4 full b010 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 2 full b011 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 3 / 4 full b100 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 7 / 8 full b101-b111 = reserved.

0x00000034.name: TXIFLSEL
0x00000034.desc: Transmit interrupt FIFO level select. The trigger points for the transmit interrupt are as follows: b000 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 8 full b001 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 4 full b010 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 2 full b011 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 3 / 4 full b100 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 7 / 8 full b101-b111 = reserved.

0x00000038.name: OEIM
0x00000038.desc: Overrun error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTOEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTOEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: BEIM
0x00000038.desc: Break error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTBEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTBEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: PEIM
0x00000038.desc: Parity error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTPEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTPEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: FEIM
0x00000038.desc: Framing error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTFEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTFEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: RTIM
0x00000038.desc: Receive timeout interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTRTINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTRTINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: TXIM
0x00000038.desc: Transmit interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTTXINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTTXINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: RXIM
0x00000038.desc: Receive interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTRXINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTRXINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: DSRMIM
0x00000038.desc: nUARTDSR modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTDSRINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTDSRINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: DCDMIM
0x00000038.desc: nUARTDCD modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTDCDINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTDCDINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: CTSMIM
0x00000038.desc: nUARTCTS modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTCTSINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTCTSINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000048.name: DMAONERR
0x00000048.desc: DMA on error. If this bit is set to 1, the DMA receive request outputs, UARTRXDMASREQ or UARTRXDMABREQ, are disabled when the UART error interrupt is asserted.

0x00000048.name: TXDMAE
0x00000048.desc: Transmit DMA enable. If this bit is set to 1, DMA for the transmit FIFO is enabled.

0x00000048.name: RXDMAE
0x00000048.desc: Receive DMA enable. If this bit is set to 1, DMA for the receive FIFO is enabled.

0x00000fe8.name: REVISION
0x00000fe8.desc: This field depends on the revision of the UART: r1p0 0x0 r1p1 0x1 r1p3 0x2 r1p4 0x2 r1p5 0x3

0x00000000.name: OE
0x00000000.desc: Overrun error. This bit is set to 1 if data is received and the receive FIFO is already full. This is cleared to 0 once there is an empty space in the FIFO and a new character can be written to it.

0x00000000.name: BE
0x00000000.desc: Break error. This bit is set to 1 if a break condition was detected, indicating that the received data input was held LOW for longer than a full-word transmission time (defined as start, data, parity and stop bits). In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO. When a break occurs, only one 0 character is loaded into the FIFO. The next character is only enabled after the receive data input goes to a 1 (marking state), and the next valid start bit is received.

0x00000000.name: PE
0x00000000.desc: Parity error. When set to 1, it indicates that the parity of the received data character does not match the parity that the EPS and SPS bits in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

0x00000000.name: FE
0x00000000.desc: Framing error. When set to 1, it indicates that the received character did not have a valid stop bit (a valid stop bit is 1). In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

0x00000000.name: DATA
0x00000000.desc: Receive (read) data character. Transmit (write) data character.

0x00000004.name: OE
0x00000004.desc: Overrun error. This bit is set to 1 if data is received and the FIFO is already full. This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. The FIFO contents remain valid because no more data is written when the FIFO is full, only the contents of the shift register are overwritten. The CPU must now read the data, to empty the FIFO.

0x00000004.name: BE
0x00000004.desc: Break error. This bit is set to 1 if a break condition was detected, indicating that the received data input was held LOW for longer than a full-word transmission time (defined as start, data, parity, and stop bits). This bit is cleared to 0 after a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO. When a break occurs, only one 0 character is loaded into the FIFO. The next character is only enabled after the receive data input goes to a 1 (marking state) and the next valid start bit is received.

0x00000004.name: PE
0x00000004.desc: Parity error. When set to 1, it indicates that the parity of the received data character does not match the parity that the EPS and SPS bits in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

0x00000004.name: FE
0x00000004.desc: Framing error. When set to 1, it indicates that the received character did not have a valid stop bit (a valid stop bit is 1). This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

0x00000018.name: RI
0x00000018.desc: Ring indicator. This bit is the complement of the UART ring indicator, nUARTRI, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTRI is LOW.

0x00000018.name: TXFE
0x00000018.desc: Transmit FIFO empty. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the transmit holding register is empty. If the FIFO is enabled, the TXFE bit is set when the transmit FIFO is empty. This bit does not indicate if there is data in the transmit shift register.

0x00000018.name: RXFF
0x00000018.desc: Receive FIFO full. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the receive holding register is full. If the FIFO is enabled, the RXFF bit is set when the receive FIFO is full.

0x00000018.name: TXFF
0x00000018.desc: Transmit FIFO full. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the transmit holding register is full. If the FIFO is enabled, the TXFF bit is set when the transmit FIFO is full.

0x00000018.name: RXFE
0x00000018.desc: Receive FIFO empty. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the receive holding register is empty. If the FIFO is enabled, the RXFE bit is set when the receive FIFO is empty.

0x00000018.name: BUSY
0x00000018.desc: UART busy. If this bit is set to 1, the UART is busy transmitting data. This bit remains set until the complete byte, including all the stop bits, has been sent from the shift register. This bit is set as soon as the transmit FIFO becomes non-empty, regardless of whether the UART is enabled or not.

0x00000018.name: DCD
0x00000018.desc: Data carrier detect. This bit is the complement of the UART data carrier detect, nUARTDCD, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTDCD is LOW.

0x00000018.name: DSR
0x00000018.desc: Data set ready. This bit is the complement of the UART data set ready, nUARTDSR, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTDSR is LOW.

0x00000018.name: CTS
0x00000018.desc: Clear to send. This bit is the complement of the UART clear to send, nUARTCTS, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTCTS is LOW.

0x00000020.name: ILPDVSR
0x00000020.desc: 8-bit low-power divisor value. These bits are cleared to 0 at reset.

0x00000024.name: BAUD_DIVINT
0x00000024.desc: The integer baud rate divisor. These bits are cleared to 0 on reset.

0x00000028.name: BAUD_DIVFRAC
0x00000028.desc: The fractional baud rate divisor. These bits are cleared to 0 on reset.

0x0000002c.name: SPS
0x0000002c.desc: Stick parity select. 0 = stick parity is disabled 1 = either: * if the EPS bit is 0 then the parity bit is transmitted and checked as a 1 * if the EPS bit is 1 then the parity bit is transmitted and checked as a 0. This bit has no effect when the PEN bit disables parity checking and generation.

0x0000002c.name: WLEN
0x0000002c.desc: Word length. These bits indicate the number of data bits transmitted or received in a frame as follows: b11 = 8 bits b10 = 7 bits b01 = 6 bits b00 = 5 bits.

0x0000002c.name: FEN
0x0000002c.desc: Enable FIFOs: 0 = FIFOs are disabled (character mode) that is, the FIFOs become 1-byte-deep holding registers 1 = transmit and receive FIFO buffers are enabled (FIFO mode).

0x0000002c.name: STP2
0x0000002c.desc: Two stop bits select. If this bit is set to 1, two stop bits are transmitted at the end of the frame. The receive logic does not check for two stop bits being received.

0x0000002c.name: EPS
0x0000002c.desc: Even parity select. Controls the type of parity the UART uses during transmission and reception: 0 = odd parity. The UART generates or checks for an odd number of 1s in the data and parity bits. 1 = even parity. The UART generates or checks for an even number of 1s in the data and parity bits. This bit has no effect when the PEN bit disables parity checking and generation.

0x0000002c.name: PEN
0x0000002c.desc: Parity enable: 0 = parity is disabled and no parity bit added to the data frame 1 = parity checking and generation is enabled.

0x0000002c.name: BRK
0x0000002c.desc: Send break. If this bit is set to 1, a low-level is continually output on the UARTTXD output, after completing transmission of the current character. For the proper execution of the break command, the software must set this bit for at least two complete frames. For normal use, this bit must be cleared to 0.

0x00000030.name: CTSEN
0x00000030.desc: CTS hardware flow control enable. If this bit is set to 1, CTS hardware flow control is enabled. Data is only transmitted when the nUARTCTS signal is asserted.

0x00000030.name: RTSEN
0x00000030.desc: RTS hardware flow control enable. If this bit is set to 1, RTS hardware flow control is enabled. Data is only requested when there is space in the receive FIFO for it to be received.

0x00000030.name: OUT2
0x00000030.desc: This bit is the complement of the UART Out2 (nUARTOut2) modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1, the output is 0. For DTE this can be used as Ring Indicator (RI).

0x00000030.name: OUT1
0x00000030.desc: This bit is the complement of the UART Out1 (nUARTOut1) modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 the output is 0. For DTE this can be used as Data Carrier Detect (DCD).

0x00000030.name: RTS
0x00000030.desc: Request to send. This bit is the complement of the UART request to send, nUARTRTS, modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 then nUARTRTS is LOW.

0x00000030.name: DTR
0x00000030.desc: Data transmit ready. This bit is the complement of the UART data transmit ready, nUARTDTR, modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 then nUARTDTR is LOW.

0x00000030.name: RXE
0x00000030.desc: Receive enable. If this bit is set to 1, the receive section of the UART is enabled. Data reception occurs for either UART signals or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit. When the UART is disabled in the middle of reception, it completes the current character before stopping.

0x00000030.name: TXE
0x00000030.desc: Transmit enable. If this bit is set to 1, the transmit section of the UART is enabled. Data transmission occurs for either UART signals, or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit. When the UART is disabled in the middle of transmission, it completes the current character before stopping.

0x00000030.name: LBE
0x00000030.desc: Loopback enable. If this bit is set to 1 and the SIREN bit is set to 1 and the SIRTEST bit in the Test Control Register, UARTTCR is set to 1, then the nSIROUT path is inverted, and fed through to the SIRIN path. The SIRTEST bit in the test register must be set to 1 to override the normal half-duplex SIR operation. This must be the requirement for accessing the test registers during normal operation, and SIRTEST must be cleared to 0 when loopback testing is finished. This feature reduces the amount of external coupling required during system test. If this bit is set to 1, and the SIRTEST bit is set to 0, the UARTTXD path is fed through to the UARTRXD path. In either SIR mode or UART mode, when this bit is set, the modem outputs are also fed through to the modem inputs. This bit is cleared to 0 on reset, to disable loopback.

0x00000030.name: SIRLP
0x00000030.desc: SIR low-power IrDA mode. This bit selects the IrDA encoding mode. If this bit is cleared to 0, low-level bits are transmitted as an active high pulse with a width of 3 / 16th of the bit period. If this bit is set to 1, low-level bits are transmitted with a pulse width that is 3 times the period of the IrLPBaud16 input signal, regardless of the selected bit rate. Setting this bit uses less power, but might reduce transmission distances.

0x00000030.name: SIREN
0x00000030.desc: SIR enable: 0 = IrDA SIR ENDEC is disabled. nSIROUT remains LOW (no light pulse generated), and signal transitions on SIRIN have no effect. 1 = IrDA SIR ENDEC is enabled. Data is transmitted and received on nSIROUT and SIRIN. UARTTXD remains HIGH, in the marking state. Signal transitions on UARTRXD or modem status inputs have no effect. This bit has no effect if the UARTEN bit disables the UART.

0x00000030.name: UARTEN
0x00000030.desc: UART enable: 0 = UART is disabled. If the UART is disabled in the middle of transmission or reception, it completes the current character before stopping. 1 = the UART is enabled. Data transmission and reception occurs for either UART signals or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit.

0x00000034.name: RXIFLSEL
0x00000034.desc: Receive interrupt FIFO level select. The trigger points for the receive interrupt are as follows: b000 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 8 full b001 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 4 full b010 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 2 full b011 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 3 / 4 full b100 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 7 / 8 full b101-b111 = reserved.

0x00000034.name: TXIFLSEL
0x00000034.desc: Transmit interrupt FIFO level select. The trigger points for the transmit interrupt are as follows: b000 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 8 full b001 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 4 full b010 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 2 full b011 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 3 / 4 full b100 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 7 / 8 full b101-b111 = reserved.

0x00000038.desc: Overrun error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTOEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTOEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: BEIM
0x00000038.desc: Break error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTBEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTBEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.desc: Receive interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTRXINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTRXINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.desc: nUARTDSR modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTDSRINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTDSRINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.name: DCDMIM
0x00000038.desc: nUARTDCD modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTDCDINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTDCDINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000038.desc: nUARTRI modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTRIINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTRIINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

0x00000fe8.name: REVISION
0x00000fe8.desc: This field depends on the revision of the UART: r1p0 0x0 r1p1 0x1 r1p3 0x2 r1p4 0x2 r1p5 0x3

0x00000000.name: ENABLE
0x00000000.desc:
On power-up this field is initialised to ENABLE 
                            The system clock must be switched to another source before setting this field to DISABLE otherwise the chip will lock up 
                            The 12-bit code is intended to give some protection against accidental writes. An invalid setting will enable the oscillator.

0x00000000.name: FREQ_RANGE
0x00000000.desc:
Controls the number of delay stages in the ROSC ring 
                            LOW uses stages 0 to 7 
                            MEDIUM uses stages 0 to 5
                            HIGH uses stages 0 to 3
                            TOOHIGH uses stages 0 to 1 and should not be used because its frequency exceeds design specifications
                            The clock output will not glitch when changing the range up one step at a time 
                            The clock output will glitch when changing the range down 
                            Note: the values here are gray coded which is why HIGH comes before TOOHIGH

0x00000004.name: PASSWD
0x00000004.desc:
Set to 0x9696 to apply the settings 
                            Any other value in this field will set all drive strengths to 0

0x00000004.name: DS3
0x00000004.desc: Stage 3 drive strength

0x00000004.name: DS2
0x00000004.desc: Stage 2 drive strength

0x00000004.name: DS1_RANDOM
0x00000004.desc: Randomises the stage 1 drive strength

0x00000004.name: DS1
0x00000004.desc: Stage 1 drive strength

0x00000004.name: DS0_RANDOM
0x00000004.desc: Randomises the stage 0 drive strength

0x00000004.name: DS0
0x00000004.desc: Stage 0 drive strength

0x00000008.name: PASSWD
0x00000008.desc:
Set to 0x9696 to apply the settings 
                            Any other value in this field will set all drive strengths to 0

0x00000008.name: DS7
0x00000008.desc: Stage 7 drive strength

0x00000008.name: DS6
0x00000008.desc: Stage 6 drive strength

0x00000008.name: DS5
0x00000008.desc: Stage 5 drive strength

0x00000008.name: DS4
0x00000008.desc: Stage 4 drive strength

0x00000010.name: DORMANT
0x00000010.desc:
This is used to save power by pausing the ROSC 
                            On power-up this field is initialised to WAKE 
                            An invalid write will also select WAKE 
                            Warning: setup the irq before selecting dormant mode

0x00000014.name: DIV
0x00000014.desc:
set to 0xaa00 + div where 
                            div = 0 divides by 128 
                            div = 1-127 divides by div 
                            any other value sets div=128 
                            this register resets to div=32

0x00000018.name: PASSWD
0x00000018.desc:
set to 0xaa 
                            any other value enables the output with shift=0

0x00000018.name: ENABLE
0x00000018.desc:
enable the phase-shifted output 
                            this can be changed on-the-fly

0x00000018.name: FLIP
0x00000018.desc:
invert the phase-shifted output 
                            this is ignored when div=1

0x00000018.name: SHIFT
0x00000018.desc:
phase shift the phase-shifted output by SHIFT input clocks 
                            this can be changed on-the-fly 
                            must be set to 0 before setting div=1

0x0000001c.name: STABLE
0x0000001c.desc: Oscillator is running and stable

0x0000001c.name: BADWRITE
0x0000001c.desc: An invalid value has been written to CTRL_ENABLE or CTRL_FREQ_RANGE or FREQA or FREQB or DIV or PHASE or DORMANT

0x0000001c.name: DIV_RUNNING
0x0000001c.desc:
post-divider is running 
                            this resets to 0 but transitions to 1 during chip startup

0x0000001c.name: ENABLED
0x0000001c.desc:
Oscillator is enabled but not necessarily running and stable 
                            this resets to 0 but transitions to 1 during chip startup

0x00000004.name: RST_N
0x00000004.desc:
returns the regulator to its startup settings 
                            0 - reset 
                            1 - not reset (default)

0x00000004.name: UNLOCK
0x00000004.desc:
unlocks the VREG control interface after power up 
                            0 - Locked (default) 
                            1 - Unlocked 
                            It cannot be relocked when it is unlocked.

0x00000004.name: ISOLATE
0x00000004.desc:
isolates the VREG control interface 
                            0 - not isolated (default) 
                            1 - isolated

0x00000004.name: DISABLE_VOLTAGE_LIMIT
0x00000004.desc: 0=not disabled, 1=enabled

0x00000004.name: HT_TH
0x00000004.desc:
high temperature protection threshold 
                            regulator power transistors are disabled when junction temperature exceeds threshold 
                            000 - 100C 
                            001 - 105C 
                            010 - 110C 
                            011 - 115C 
                            100 - 120C 
                            101 - 125C 
                            110 - 135C 
                            111 - 150C

0x00000008.name: VOUT_OK
0x00000008.desc:
output regulation status 
                            0=not in regulation, 1=in regulation

0x00000008.name: STARTUP
0x00000008.desc:
startup status 
                            0=startup complete, 1=starting up

0x0000000c.name: UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS
0x0000000c.desc:
regulator state is being updated 
                            writes to the vreg register will be ignored when this field is set

0x0000000c.name: HIZ
0x0000000c.desc:
high impedance mode select 
                            0=not in high impedance mode, 1=in high impedance mode

0x00000010.name: MODE
0x00000010.desc:
selects either normal (switching) mode or low power (linear) mode 
                            low power mode can only be selected for output voltages up to 1.3V 
                            0 = normal mode (switching) 
                            1 = low power mode (linear)

0x00000010.name: HIZ
0x00000010.desc:
high impedance mode select 
                            0=not in high impedance mode, 1=in high impedance mode

0x00000014.name: MODE
0x00000014.desc:
selects either normal (switching) mode or low power (linear) mode 
                            low power mode can only be selected for output voltages up to 1.3V 
                            0 = normal mode (switching) 
                            1 = low power mode (linear)

0x00000014.name: HIZ
0x00000014.desc:
high impedance mode select 
                            0=not in high impedance mode, 1=in high impedance mode

0x00000018.name: ISOLATE
0x00000018.desc:
isolates the brown-out detection control interface 
                            0 - not isolated (default) 
                            1 - isolated

0x0000001c.name: EN
0x0000001c.desc:
enable brown-out detection 
                            0=not enabled, 1=enabled

0x00000020.name: EN
0x00000020.desc:
enable brown-out detection 
                            0=not enabled, 1=enabled

0x00000024.name: EN
0x00000024.desc:
enable brown-out detection 
                            0=not enabled, 1=enabled

0x00000028.name: TRIM
0x00000028.desc: Frequency trim - the trim step is typically 1% of the reset frequency, but can be up to 3%

0x00000028.name: MODE
0x00000028.desc: This feature has been removed

0x0000002c.name: HAD_WATCHDOG_RESET_RSM
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was a watchdog timeout which was configured to reset the power-on state machine 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        no 
                            timer              no 
                            powman             no 
                            swcore             no 
                            psm                yes 
                            and does not change the power state

0x0000002c.name: HAD_HZD_SYS_RESET_REQ
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was a system reset from the hazard debugger 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        no 
                            timer              no 
                            powman             no 
                            swcore             no 
                            psm                yes 
                            and does not change the power state

0x0000002c.name: HAD_GLITCH_DETECT
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was due to a power supply glitch 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        no 
                            timer              no 
                            powman             no 
                            swcore             no 
                            psm                yes 
                            and does not change the power state

0x0000002c.name: HAD_SWCORE_PD
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was a switched core powerdown 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        no 
                            timer              no 
                            powman             no 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: HAD_WATCHDOG_RESET_SWCORE
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was a watchdog timeout which was configured to reset the switched-core 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        no 
                            timer              no 
                            powman             no 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: HAD_WATCHDOG_RESET_POWMAN
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was a watchdog timeout which was configured to reset the power manager 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        no 
                            timer              yes 
                            powman             yes 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: HAD_WATCHDOG_RESET_POWMAN_ASYNC
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was a watchdog timeout which was configured to reset the power manager asynchronously 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        no 
                            timer              yes 
                            powman             yes 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: HAD_RESCUE
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was a rescue reset from the debugger 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        no, it sets this flag 
                            timer              yes 
                            powman             yes 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: HAD_DP_RESET_REQ
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was an reset request from the arm debugger 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 no 
                            RPAP               no 
                            rescue_flag        yes 
                            timer              yes 
                            powman             yes 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: HAD_RUN_LOW
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was from the RUN pin 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    no 
                            DP                 yes 
                            RPAP               yes 
                            rescue_flag        yes 
                            timer              yes 
                            powman             yes 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: HAD_BOR
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was from the brown-out detection block 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    yes 
                            DP                 yes 
                            RPAP               yes 
                            rescue_flag        yes 
                            timer              yes 
                            powman             yes 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: HAD_POR
0x0000002c.desc:
Last reset was from the power-on reset 
                            This resets: 
                            double_tap flag    yes 
                            DP                 yes 
                            RPAP               yes 
                            rescue_flag        yes 
                            timer              yes 
                            powman             yes 
                            swcore             yes 
                            psm                yes 
                            then starts the power sequencer

0x0000002c.name: RESCUE_FLAG
0x0000002c.desc:
This is set by a rescue reset from the RP-AP. 
                            Its purpose is to halt before the bootrom before booting from flash in order to recover from a boot lock-up. 
                            The debugger can then attach once the bootrom has been halted and flash some working code that does not lock up.

0x0000002c.name: DOUBLE_TAP
0x0000002c.desc: This flag is set by double-tapping RUN. It tells bootcode to go into the bootloader.

0x00000030.name: RESET_RSM
0x00000030.desc:
If set to 1, a watchdog reset will run the full power-on state machine (PSM) sequence 
                            From a user perspective it is the same as setting RSM_WDSEL_PROC_COLD 
                            From a hardware debug perspective it has the same effect as a reset from a glitch detector

0x00000030.name: RESET_SWCORE
0x00000030.desc:
If set to 1, a watchdog reset will reset the switched core power domain and run the full power-on state machine (PSM) sequence 
                            From a user perspective it is the same as setting RSM_WDSEL_PROC_COLD 
                            From a hardware debug perspective it has the same effect as a power-on reset for the switched core power domain

0x00000030.name: RESET_POWMAN
0x00000030.desc:
If set to 1, a watchdog reset will restore powman defaults, reset the timer, reset the switched core power domain 
                            and run the full power-on state machine (PSM) sequence 
                            This relies on clk_ref running. Use reset_powman_async if that may not be true

0x00000030.name: RESET_POWMAN_ASYNC
0x00000030.desc:
If set to 1, a watchdog reset will restore powman defaults, reset the timer, 
                            reset the switched core domain and run the full power-on state machine (PSM) sequence 
                            This does not rely on clk_ref running

0x00000034.name: USING_FAST_POWCK
0x00000034.desc:
0 indicates the POWMAN clock is running from the low power oscillator (32kHz) 
                            1 indicates the POWMAN clock is running from the reference clock (2-50MHz)

0x00000034.name: USING_BOD_LP
0x00000034.desc:
Indicates the brown-out detector (BOD) mode 
                            0 = BOD high power mode which is the default 
                            1 = BOD low power mode

0x00000034.name: USING_VREG_LP
0x00000034.desc:
Indicates the voltage regulator (VREG) mode 
                            0 = VREG high power mode which is the default 
                            1 = VREG low power mode

0x00000034.name: USE_FAST_POWCK
0x00000034.desc:
selects the reference clock (clk_ref) as the source of the POWMAN clock when switched-core is powered. The POWMAN clock always switches to the slow clock (lposc) when switched-core is powered down because the fast clock stops running. 
                            0 always run the POWMAN clock from the slow clock (lposc) 
                            1 run the POWMAN clock from the fast clock when available 
                            This setting takes effect when a power up sequence is next run

0x00000034.name: RUN_LPOSC_IN_LP
0x00000034.desc:
Set to 0 to stop the low power osc when the switched-core is powered down, which is unwise if using it to clock the timer 
                            This setting takes effect when the swcore is next powered down

0x00000034.name: USE_BOD_HP
0x00000034.desc:
Set to 0 to prevent automatic switching to bod high power mode when switched-core is powered up 
                            This setting takes effect when the swcore is next powered up

0x00000034.name: USE_BOD_LP
0x00000034.desc:
Set to 0 to prevent automatic switching to bod low power mode when switched-core is powered down 
                            This setting takes effect when the swcore is next powered down

0x00000034.name: USE_VREG_HP
0x00000034.desc:
Set to 0 to prevent automatic switching to vreg high power mode when switched-core is powered up 
                            This setting takes effect when the swcore is next powered up

0x00000034.name: USE_VREG_LP
0x00000034.desc:
Set to 0 to prevent automatic switching to vreg low power mode when switched-core is powered down 
                            This setting takes effect when the swcore is next powered down

0x00000034.name: HW_PWRUP_SRAM0
0x00000034.desc:
Specifies the power state of SRAM0 when powering up swcore from a low power state (P1.xxx) to a high power state (P0.0xx). 
                            0=power-up 
                            1=no change

0x00000034.name: HW_PWRUP_SRAM1
0x00000034.desc:
Specifies the power state of SRAM1 when powering up swcore from a low power state (P1.xxx) to a high power state (P0.0xx). 
                            0=power-up 
                            1=no change

0x00000038.name: CHANGING
0x00000038.desc: Indicates a power state change is in progress

0x00000038.name: WAITING
0x00000038.desc: Indicates the power manager has received a state change request and is waiting for other actions to complete before executing it

0x00000038.name: BAD_HW_REQ
0x00000038.desc: Invalid hardware initiated state request, power up requests actioned, power down requests ignored

0x00000038.name: BAD_SW_REQ
0x00000038.desc: Invalid software initiated state request ignored

0x00000038.name: PWRUP_WHILE_WAITING
0x00000038.desc: Indicates that a power state change request was ignored because of a pending power state change request

0x00000038.name: REQ_IGNORED
0x00000038.desc: Indicates that a software state change request was ignored because it clashed with an ongoing hardware or debugger request

0x00000038.name: REQ
0x00000038.desc: This is written by software or hardware to request a new power state

0x00000038.name: CURRENT
0x00000038.desc: Indicates the current power state

0x0000003c.name: POW_FASTDIV
0x0000003c.desc:
divides the POWMAN clock to provide a tick for the delay module and state machines 
                            when clk_pow is running from the slow clock it is not divided 
                            when clk_pow is running from the fast clock it is divided by tick_div

0x00000040.name: SRAM_STEP
0x00000040.desc:
timing between the sram0 and sram1 power state machine steps 
                            measured in units of the powman tick period (>=1us), 0 gives a delay of 1 unit

0x00000040.name: XIP_STEP
0x00000040.desc:
timing between the xip power state machine steps 
                            measured in units of the lposc period, 0 gives a delay of 1 unit

0x00000040.name: SWCORE_STEP
0x00000040.desc:
timing between the swcore power state machine steps 
                            measured in units of the lposc period, 0 gives a delay of 1 unit

0x00000044.name: LP_EXIT_STATE
0x00000044.desc: output level when exiting the low power state

0x00000044.name: LP_ENTRY_STATE
0x00000044.desc: output level when entering the low power state

0x00000044.name: GPIO_SELECT
0x00000044.desc:
selects from gpio 0->30 
                            set to 31 to disable this feature

0x00000048.name: LP_EXIT_STATE
0x00000048.desc: output level when exiting the low power state

0x00000048.name: LP_ENTRY_STATE
0x00000048.desc: output level when entering the low power state

0x00000048.name: GPIO_SELECT
0x00000048.desc:
selects from gpio 0->30 
                            set to 31 to disable this feature

0x0000004c.name: DRIVE_LPCK
0x0000004c.desc: Use the selected GPIO to drive the 32kHz low power clock, in place of LPOSC. This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0

0x0000004c.name: SOURCE_SEL
0x0000004c.desc:
0 ->  gpio12 
                            1 ->  gpio20 
                            2 ->  gpio14 
                            3 ->  gpio22

0x00000050.name: LPOSC_FREQ_KHZ_INT
0x00000050.desc: Integer component of the LPOSC or GPIO clock source frequency in kHz. Default = 32 This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0 or POWMAN_TIMER_USING_XOSC=1

0x00000054.name: LPOSC_FREQ_KHZ_FRAC
0x00000054.desc: Fractional component of the LPOSC or GPIO clock source frequency in kHz. Default = 0.768 This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0 or POWMAN_TIMER_USING_XOSC=1

0x00000058.name: XOSC_FREQ_KHZ_INT
0x00000058.desc: Integer component of the XOSC frequency in kHz. Default = 12000 Must be >1 This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0 or POWMAN_TIMER_USING_XOSC=0

0x0000005c.name: XOSC_FREQ_KHZ_FRAC
0x0000005c.desc: Fractional component of the XOSC frequency in kHz. This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0 or POWMAN_TIMER_USING_XOSC=0

0x00000060.name: SET_TIME_63TO48
0x00000060.desc: For setting the time, do not use for reading the time, use POWMAN_READ_TIME_UPPER and POWMAN_READ_TIME_LOWER. This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0

0x00000064.name: SET_TIME_47TO32
0x00000064.desc: For setting the time, do not use for reading the time, use POWMAN_READ_TIME_UPPER and POWMAN_READ_TIME_LOWER. This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0

0x00000068.name: SET_TIME_31TO16
0x00000068.desc: For setting the time, do not use for reading the time, use POWMAN_READ_TIME_UPPER and POWMAN_READ_TIME_LOWER. This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0

0x0000006c.name: SET_TIME_15TO0
0x0000006c.desc: For setting the time, do not use for reading the time, use POWMAN_READ_TIME_UPPER and POWMAN_READ_TIME_LOWER. This field must only be written when POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=0

0x00000070.name: READ_TIME_UPPER
0x00000070.desc: For reading bits 63:32 of the timer. When reading all 64 bits it is possible for the LOWER count to rollover during the read. It is recommended to read UPPER, then LOWER, then re-read UPPER and, if it has changed, re-read LOWER.

0x00000074.name: READ_TIME_LOWER
0x00000074.desc: For reading bits 31:0 of the timer.

0x00000078.name: ALARM_TIME_63TO48
0x00000078.desc: This field must only be written when POWMAN_ALARM_ENAB=0

0x0000007c.name: ALARM_TIME_47TO32
0x0000007c.desc: This field must only be written when POWMAN_ALARM_ENAB=0

0x00000080.name: ALARM_TIME_31TO16
0x00000080.desc: This field must only be written when POWMAN_ALARM_ENAB=0

0x00000084.name: ALARM_TIME_15TO0
0x00000084.desc: This field must only be written when POWMAN_ALARM_ENAB=0

0x00000088.name: USING_GPIO_1HZ
0x00000088.desc: Timer is synchronised to a 1hz gpio source

0x00000088.name: USING_GPIO_1KHZ
0x00000088.desc: Timer is running from a 1khz gpio source

0x00000088.name: USING_LPOSC
0x00000088.desc: Timer is running from lposc

0x00000088.name: USING_XOSC
0x00000088.desc: Timer is running from xosc

0x00000088.name: USE_GPIO_1HZ
0x00000088.desc: Selects the gpio source as the reference for the sec counter. The msec counter will continue to use the lposc or xosc reference.

0x00000088.name: USE_GPIO_1KHZ
0x00000088.desc: switch to gpio as the source of the 1kHz timer tick

0x00000088.name: USE_XOSC
0x00000088.desc: switch to xosc as the source of the 1kHz timer tick

0x00000088.name: USE_LPOSC
0x00000088.desc: Switch to lposc as the source of the 1kHz timer tick

0x00000088.name: ALARM
0x00000088.desc: Alarm has fired. Write to 1 to clear the alarm.

0x00000088.name: PWRUP_ON_ALARM
0x00000088.desc: Alarm wakes the chip from low power mode

0x00000088.name: ALARM_ENAB
0x00000088.desc: Enables the alarm. The alarm must be disabled while writing the alarm time.

0x00000088.name: CLEAR
0x00000088.desc: Clears the timer, does not disable the timer and does not affect the alarm. This control can be written at any time.

0x00000088.name: RUN
0x00000088.desc:
Timer enable. Setting this bit causes the timer to begin counting up from its current value. Clearing this bit stops the timer from counting.

                            Before enabling the timer, set the POWMAN_LPOSC_FREQ* and POWMAN_XOSC_FREQ* registers to configure the count rate, and initialise the current time by writing to SET_TIME_63TO48 through SET_TIME_15TO0. You must not write to the SET_TIME_x registers when the timer is running.

                            Once configured, start the timer by setting POWMAN_TIMER_RUN=1. This will start the timer running from the LPOSC. When the XOSC is available switch the reference clock to XOSC then select it as the timer clock by setting POWMAN_TIMER_USE_XOSC=1

0x00000088.name: NONSEC_WRITE
0x00000088.desc: Control whether Non-secure software can write to the timer registers. All other registers are hardwired to be inaccessible to Non-secure.

0x0000008c.name: RAW_STATUS
0x0000008c.desc: Value of selected gpio pin (only if enable == 1)

0x0000008c.name: STATUS
0x0000008c.desc: Status of gpio wakeup. Write to 1 to clear a latched edge detect.

0x0000008c.name: MODE
0x0000008c.desc: Edge or level detect. Edge will detect a 0 to 1 transition (or 1 to 0 transition). Level will detect a 1 or 0. Both types of event get latched into the current_pwrup_req register.

0x0000008c.name: ENABLE
0x0000008c.desc:
Set to 1 to enable the wakeup source. Set to 0 to disable the wakeup source and clear a pending wakeup event. 
                            If using edge detect a latched edge needs to be cleared by writing 1 to the status register also.

0x00000090.name: RAW_STATUS
0x00000090.desc: Value of selected gpio pin (only if enable == 1)

0x00000090.name: STATUS
0x00000090.desc: Status of gpio wakeup. Write to 1 to clear a latched edge detect.

0x00000090.name: MODE
0x00000090.desc: Edge or level detect. Edge will detect a 0 to 1 transition (or 1 to 0 transition). Level will detect a 1 or 0. Both types of event get latched into the current_pwrup_req register.

0x00000090.name: ENABLE
0x00000090.desc:
Set to 1 to enable the wakeup source. Set to 0 to disable the wakeup source and clear a pending wakeup event. 
                            If using edge detect a latched edge needs to be cleared by writing 1 to the status register also.

0x00000094.name: RAW_STATUS
0x00000094.desc: Value of selected gpio pin (only if enable == 1)

0x00000094.name: STATUS
0x00000094.desc: Status of gpio wakeup. Write to 1 to clear a latched edge detect.

0x00000094.name: MODE
0x00000094.desc: Edge or level detect. Edge will detect a 0 to 1 transition (or 1 to 0 transition). Level will detect a 1 or 0. Both types of event get latched into the current_pwrup_req register.

0x00000094.name: ENABLE
0x00000094.desc:
Set to 1 to enable the wakeup source. Set to 0 to disable the wakeup source and clear a pending wakeup event. 
                            If using edge detect a latched edge needs to be cleared by writing 1 to the status register also.

0x00000098.name: RAW_STATUS
0x00000098.desc: Value of selected gpio pin (only if enable == 1)

0x00000098.name: STATUS
0x00000098.desc: Status of gpio wakeup. Write to 1 to clear a latched edge detect.

0x00000098.name: MODE
0x00000098.desc: Edge or level detect. Edge will detect a 0 to 1 transition (or 1 to 0 transition). Level will detect a 1 or 0. Both types of event get latched into the current_pwrup_req register.

0x00000098.name: ENABLE
0x00000098.desc:
Set to 1 to enable the wakeup source. Set to 0 to disable the wakeup source and clear a pending wakeup event. 
                            If using edge detect a latched edge needs to be cleared by writing 1 to the status register also.

0x000000a4.name: IGNORE
0x000000a4.desc: Ignore pwrup req from debugger. If pwrup req is asserted then this will prevent power down and set powerdown blocked. Set ignore to stop paying attention to pwrup_req

0x000000a8.name: NEXT
0x000000a8.desc:
This flag always ORs writes into its current contents. It can be set but not cleared by software.

                            The BOOTDIS_NEXT bit is OR'd into the BOOTDIS_NOW bit when the core is powered down. Simultaneously, the BOOTDIS_NEXT bit is cleared. Setting this bit means that the BOOT0..3 registers will be ignored following the next reset of the RSM by powman.

                            This flag should be set by an early boot stage that has soft-locked OTP pages, to prevent later stages from unlocking it by power cycling.

0x000000a8.name: NOW
0x000000a8.desc:
When powman resets the RSM, the current value of BOOTDIS_NEXT is OR'd into BOOTDIS_NOW, and BOOTDIS_NEXT is cleared.

                            The bootrom checks this flag before reading the BOOT0..3 registers. If it is set, the bootrom clears it, and ignores the BOOT registers. This prevents Secure software from diverting the boot path before a bootloader has had the chance to soft lock OTP pages containing sensitive data.

Configure DP instance ID for SWD multidrop selection. 
                            Recommend that this is NOT changed until you require debug access in multi-chip environment

0x00000000.name: TRIGGER
0x00000000.desc: Trigger a watchdog reset

0x00000000.name: ENABLE
0x00000000.desc: When not enabled the watchdog timer is paused

0x00000000.name: PAUSE_DBG1
0x00000000.desc: Pause the watchdog timer when processor 1 is in debug mode

0x00000000.name: PAUSE_DBG0
0x00000000.desc: Pause the watchdog timer when processor 0 is in debug mode

0x00000000.name: PAUSE_JTAG
0x00000000.desc: Pause the watchdog timer when JTAG is accessing the bus fabric

0x00000000.name: TIME
0x00000000.desc: Indicates the time in usec before a watchdog reset will be triggered

0x00000000.name: CH0_READ_ADDR
0x00000000.desc: This register updates automatically each time a read completes. The current value is the next address to be read by this channel.

0x00000004.name: CH0_WRITE_ADDR
0x00000004.desc: This register updates automatically each time a write completes. The current value is the next address to be written by this channel.

0x00000008.name: MODE
0x00000008.desc:
When MODE is 0x0, the transfer count decrements with each transfer until 0, and then the channel triggers the next channel indicated by CTRL_CHAIN_TO.

                            When MODE is 0x1, the transfer count decrements with each transfer until 0, and then the channel re-triggers itself, in addition to the trigger indicated by CTRL_CHAIN_TO. This is useful for e.g. an endless ring-buffer DMA with periodic interrupts.

                            When MODE is 0xf, the transfer count does not decrement. The DMA channel performs an endless sequence of transfers, never triggering other channels or raising interrupts, until an ABORT is raised.

                            All other values are reserved.

0x00000008.name: COUNT
0x00000008.desc:
28-bit transfer count (256 million transfers maximum).

                            Program the number of bus transfers a channel will perform before halting. Note that, if transfers are larger than one byte in size, this is not equal to the number of bytes transferred (see CTRL_DATA_SIZE).

                            When the channel is active, reading this register shows the number of transfers remaining, updating automatically each time a write transfer completes.

                            Writing this register sets the RELOAD value for the transfer counter. Each time this channel is triggered, the RELOAD value is copied into the live transfer counter. The channel can be started multiple times, and will perform the same number of transfers each time, as programmed by most recent write.

                            The RELOAD value can be observed at CHx_DBG_TCR. If TRANS_COUNT is used as a trigger, the written value is used immediately as the length of the new transfer sequence, as well as being written to RELOAD.

0x0000000c.name: AHB_ERROR
0x0000000c.desc: Logical OR of the READ_ERROR and WRITE_ERROR flags. The channel halts when it encounters any bus error, and always raises its channel IRQ flag.

0x0000000c.name: READ_ERROR
0x0000000c.desc:
If 1, the channel received a read bus error. Write one to clear. 
                            READ_ADDR shows the approximate address where the bus error was encountered (will not be earlier, or more than 3 transfers later)

0x0000000c.name: WRITE_ERROR
0x0000000c.desc:
If 1, the channel received a write bus error. Write one to clear. 
                            WRITE_ADDR shows the approximate address where the bus error was encountered (will not be earlier, or more than 5 transfers later)

0x0000000c.name: BUSY
0x0000000c.desc:
This flag goes high when the channel starts a new transfer sequence, and low when the last transfer of that sequence completes. Clearing EN while BUSY is high pauses the channel, and BUSY will stay high while paused.

                            To terminate a sequence early (and clear the BUSY flag), see CHAN_ABORT.

0x0000000c.name: SNIFF_EN
0x0000000c.desc:
If 1, this channel's data transfers are visible to the sniff hardware, and each transfer will advance the state of the checksum. This only applies if the sniff hardware is enabled, and has this channel selected.

                            This allows checksum to be enabled or disabled on a per-control- block basis.

0x0000000c.name: BSWAP
0x0000000c.desc:
Apply byte-swap transformation to DMA data. 
                            For byte data, this has no effect. For halfword data, the two bytes of each halfword are swapped. For word data, the four bytes of each word are swapped to reverse order.

0x0000000c.name: IRQ_QUIET
0x0000000c.desc:
In QUIET mode, the channel does not generate IRQs at the end of every transfer block. Instead, an IRQ is raised when NULL is written to a trigger register, indicating the end of a control block chain.

                            This reduces the number of interrupts to be serviced by the CPU when transferring a DMA chain of many small control blocks.

0x0000000c.name: TREQ_SEL
0x0000000c.desc:
Select a Transfer Request signal. 
                            The channel uses the transfer request signal to pace its data transfer rate. Sources for TREQ signals are internal (TIMERS) or external (DREQ, a Data Request from the system). 
                            0x0 to 0x3a -> select DREQ n as TREQ

0x0000000c.name: CHAIN_TO
0x0000000c.desc:
When this channel completes, it will trigger the channel indicated by CHAIN_TO. Disable by setting CHAIN_TO = _(this channel)_.

                            Note this field resets to 0, so channels 1 and above will chain to channel 0 by default. Set this field to avoid this behaviour.

0x0000000c.name: RING_SEL
0x0000000c.desc:
Select whether RING_SIZE applies to read or write addresses. 
                            If 0, read addresses are wrapped on a (1 << RING_SIZE) boundary. If 1, write addresses are wrapped.

0x0000000c.name: RING_SIZE
0x0000000c.desc:
Size of address wrap region. If 0, don't wrap. For values n > 0, only the lower n bits of the address will change. This wraps the address on a (1 << n) byte boundary, facilitating access to naturally-aligned ring buffers.

                            Ring sizes between 2 and 32768 bytes are possible. This can apply to either read or write addresses, based on value of RING_SEL.

0x0000000c.name: INCR_WRITE_REV
0x0000000c.desc:
If 1, and INCR_WRITE is 1, the write address is decremented rather than incremented with each transfer.

                            If 1, and INCR_WRITE is 0, this otherwise-unused combination causes the write address to be incremented by twice the transfer size, i.e. skipping over alternate addresses.

0x0000000c.name: INCR_WRITE
0x0000000c.desc:
If 1, the write address increments with each transfer. If 0, each write is directed to the same, initial address.

                            Generally this should be disabled for memory-to-peripheral transfers.

0x0000000c.name: INCR_READ_REV
0x0000000c.desc:
If 1, and INCR_READ is 1, the read address is decremented rather than incremented with each transfer.

                            If 1, and INCR_READ is 0, this otherwise-unused combination causes the read address to be incremented by twice the transfer size, i.e. skipping over alternate addresses.

0x0000000c.name: INCR_READ
0x0000000c.desc:
If 1, the read address increments with each transfer. If 0, each read is directed to the same, initial address.

                            Generally this should be disabled for peripheral-to-memory transfers.

0x0000000c.name: DATA_SIZE
0x0000000c.desc: Set the size of each bus transfer (byte/halfword/word). READ_ADDR and WRITE_ADDR advance by this amount (1/2/4 bytes) with each transfer.

0x0000000c.name: HIGH_PRIORITY
0x0000000c.desc:
HIGH_PRIORITY gives a channel preferential treatment in issue scheduling: in each scheduling round, all high priority channels are considered first, and then only a single low priority channel, before returning to the high priority channels.

                            This only affects the order in which the DMA schedules channels. The DMA's bus priority is not changed. If the DMA is not saturated then a low priority channel will see no loss of throughput.

0x0000000c.name: EN
0x0000000c.desc:
DMA Channel Enable. 
                            When 1, the channel will respond to triggering events, which will cause it to become BUSY and start transferring data. When 0, the channel will ignore triggers, stop issuing transfers, and pause the current transfer sequence (i.e. BUSY will remain high if already high)

0x000000cc.name: INCR_WRITE_REV
0x000000cc.desc:
If 1, and INCR_WRITE is 1, the write address is decremented rather than incremented with each transfer.

                            If 1, and INCR_WRITE is 0, this otherwise-unused combination causes the write address to be incremented by twice the transfer size, i.e. skipping over alternate addresses.

0x000000cc.name: INCR_WRITE
0x000000cc.desc:
If 1, the write address increments with each transfer. If 0, each write is directed to the same, initial address.

                            Generally this should be disabled for memory-to-peripheral transfers.

0x000000cc.name: INCR_READ_REV
0x000000cc.desc:
If 1, and INCR_READ is 1, the read address is decremented rather than incremented with each transfer.

                            If 1, and INCR_READ is 0, this otherwise-unused combination causes the read address to be incremented by twice the transfer size, i.e. skipping over alternate addresses.

0x000000cc.name: INCR_READ
0x000000cc.desc:
If 1, the read address increments with each transfer. If 0, each read is directed to the same, initial address.

                            Generally this should be disabled for peripheral-to-memory transfers.

0x000000cc.name: DATA_SIZE
0x000000cc.desc: Set the size of each bus transfer (byte/halfword/word). READ_ADDR and WRITE_ADDR advance by this amount (1/2/4 bytes) with each transfer.

0x000000cc.name: HIGH_PRIORITY
0x000000cc.desc:
HIGH_PRIORITY gives a channel preferential treatment in issue scheduling: in each scheduling round, all high priority channels are considered first, and then only a single low priority channel, before returning to the high priority channels.

                            This only affects the order in which the DMA schedules channels. The DMA's bus priority is not changed. If the DMA is not saturated then a low priority channel will see no loss of throughput.

0x000000cc.name: EN
0x000000cc.desc:
DMA Channel Enable. 
                            When 1, the channel will respond to triggering events, which will cause it to become BUSY and start transferring data. When 0, the channel will ignore triggers, stop issuing transfers, and pause the current transfer sequence (i.e. BUSY will remain high if already high)

0x00000100.name: CH4_READ_ADDR
0x00000100.desc: This register updates automatically each time a read completes. The current value is the next address to be read by this channel.

0x00000104.name: CH4_WRITE_ADDR
0x00000104.desc: This register updates automatically each time a write completes. The current value is the next address to be written by this channel.

0x00000108.name: MODE
0x00000108.desc:
When MODE is 0x0, the transfer count decrements with each transfer until 0, and then the channel triggers the next channel indicated by CTRL_CHAIN_TO.

                            When MODE is 0x1, the transfer count decrements with each transfer until 0, and then the channel re-triggers itself, in addition to the trigger indicated by CTRL_CHAIN_TO. This is useful for e.g. an endless ring-buffer DMA with periodic interrupts.

                            When MODE is 0xf, the transfer count does not decrement. The DMA channel performs an endless sequence of transfers, never triggering other channels or raising interrupts, until an ABORT is raised.

                            All other values are reserved.

0x00000108.name: COUNT
0x00000108.desc:
28-bit transfer count (256 million transfers maximum).

                            Program the number of bus transfers a channel will perform before halting. Note that, if transfers are larger than one byte in size, this is not equal to the number of bytes transferred (see CTRL_DATA_SIZE).

                            When the channel is active, reading this register shows the number of transfers remaining, updating automatically each time a write transfer completes.

                            Writing this register sets the RELOAD value for the transfer counter. Each time this channel is triggered, the RELOAD value is copied into the live transfer counter. The channel can be started multiple times, and will perform the same number of transfers each time, as programmed by most recent write.

                            The RELOAD value can be observed at CHx_DBG_TCR. If TRANS_COUNT is used as a trigger, the written value is used immediately as the length of the new transfer sequence, as well as being written to RELOAD.

0x0000010c.name: AHB_ERROR
0x0000010c.desc: Logical OR of the READ_ERROR and WRITE_ERROR flags. The channel halts when it encounters any bus error, and always raises its channel IRQ flag.

0x0000010c.name: READ_ERROR
0x0000010c.desc:
If 1, the channel received a read bus error. Write one to clear. 
                            READ_ADDR shows the approximate address where the bus error was encountered (will not be earlier, or more than 3 transfers later)

0x0000010c.name: WRITE_ERROR
0x0000010c.desc:
If 1, the channel received a write bus error. Write one to clear. 
                            WRITE_ADDR shows the approximate address where the bus error was encountered (will not be earlier, or more than 5 transfers later)

0x0000010c.name: BUSY
0x0000010c.desc:
This flag goes high when the channel starts a new transfer sequence, and low when the last transfer of that sequence completes. Clearing EN while BUSY is high pauses the channel, and BUSY will stay high while paused.

                            To terminate a sequence early (and clear the BUSY flag), see CHAN_ABORT.

0x0000010c.name: SNIFF_EN
0x0000010c.desc:
If 1, this channel's data transfers are visible to the sniff hardware, and each transfer will advance the state of the checksum. This only applies if the sniff hardware is enabled, and has this channel selected.

                            This allows checksum to be enabled or disabled on a per-control- block basis.

0x0000010c.name: BSWAP
0x0000010c.desc:
Apply byte-swap transformation to DMA data. 
                            For byte data, this has no effect. For halfword data, the two bytes of each halfword are swapped. For word data, the four bytes of each word are swapped to reverse order.

0x0000010c.name: IRQ_QUIET
0x0000010c.desc:
In QUIET mode, the channel does not generate IRQs at the end of every transfer block. Instead, an IRQ is raised when NULL is written to a trigger register, indicating the end of a control block chain.

                            This reduces the number of interrupts to be serviced by the CPU when transferring a DMA chain of many small control blocks.

0x0000010c.name: TREQ_SEL
0x0000010c.desc:
Select a Transfer Request signal. 
                            The channel uses the transfer request signal to pace its data transfer rate. Sources for TREQ signals are internal (TIMERS) or external (DREQ, a Data Request from the system). 
                            0x0 to 0x3a -> select DREQ n as TREQ

0x0000010c.name: CHAIN_TO
0x0000010c.desc:
When this channel completes, it will trigger the channel indicated by CHAIN_TO. Disable by setting CHAIN_TO = _(this channel)_.

                            Note this field resets to 0, so channels 1 and above will chain to channel 0 by default. Set this field to avoid this behaviour.

0x0000010c.name: RING_SEL
0x0000010c.desc:
Select whether RING_SIZE applies to read or write addresses. 
                            If 0, read addresses are wrapped on a (1 << RING_SIZE) boundary. If 1, write addresses are wrapped.

0x0000010c.name: RING_SIZE
0x0000010c.desc:
Size of address wrap region. If 0, don't wrap. For values n > 0, only the lower n bits of the address will change. This wraps the address on a (1 << n) byte boundary, facilitating access to naturally-aligned ring buffers.

                            Ring sizes between 2 and 32768 bytes are possible. This can apply to either read or write addresses, based on value of RING_SEL.

0x0000010c.name: INCR_WRITE_REV
0x0000010c.desc:
If 1, and INCR_WRITE is 1, the write address is decremented rather than incremented with each transfer.

                            If 1, and INCR_WRITE is 0, this otherwise-unused combination causes the write address to be incremented by twice the transfer size, i.e. skipping over alternate addresses.

0x0000010c.name: INCR_WRITE
0x0000010c.desc:
If 1, the write address increments with each transfer. If 0, each write is directed to the same, initial address.

                            Generally this should be disabled for memory-to-peripheral transfers.

                            Generally this should be disabled for peripheral-to-memory transfers.
