                            Note this masking is applied in addition to the masking usually performed by the IN instruction. This is mainly useful for the MOV x, PINS instruction, which otherwise has no way of masking pins.

0x0000010c.name: SIDESET_COUNT
0x0000010c.desc: The number of MSBs of the Delay/Side-set instruction field which are used for side-set. Inclusive of the enable bit, if present. Minimum of 0 (all delay bits, no side-set) and maximum of 5 (all side-set, no delay).

0x0000010c.name: SET_COUNT
0x0000010c.desc: The number of pins asserted by a SET. In the range 0 to 5 inclusive.

0x0000010c.name: OUT_COUNT
0x0000010c.desc: The number of pins asserted by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. In the range 0 to 32 inclusive.

0x0000010c.name: IN_BASE
0x0000010c.desc: The pin which is mapped to the least-significant bit of a state machine's IN data bus. Higher-numbered pins are mapped to consecutively more-significant data bits, with a modulo of 32 applied to pin number.

0x0000010c.name: SIDESET_BASE
0x0000010c.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a side-set operation. The MSBs of an instruction's side-set/delay field (up to 5, determined by SIDESET_COUNT) are used for side-set data, with the remaining LSBs used for delay. The least-significant bit of the side-set portion is the bit written to this pin, with more-significant bits written to higher-numbered pins.

0x0000010c.name: SET_BASE
0x0000010c.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a SET PINS or SET PINDIRS instruction. The data written to this pin is the least-significant bit of the SET data.

0x0000010c.name: OUT_BASE
0x0000010c.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. The data written to this pin will always be the least-significant bit of the OUT or MOV data.

0x00000110.name: INT
0x00000110.desc:
Effective frequency is sysclk/(int + frac/256). 
                            Value of 0 is interpreted as 65536. If INT is 0, FRAC must also be 0.

0x00000114.name: EXEC_STALLED
0x00000114.desc: If 1, an instruction written to SMx_INSTR is stalled, and latched by the state machine. Will clear to 0 once this instruction completes.

0x00000114.name: SIDE_EN
0x00000114.desc: If 1, the MSB of the Delay/Side-set instruction field is used as side-set enable, rather than a side-set data bit. This allows instructions to perform side-set optionally, rather than on every instruction, but the maximum possible side-set width is reduced from 5 to 4. Note that the value of PINCTRL_SIDESET_COUNT is inclusive of this enable bit.

0x00000114.name: SIDE_PINDIR
0x00000114.desc: If 1, side-set data is asserted to pin directions, instead of pin values

0x00000114.name: JMP_PIN
0x00000114.desc: The GPIO number to use as condition for JMP PIN. Unaffected by input mapping.

0x00000114.name: OUT_EN_SEL
0x00000114.desc: Which data bit to use for inline OUT enable

0x00000114.name: INLINE_OUT_EN
0x00000114.desc:
If 1, use a bit of OUT data as an auxiliary write enable 
                            When used in conjunction with OUT_STICKY, writes with an enable of 0 will 
                            deassert the latest pin write. This can create useful masking/override behaviour 
                            due to the priority ordering of state machine pin writes (SM0 < SM1 < ...)

0x00000114.name: OUT_STICKY
0x00000114.desc: Continuously assert the most recent OUT/SET to the pins

0x00000114.name: WRAP_TOP
0x00000114.desc:
After reaching this address, execution is wrapped to wrap_bottom. 
                            If the instruction is a jump, and the jump condition is true, the jump takes priority.

0x00000114.name: WRAP_BOTTOM
0x00000114.desc: After reaching wrap_top, execution is wrapped to this address.

0x00000114.name: STATUS_SEL
0x00000114.desc: Comparison used for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

0x00000114.name: STATUS_N
0x00000114.desc:
Comparison level or IRQ index for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

                            If STATUS_SEL is TXLEVEL or RXLEVEL, then values of STATUS_N greater than the current FIFO depth are reserved, and have undefined behaviour.

0x00000118.name: FJOIN_RX
0x00000118.desc:
When 1, RX FIFO steals the TX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            TX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

0x00000118.name: FJOIN_TX
0x00000118.desc:
When 1, TX FIFO steals the RX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            RX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

0x00000118.name: PULL_THRESH
0x00000118.desc:
Number of bits shifted out of OSR before autopull, or conditional pull (PULL IFEMPTY), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

0x00000118.name: PUSH_THRESH
0x00000118.desc:
Number of bits shifted into ISR before autopush, or conditional push (PUSH IFFULL), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

0x00000118.name: OUT_SHIFTDIR
0x00000118.desc: 1 = shift out of output shift register to right. 0 = to left.

0x00000118.name: IN_SHIFTDIR
0x00000118.desc: 1 = shift input shift register to right (data enters from left). 0 = to left.

0x00000118.name: AUTOPULL
0x00000118.desc: Pull automatically when the output shift register is emptied, i.e. on or following an OUT instruction which causes the output shift counter to reach or exceed PULL_THRESH.

0x00000118.name: AUTOPUSH
0x00000118.desc: Push automatically when the input shift register is filled, i.e. on an IN instruction which causes the input shift counter to reach or exceed PUSH_THRESH.

0x00000118.name: FJOIN_RX_PUT
0x00000118.desc:
If 1, disable this state machine's RX FIFO, make its storage available for random write access by the state machine (using the `put` instruction) and, unless FJOIN_RX_GET is also set, random read access by the processor (through the RXFx_PUTGETy registers).

                            If FJOIN_RX_PUT and FJOIN_RX_GET are both set, then the RX FIFO's registers can be randomly read/written by the state machine, but are completely inaccessible to the processor.

                            Setting this bit will clear the FJOIN_TX and FJOIN_RX bits.

0x00000118.name: FJOIN_RX_GET
0x00000118.desc:
If 1, disable this state machine's RX FIFO, make its storage available for random read access by the state machine (using the `get` instruction) and, unless FJOIN_RX_PUT is also set, random write access by the processor (through the RXFx_PUTGETy registers).

                            If FJOIN_RX_PUT and FJOIN_RX_GET are both set, then the RX FIFO's registers can be randomly read/written by the state machine, but are completely inaccessible to the processor.

                            Setting this bit will clear the FJOIN_TX and FJOIN_RX bits.

0x00000118.name: IN_COUNT
0x00000118.desc:
Set the number of pins which are not masked to 0 when read by an IN PINS, WAIT PIN or MOV x, PINS instruction.

                            For example, an IN_COUNT of 5 means that the 5 LSBs of the IN pin group are visible (bits 4:0), but the remaining 27 MSBs are masked to 0. A count of 32 is encoded with a field value of 0, so the default behaviour is to not perform any masking.

                            Note this masking is applied in addition to the masking usually performed by the IN instruction. This is mainly useful for the MOV x, PINS instruction, which otherwise has no way of masking pins.

0x00000124.name: SIDESET_COUNT
0x00000124.desc: The number of MSBs of the Delay/Side-set instruction field which are used for side-set. Inclusive of the enable bit, if present. Minimum of 0 (all delay bits, no side-set) and maximum of 5 (all side-set, no delay).

0x00000124.name: SET_COUNT
0x00000124.desc: The number of pins asserted by a SET. In the range 0 to 5 inclusive.

0x00000124.name: OUT_COUNT
0x00000124.desc: The number of pins asserted by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. In the range 0 to 32 inclusive.

0x00000124.name: IN_BASE
0x00000124.desc: The pin which is mapped to the least-significant bit of a state machine's IN data bus. Higher-numbered pins are mapped to consecutively more-significant data bits, with a modulo of 32 applied to pin number.

0x00000124.name: SIDESET_BASE
0x00000124.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a side-set operation. The MSBs of an instruction's side-set/delay field (up to 5, determined by SIDESET_COUNT) are used for side-set data, with the remaining LSBs used for delay. The least-significant bit of the side-set portion is the bit written to this pin, with more-significant bits written to higher-numbered pins.

0x00000124.name: SET_BASE
0x00000124.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a SET PINS or SET PINDIRS instruction. The data written to this pin is the least-significant bit of the SET data.

0x00000124.name: OUT_BASE
0x00000124.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. The data written to this pin will always be the least-significant bit of the OUT or MOV data.

0x00000000.name: NEXTPREV_CLKDIV_RESTART
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to restart the clock dividers of state machines in neighbouring PIO blocks, as specified by NEXT_PIO_MASK and PREV_PIO_MASK in the same write.

                            This is equivalent to writing 1 to the corresponding CLKDIV_RESTART bits in those PIOs' CTRL registers.

0x00000000.name: NEXTPREV_SM_DISABLE
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to disable state machines in neighbouring PIO blocks, as specified by NEXT_PIO_MASK and PREV_PIO_MASK in the same write.

                            This is equivalent to clearing the corresponding SM_ENABLE bits in those PIOs' CTRL registers.

0x00000000.name: NEXTPREV_SM_ENABLE
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to enable state machines in neighbouring PIO blocks, as specified by NEXT_PIO_MASK and PREV_PIO_MASK in the same write.

                            This is equivalent to setting the corresponding SM_ENABLE bits in those PIOs' CTRL registers.

                            If both OTHERS_SM_ENABLE and OTHERS_SM_DISABLE are set, the disable takes precedence.

0x00000000.name: NEXT_PIO_MASK
0x00000000.desc:
A mask of state machines in the neighbouring higher-numbered PIO block in the system (or PIO block 0 if this is the highest-numbered PIO block) to which to apply the operations specified by NEXTPREV_CLKDIV_RESTART, NEXTPREV_SM_ENABLE, and NEXTPREV_SM_DISABLE in the same write.

                            This allows state machines in a neighbouring PIO block to be started/stopped/clock-synced exactly simultaneously with a write to this PIO block's CTRL register.

                            Note that in a system with two PIOs, NEXT_PIO_MASK and PREV_PIO_MASK actually indicate the same PIO block. In this case the effects are applied cumulatively (as though the masks were OR'd together).

                            Neighbouring PIO blocks are disconnected (status signals tied to 0 and control signals ignored) if one block is accessible to NonSecure code, and one is not.

0x00000000.name: PREV_PIO_MASK
0x00000000.desc:
A mask of state machines in the neighbouring lower-numbered PIO block in the system (or the highest-numbered PIO block if this is PIO block 0) to which to apply the operations specified by OP_CLKDIV_RESTART, OP_ENABLE, OP_DISABLE in the same write.

                            This allows state machines in a neighbouring PIO block to be started/stopped/clock-synced exactly simultaneously with a write to this PIO block's CTRL register.

                            Neighbouring PIO blocks are disconnected (status signals tied to 0 and control signals ignored) if one block is accessible to NonSecure code, and one is not.

0x00000000.name: CLKDIV_RESTART
0x00000000.desc:
Restart a state machine's clock divider from an initial phase of 0. Clock dividers are free-running, so once started, their output (including fractional jitter) is completely determined by the integer/fractional divisor configured in SMx_CLKDIV. This means that, if multiple clock dividers with the same divisor are restarted simultaneously, by writing multiple 1 bits to this field, the execution clocks of those state machines will run in precise lockstep.

                            Note that setting/clearing SM_ENABLE does not stop the clock divider from running, so once multiple state machines' clocks are synchronised, it is safe to disable/reenable a state machine, whilst keeping the clock dividers in sync.

                            Note also that CLKDIV_RESTART can be written to whilst the state machine is running, and this is useful to resynchronise clock dividers after the divisors (SMx_CLKDIV) have been changed on-the-fly.

0x00000000.name: SM_RESTART
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to instantly clear internal SM state which may be otherwise difficult to access and will affect future execution.

                            Specifically, the following are cleared: input and output shift counters; the contents of the input shift register; the delay counter; the waiting-on-IRQ state; any stalled instruction written to SMx_INSTR or run by OUT/MOV EXEC; any pin write left asserted due to OUT_STICKY.

                            The contents of the output shift register and the X/Y scratch registers are not affected.

0x00000000.name: SM_ENABLE
0x00000000.desc: Enable/disable each of the four state machines by writing 1/0 to each of these four bits. When disabled, a state machine will cease executing instructions, except those written directly to SMx_INSTR by the system. Multiple bits can be set/cleared at once to run/halt multiple state machines simultaneously.

0x00000004.name: TXEMPTY
0x00000004.desc: State machine TX FIFO is empty

0x00000004.name: TXFULL
0x00000004.desc: State machine TX FIFO is full

0x00000004.name: RXEMPTY
0x00000004.desc: State machine RX FIFO is empty

0x00000004.name: RXFULL
0x00000004.desc: State machine RX FIFO is full

0x00000008.name: TXSTALL
0x00000008.desc: State machine has stalled on empty TX FIFO during a blocking PULL, or an OUT with autopull enabled. Write 1 to clear.

0x00000008.name: TXOVER
0x00000008.desc: TX FIFO overflow (i.e. write-on-full by the system) has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that write-on-full does not alter the state or contents of the FIFO in any way, but the data that the system attempted to write is dropped, so if this flag is set, your software has quite likely dropped some data on the floor.

0x00000008.name: RXUNDER
0x00000008.desc: RX FIFO underflow (i.e. read-on-empty by the system) has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that read-on-empty does not perturb the state of the FIFO in any way, but the data returned by reading from an empty FIFO is undefined, so this flag generally only becomes set due to some kind of software error.

0x00000008.name: RXSTALL
0x00000008.desc: State machine has stalled on full RX FIFO during a blocking PUSH, or an IN with autopush enabled. This flag is also set when a nonblocking PUSH to a full FIFO took place, in which case the state machine has dropped data. Write 1 to clear.

0x00000044.name: VERSION
0x00000044.desc: Version of the core PIO hardware.

0x00000044.name: IMEM_SIZE
0x00000044.desc: The size of the instruction memory, measured in units of one instruction

0x00000044.name: SM_COUNT
0x00000044.desc: The number of state machines this PIO instance is equipped with.

0x00000044.name: FIFO_DEPTH
0x00000044.desc:
The depth of the state machine TX/RX FIFOs, measured in words. 
                            Joining fifos via SHIFTCTRL_FJOIN gives one FIFO with double 
                            this depth.

0x000000c8.name: FRAC

0x000000e8.name: FJOIN_RX_GET
0x000000e8.desc:
If 1, disable this state machine's RX FIFO, make its storage available for random read access by the state machine (using the `get` instruction) and, unless FJOIN_RX_PUT is also set, random write access by the processor (through the RXFx_PUTGETy registers).

                            If FJOIN_RX_PUT and FJOIN_RX_GET are both set, then the RX FIFO's registers can be randomly read/written by the state machine, but are completely inaccessible to the processor.

                            Setting this bit will clear the FJOIN_TX and FJOIN_RX bits.

0x000000e8.name: IN_COUNT
0x000000e8.desc:
Set the number of pins which are not masked to 0 when read by an IN PINS, WAIT PIN or MOV x, PINS instruction.

                            For example, an IN_COUNT of 5 means that the 5 LSBs of the IN pin group are visible (bits 4:0), but the remaining 27 MSBs are masked to 0. A count of 32 is encoded with a field value of 0, so the default behaviour is to not perform any masking.

                            Note this masking is applied in addition to the masking usually performed by the IN instruction. This is mainly useful for the MOV x, PINS instruction, which otherwise has no way of masking pins.

0x000000f4.name: SIDESET_COUNT
0x000000f4.desc: The number of MSBs of the Delay/Side-set instruction field which are used for side-set. Inclusive of the enable bit, if present. Minimum of 0 (all delay bits, no side-set) and maximum of 5 (all side-set, no delay).

0x000000f4.name: SET_COUNT
0x000000f4.desc: The number of pins asserted by a SET. In the range 0 to 5 inclusive.

0x000000f4.name: OUT_COUNT
0x000000f4.desc: The number of pins asserted by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. In the range 0 to 32 inclusive.

0x000000f4.name: IN_BASE
0x000000f4.desc: The pin which is mapped to the least-significant bit of a state machine's IN data bus. Higher-numbered pins are mapped to consecutively more-significant data bits, with a modulo of 32 applied to pin number.

0x000000f4.name: SIDESET_BASE
0x000000f4.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a side-set operation. The MSBs of an instruction's side-set/delay field (up to 5, determined by SIDESET_COUNT) are used for side-set data, with the remaining LSBs used for delay. The least-significant bit of the side-set portion is the bit written to this pin, with more-significant bits written to higher-numbered pins.

0x000000f4.name: SET_BASE
0x000000f4.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a SET PINS or SET PINDIRS instruction. The data written to this pin is the least-significant bit of the SET data.

0x000000f4.name: OUT_BASE
0x000000f4.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. The data written to this pin will always be the least-significant bit of the OUT or MOV data.

0x000000f8.name: INT
0x000000f8.desc:
Effective frequency is sysclk/(int + frac/256). 
                            Value of 0 is interpreted as 65536. If INT is 0, FRAC must also be 0.

0x000000f8.name: FRAC
0x000000f8.desc: Fractional part of clock divisor

0x000000fc.name: EXEC_STALLED
0x000000fc.desc: If 1, an instruction written to SMx_INSTR is stalled, and latched by the state machine. Will clear to 0 once this instruction completes.

0x000000fc.name: SIDE_EN
0x000000fc.desc: If 1, the MSB of the Delay/Side-set instruction field is used as side-set enable, rather than a side-set data bit. This allows instructions to perform side-set optionally, rather than on every instruction, but the maximum possible side-set width is reduced from 5 to 4. Note that the value of PINCTRL_SIDESET_COUNT is inclusive of this enable bit.

0x000000fc.name: SIDE_PINDIR
0x000000fc.desc: If 1, side-set data is asserted to pin directions, instead of pin values

0x000000fc.name: JMP_PIN
0x000000fc.desc: The GPIO number to use as condition for JMP PIN. Unaffected by input mapping.

0x000000fc.name: OUT_EN_SEL
0x000000fc.desc: Which data bit to use for inline OUT enable

0x000000fc.name: INLINE_OUT_EN
0x000000fc.desc:
If 1, use a bit of OUT data as an auxiliary write enable 
                            When used in conjunction with OUT_STICKY, writes with an enable of 0 will 
                            deassert the latest pin write. This can create useful masking/override behaviour 
                            due to the priority ordering of state machine pin writes (SM0 < SM1 < ...)

0x000000fc.name: OUT_STICKY
0x000000fc.desc: Continuously assert the most recent OUT/SET to the pins

0x000000fc.name: WRAP_TOP
0x000000fc.desc:
After reaching this address, execution is wrapped to wrap_bottom. 
                            If the instruction is a jump, and the jump condition is true, the jump takes priority.

0x000000fc.name: WRAP_BOTTOM
0x000000fc.desc: After reaching wrap_top, execution is wrapped to this address.

0x000000fc.name: STATUS_SEL
0x000000fc.desc: Comparison used for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

0x000000fc.name: STATUS_N
0x000000fc.desc:
Comparison level or IRQ index for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

                            If STATUS_SEL is TXLEVEL or RXLEVEL, then values of STATUS_N greater than the current FIFO depth are reserved, and have undefined behaviour.

0x00000100.name: FJOIN_RX
0x00000100.desc:
When 1, RX FIFO steals the TX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            TX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

0x00000100.name: FJOIN_TX
0x00000100.desc:

                            RX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

0x00000100.name: PULL_THRESH
0x00000100.desc:
Number of bits shifted out of OSR before autopull, or conditional pull (PULL IFEMPTY), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

0x00000100.name: PUSH_THRESH
0x00000100.desc:

                            Write 0 for value of 32.

0x00000100.name: OUT_SHIFTDIR
0x00000100.desc: 1 = shift out of output shift register to right. 0 = to left.

0x00000100.name: IN_SHIFTDIR
0x00000100.desc: 1 = shift input shift register to right (data enters from left). 0 = to left.

0x00000100.name: AUTOPULL
0x00000100.desc: Pull automatically when the output shift register is emptied, i.e. on or following an OUT instruction which causes the output shift counter to reach or exceed PULL_THRESH.

0x00000100.name: AUTOPUSH
0x00000100.desc: Push automatically when the input shift register is filled, i.e. on an IN instruction which causes the input shift counter to reach or exceed PUSH_THRESH.

0x00000100.name: FJOIN_RX_PUT
0x00000100.desc:

                            Setting this bit will clear the FJOIN_TX and FJOIN_RX bits.

0x00000124.name: SET_COUNT

0x00000000.name: NEXTPREV_CLKDIV_RESTART
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to restart the clock dividers of state machines in neighbouring PIO blocks, as specified by NEXT_PIO_MASK and PREV_PIO_MASK in the same write.

                            This is equivalent to writing 1 to the corresponding CLKDIV_RESTART bits in those PIOs' CTRL registers.

0x00000000.name: NEXTPREV_SM_DISABLE
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to disable state machines in neighbouring PIO blocks, as specified by NEXT_PIO_MASK and PREV_PIO_MASK in the same write.

                            This is equivalent to clearing the corresponding SM_ENABLE bits in those PIOs' CTRL registers.

0x00000000.name: NEXTPREV_SM_ENABLE
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to enable state machines in neighbouring PIO blocks, as specified by NEXT_PIO_MASK and PREV_PIO_MASK in the same write.

                            This is equivalent to setting the corresponding SM_ENABLE bits in those PIOs' CTRL registers.

                            If both OTHERS_SM_ENABLE and OTHERS_SM_DISABLE are set, the disable takes precedence.

0x00000000.name: NEXT_PIO_MASK
0x00000000.desc:
A mask of state machines in the neighbouring higher-numbered PIO block in the system (or PIO block 0 if this is the highest-numbered PIO block) to which to apply the operations specified by NEXTPREV_CLKDIV_RESTART, NEXTPREV_SM_ENABLE, and NEXTPREV_SM_DISABLE in the same write.

                            This allows state machines in a neighbouring PIO block to be started/stopped/clock-synced exactly simultaneously with a write to this PIO block's CTRL register.

                            Note that in a system with two PIOs, NEXT_PIO_MASK and PREV_PIO_MASK actually indicate the same PIO block. In this case the effects are applied cumulatively (as though the masks were OR'd together).

                            Neighbouring PIO blocks are disconnected (status signals tied to 0 and control signals ignored) if one block is accessible to NonSecure code, and one is not.

0x00000000.name: PREV_PIO_MASK
0x00000000.desc:
A mask of state machines in the neighbouring lower-numbered PIO block in the system (or the highest-numbered PIO block if this is PIO block 0) to which to apply the operations specified by OP_CLKDIV_RESTART, OP_ENABLE, OP_DISABLE in the same write.

                            This allows state machines in a neighbouring PIO block to be started/stopped/clock-synced exactly simultaneously with a write to this PIO block's CTRL register.

                            Neighbouring PIO blocks are disconnected (status signals tied to 0 and control signals ignored) if one block is accessible to NonSecure code, and one is not.

0x00000000.name: CLKDIV_RESTART
0x00000000.desc:
Restart a state machine's clock divider from an initial phase of 0. Clock dividers are free-running, so once started, their output (including fractional jitter) is completely determined by the integer/fractional divisor configured in SMx_CLKDIV. This means that, if multiple clock dividers with the same divisor are restarted simultaneously, by writing multiple 1 bits to this field, the execution clocks of those state machines will run in precise lockstep.

                            Note that setting/clearing SM_ENABLE does not stop the clock divider from running, so once multiple state machines' clocks are synchronised, it is safe to disable/reenable a state machine, whilst keeping the clock dividers in sync.

                            Note also that CLKDIV_RESTART can be written to whilst the state machine is running, and this is useful to resynchronise clock dividers after the divisors (SMx_CLKDIV) have been changed on-the-fly.

0x00000000.name: SM_RESTART
0x00000000.desc:
Write 1 to instantly clear internal SM state which may be otherwise difficult to access and will affect future execution.

                            Specifically, the following are cleared: input and output shift counters; the contents of the input shift register; the delay counter; the waiting-on-IRQ state; any stalled instruction written to SMx_INSTR or run by OUT/MOV EXEC; any pin write left asserted due to OUT_STICKY.

                            The contents of the output shift register and the X/Y scratch registers are not affected.

0x00000000.name: SM_ENABLE
0x00000000.desc: Enable/disable each of the four state machines by writing 1/0 to each of these four bits. When disabled, a state machine will cease executing instructions, except those written directly to SMx_INSTR by the system. Multiple bits can be set/cleared at once to run/halt multiple state machines simultaneously.

0x00000004.name: TXEMPTY
0x00000004.desc: State machine TX FIFO is empty

0x00000004.name: TXFULL
0x00000004.desc: State machine TX FIFO is full

0x00000004.name: RXEMPTY
0x00000004.desc: State machine RX FIFO is empty

0x00000004.name: RXFULL
0x00000004.desc: State machine RX FIFO is full

0x00000008.name: TXSTALL
0x00000008.desc: State machine has stalled on empty TX FIFO during a blocking PULL, or an OUT with autopull enabled. Write 1 to clear.

0x00000008.name: TXOVER
0x00000008.desc: TX FIFO overflow (i.e. write-on-full by the system) has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that write-on-full does not alter the state or contents of the FIFO in any way, but the data that the system attempted to write is dropped, so if this flag is set, your software has quite likely dropped some data on the floor.

0x00000008.name: RXUNDER
0x00000008.desc: RX FIFO underflow (i.e. read-on-empty by the system) has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that read-on-empty does not perturb the state of the FIFO in any way, but the data returned by reading from an empty FIFO is undefined, so this flag generally only becomes set due to some kind of software error.

0x00000008.name: RXSTALL
0x00000008.desc: State machine has stalled on full RX FIFO during a blocking PUSH, or an IN with autopush enabled. This flag is also set when a nonblocking PUSH to a full FIFO took place, in which case the state machine has dropped data. Write 1 to clear.

0x000000d0.name: AUTOPULL
0x000000d0.desc: Pull automatically when the output shift register is emptied, i.e. on or following an OUT instruction which causes the output shift counter to reach or exceed PULL_THRESH.

0x000000d0.name: AUTOPUSH
0x000000d0.desc: Push automatically when the input shift register is filled, i.e. on an IN instruction which causes the input shift counter to reach or exceed PUSH_THRESH.

0x000000d0.name: FJOIN_RX_PUT
0x000000d0.desc:
If 1, disable this state machine's RX FIFO, make its storage available for random write access by the state machine (using the `put` instruction) and, unless FJOIN_RX_GET is also set, random read access by the processor (through the RXFx_PUTGETy registers).

                            If FJOIN_RX_PUT and FJOIN_RX_GET are both set, then the RX FIFO's registers can be randomly read/written by the state machine, but are completely inaccessible to the processor.

                            Setting this bit will clear the FJOIN_TX and FJOIN_RX bits.

0x000000d0.name: FJOIN_RX_GET
0x000000d0.desc:
If 1, disable this state machine's RX FIFO, make its storage available for random read access by the state machine (using the `get` instruction) and, unless FJOIN_RX_PUT is also set, random write access by the processor (through the RXFx_PUTGETy registers).

                            If FJOIN_RX_PUT and FJOIN_RX_GET are both set, then the RX FIFO's registers can be randomly read/written by the state machine, but are completely inaccessible to the processor.

                            Setting this bit will clear the FJOIN_TX and FJOIN_RX bits.

0x000000d0.name: IN_COUNT
0x000000d0.desc:
Set the number of pins which are not masked to 0 when read by an IN PINS, WAIT PIN or MOV x, PINS instruction.

                            For example, an IN_COUNT of 5 means that the 5 LSBs of the IN pin group are visible (bits 4:0), but the remaining 27 MSBs are masked to 0. A count of 32 is encoded with a field value of 0, so the default behaviour is to not perform any masking.

                            Note this masking is applied in addition to the masking usually performed by the IN instruction. This is mainly useful for the MOV x, PINS instruction, which otherwise has no way of masking pins.

0x000000dc.name: SIDESET_COUNT
0x000000dc.desc: The number of MSBs of the Delay/Side-set instruction field which are used for side-set. Inclusive of the enable bit, if present. Minimum of 0 (all delay bits, no side-set) and maximum of 5 (all side-set, no delay).

0x000000dc.name: SET_COUNT
0x000000dc.desc: The number of pins asserted by a SET. In the range 0 to 5 inclusive.

0x000000dc.name: OUT_COUNT
0x000000dc.desc: The number of pins asserted by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. In the range 0 to 32 inclusive.

0x000000dc.name: IN_BASE
0x000000dc.desc: The pin which is mapped to the least-significant bit of a state machine's IN data bus. Higher-numbered pins are mapped to consecutively more-significant data bits, with a modulo of 32 applied to pin number.

0x000000dc.name: SIDESET_BASE
0x000000dc.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a side-set operation. The MSBs of an instruction's side-set/delay field (up to 5, determined by SIDESET_COUNT) are used for side-set data, with the remaining LSBs used for delay. The least-significant bit of the side-set portion is the bit written to this pin, with more-significant bits written to higher-numbered pins.

0x000000dc.name: SET_BASE
0x000000dc.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a SET PINS or SET PINDIRS instruction. The data written to this pin is the least-significant bit of the SET data.

0x000000dc.name: OUT_BASE
0x000000dc.desc: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. The data written to this pin will always be the least-significant bit of the OUT or MOV data.

0x000000e0.name: INT
0x000000e0.desc:
Effective frequency is sysclk/(int + frac/256). 
                            Value of 0 is interpreted as 65536. If INT is 0, FRAC must also be 0.

0x000000e0.name: FRAC
0x000000e0.desc: Fractional part of clock divisor

0x000000e4.name: EXEC_STALLED
0x000000e4.desc: If 1, an instruction written to SMx_INSTR is stalled, and latched by the state machine. Will clear to 0 once this instruction completes.

0x000000e4.name: SIDE_EN
0x000000e4.desc: If 1, the MSB of the Delay/Side-set instruction field is used as side-set enable, rather than a side-set data bit. This allows instructions to perform side-set optionally, rather than on every instruction, but the maximum possible side-set width is reduced from 5 to 4. Note that the value of PINCTRL_SIDESET_COUNT is inclusive of this enable bit.

0x000000e4.name: SIDE_PINDIR
0x000000e4.desc: If 1, side-set data is asserted to pin directions, instead of pin values

0x000000e4.name: JMP_PIN
0x000000e4.desc: The GPIO number to use as condition for JMP PIN. Unaffected by input mapping.

0x000000e4.name: OUT_EN_SEL
0x000000e4.desc: Which data bit to use for inline OUT enable

0x000000e4.name: INLINE_OUT_EN
0x000000e4.desc:
If 1, use a bit of OUT data as an auxiliary write enable 
                            When used in conjunction with OUT_STICKY, writes with an enable of 0 will 
                            deassert the latest pin write. This can create useful masking/override behaviour 
                            due to the priority ordering of state machine pin writes (SM0 < SM1 < ...)

0x000000e4.name: OUT_STICKY
0x000000e4.desc: Continuously assert the most recent OUT/SET to the pins

0x000000e4.name: WRAP_TOP
0x000000e4.desc:
After reaching this address, execution is wrapped to wrap_bottom. 
                            If the instruction is a jump, and the jump condition is true, the jump takes priority.

0x000000e4.name: WRAP_BOTTOM
0x000000e4.desc: After reaching wrap_top, execution is wrapped to this address.

0x000000e4.name: STATUS_SEL
0x000000e4.desc: Comparison used for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

0x000000e4.name: STATUS_N
0x000000e4.desc:
Comparison level or IRQ index for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

                            If STATUS_SEL is TXLEVEL or RXLEVEL, then values of STATUS_N greater than the current FIFO depth are reserved, and have undefined behaviour.

0x000000e8.name: FJOIN_RX
0x000000e8.desc:
When 1, RX FIFO steals the TX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            TX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

0x000000e8.name: FJOIN_TX
0x000000e8.desc:
When 1, TX FIFO steals the RX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            RX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

0x000000e8.name: PULL_THRESH
0x000000e8.desc:
Number of bits shifted out of OSR before autopull, or conditional pull (PULL IFEMPTY), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

0x000000e8.name: PUSH_THRESH
0x000000e8.desc:
Number of bits shifted into ISR before autopush, or conditional push (PUSH IFFULL), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

0x000000e8.name: OUT_SHIFTDIR
0x000000e8.desc: 1 = shift out of output shift register to right. 0 = to left.

0x000000e8.name: IN_SHIFTDIR
0x000000e8.desc: 1 = shift input shift register to right (data enters from left). 0 = to left.

0x000000e8.name: AUTOPULL
0x000000e8.desc: Pull automatically when the output shift register is emptied, i.e. on or following an OUT instruction which causes the output shift counter to reach or exceed PULL_THRESH.

0x000000e8.name: AUTOPUSH
0x000000e8.desc: Push automatically when the input shift register is filled, i.e. on an IN instruction which causes the input shift counter to reach or exceed PUSH_THRESH.

0x000000e8.name: FJOIN_RX_PUT
0x000000e8.desc:
If 1, disable this state machine's RX FIFO, make its storage available for random write access by the state machine (using the `put` instruction) and, unless FJOIN_RX_GET is also set, random read access by the processor (through the RXFx_PUTGETy registers).

                            If FJOIN_RX_PUT and FJOIN_RX_GET are both set, then the RX FIFO's registers can be randomly read/written by the state machine, but are completely inaccessible to the processor.

                            Setting this bit will clear the FJOIN_TX and FJOIN_RX bits.

0x000000e8.name: FJOIN_RX_GET
0x000000e8.desc:
If 1, disable this state machine's RX FIFO, make its storage available for random read access by the state machine (using the `get` instruction) and, unless FJOIN_RX_PUT is also set, random write access by the processor (through the RXFx_PUTGETy registers).

                            If FJOIN_RX_PUT and FJOIN_RX_GET are both set, then the RX FIFO's registers can be randomly read/written by the state machine, but are completely inaccessible to the processor.

                            Setting this bit will clear the FJOIN_TX and FJOIN_RX bits.

0x00000000.name: DMA_W
0x00000000.desc: 0 - low priority, 1 - high priority

0x00000000.name: DMA_R
0x00000000.desc: 0 - low priority, 1 - high priority

0x00000000.name: PROC1
0x00000000.desc: 0 - low priority, 1 - high priority

0x00000000.name: PROC0
0x00000000.desc: 0 - low priority, 1 - high priority

0x00000004.name: BUS_PRIORITY_ACK
0x00000004.desc:
Goes to 1 once all arbiters have registered the new global priority levels. 
                            Arbiters update their local priority when servicing a new nonsequential access. 
                            In normal circumstances this will happen almost immediately.

0x0000000c.name: PERFCTR0
0x0000000c.desc:
Busfabric saturating performance counter 0 
                            Count some event signal from the busfabric arbiters, if PERFCTR_EN is set. 
                            Write any value to clear. Select an event to count using PERFSEL0

0x00000010.name: PERFSEL0
0x00000010.desc: Select an event for PERFCTR0. For each downstream port of the main crossbar, four events are available: ACCESS, an access took place; ACCESS_CONTESTED, an access took place that previously stalled due to contention from other masters; STALL_DOWNSTREAM, count cycles where any master stalled due to a stall on the downstream bus; STALL_UPSTREAM, count cycles where any master stalled for any reason, including contention from other masters.

0x00000014.name: PERFCTR1
0x00000014.desc:
Busfabric saturating performance counter 1 
                            Count some event signal from the busfabric arbiters, if PERFCTR_EN is set. 
                            Write any value to clear. Select an event to count using PERFSEL1

0x00000018.name: PERFSEL1
0x00000018.desc: Select an event for PERFCTR1. For each downstream port of the main crossbar, four events are available: ACCESS, an access took place; ACCESS_CONTESTED, an access took place that previously stalled due to contention from other masters; STALL_DOWNSTREAM, count cycles where any master stalled due to a stall on the downstream bus; STALL_UPSTREAM, count cycles where any master stalled for any reason, including contention from other masters.

0x0000001c.name: PERFCTR2
0x0000001c.desc:
Busfabric saturating performance counter 2 
                            Count some event signal from the busfabric arbiters, if PERFCTR_EN is set. 
                            Write any value to clear. Select an event to count using PERFSEL2

0x00000020.name: PERFSEL2
0x00000020.desc: Select an event for PERFCTR2. For each downstream port of the main crossbar, four events are available: ACCESS, an access took place; ACCESS_CONTESTED, an access took place that previously stalled due to contention from other masters; STALL_DOWNSTREAM, count cycles where any master stalled due to a stall on the downstream bus; STALL_UPSTREAM, count cycles where any master stalled for any reason, including contention from other masters.

0x00000024.name: PERFCTR3
0x00000024.desc:
Busfabric saturating performance counter 3 
                            Count some event signal from the busfabric arbiters, if PERFCTR_EN is set. 
                            Write any value to clear. Select an event to count using PERFSEL3

0x00000028.name: PERFSEL3
0x00000028.desc: Select an event for PERFCTR3. For each downstream port of the main crossbar, four events are available: ACCESS, an access took place; ACCESS_CONTESTED, an access took place that previously stalled due to contention from other masters; STALL_DOWNSTREAM, count cycles where any master stalled due to a stall on the downstream bus; STALL_UPSTREAM, count cycles where any master stalled for any reason, including contention from other masters.

0x00000000.name: CPUID
0x00000000.desc: Value is 0 when read from processor core 0, and 1 when read from processor core 1.

0x00000008.name: QSPI_SD
0x00000008.desc: Input value on QSPI SD0 (MOSI), SD1 (MISO), SD2 and SD3 pins

0x00000008.name: QSPI_CSN
0x00000008.desc: Input value on QSPI CSn pin

0x00000008.name: QSPI_SCK
0x00000008.desc: Input value on QSPI SCK pin

0x00000008.name: USB_DM
0x00000008.desc: Input value on USB D- pin

0x00000008.name: USB_DP
0x00000008.desc: Input value on USB D+ pin

0x00000008.name: GPIO
0x00000008.desc: Input value on GPIO32...47

0x00000010.name: GPIO_OUT
0x00000010.desc:
Set output level (1/0 -> high/low) for GPIO0...31. Reading back gives the last value written, NOT the input value from the pins.

                            If core 0 and core 1 both write to GPIO_OUT simultaneously (or to a SET/CLR/XOR alias), the result is as though the write from core 0 took place first, and the write from core 1 was then applied to that intermediate result.

                            In the Non-secure SIO, Secure-only GPIOs (as per ACCESSCTRL) ignore writes, and their output status reads back as zero. This is also true for SET/CLR/XOR aliases of this register.

0x00000014.name: QSPI_SD
0x00000014.desc: Output value for QSPI SD0 (MOSI), SD1 (MISO), SD2 and SD3 pins

0x00000014.name: QSPI_CSN
0x00000014.desc: Output value for QSPI CSn pin

0x00000014.name: QSPI_SCK
0x00000014.desc: Output value for QSPI SCK pin

0x00000014.name: USB_DM
0x00000014.desc: Output value for USB D- pin

0x00000014.name: USB_DP
0x00000014.desc: Output value for USB D+ pin

0x00000014.name: GPIO
0x00000014.desc: Output value for GPIO32...47

0x00000018.name: GPIO_OUT_SET
0x00000018.desc: Perform an atomic bit-set on GPIO_OUT, i.e. `GPIO_OUT |= wdata`

0x00000020.name: GPIO_OUT_CLR
0x00000020.desc: Perform an atomic bit-clear on GPIO_OUT, i.e. `GPIO_OUT &= ~wdata`

0x00000028.name: GPIO_OUT_XOR
0x00000028.desc: Perform an atomic bitwise XOR on GPIO_OUT, i.e. `GPIO_OUT ^= wdata`

0x00000030.name: GPIO_OE

                            In the Non-secure SIO, Secure-only GPIOs (as per ACCESSCTRL) ignore writes, and their output status reads back as zero. This is also true for SET/CLR/XOR aliases of this register.

0x00000050.name: ROE
0x00000050.desc: Sticky flag indicating the RX FIFO was read when empty. This read was ignored by the FIFO.

0x00000050.name: WOF
0x00000050.desc: Sticky flag indicating the TX FIFO was written when full. This write was ignored by the FIFO.

0x00000050.name: RDY
0x00000050.desc: Value is 1 if this core's TX FIFO is not full (i.e. if FIFO_WR is ready for more data)

0x00000050.name: VLD
0x00000050.desc: Value is 1 if this core's RX FIFO is not empty (i.e. if FIFO_RD is valid)

0x000000ac.name: OVERF
0x000000ac.desc: Set if either OVERF0 or OVERF1 is set.

0x000000ac.name: OVERF1
0x000000ac.desc: Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM1 are set.

0x000000ac.name: OVERF0
0x000000ac.desc: Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM0 are set.

0x000000ac.name: BLEND
0x000000ac.desc:
Only present on INTERP0 on each core. If BLEND mode is enabled: 
                            - LANE1 result is a linear interpolation between BASE0 and BASE1, controlled 
                            by the 8 LSBs of lane 1 shift and mask value (a fractional number between 
                            0 and 255/256ths) 
                            - LANE0 result does not have BASE0 added (yields only the 8 LSBs of lane 1 shift+mask value) 
                            - FULL result does not have lane 1 shift+mask value added (BASE2 + lane 0 shift+mask) 
                            LANE1 SIGNED flag controls whether the interpolation is signed or unsigned.

0x000000ac.name: FORCE_MSB
0x000000ac.desc:
ORed into bits 29:28 of the lane result presented to the processor on the bus. 
                            No effect on the internal 32-bit datapath. Handy for using a lane to generate sequence 
                            of pointers into flash or SRAM.

0x000000ac.name: ADD_RAW
0x000000ac.desc: If 1, mask + shift is bypassed for LANE0 result. This does not affect FULL result.

0x000000ac.name: CROSS_RESULT
0x000000ac.desc: If 1, feed the opposite lane's result into this lane's accumulator on POP.

0x000000ac.name: CROSS_INPUT
0x000000ac.desc:
If 1, feed the opposite lane's accumulator into this lane's shift + mask hardware. 
                            Takes effect even if ADD_RAW is set (the CROSS_INPUT mux is before the shift+mask bypass)

0x000000ac.name: SIGNED
0x000000ac.desc:
If SIGNED is set, the shifted and masked accumulator value is sign-extended to 32 bits 
                            before adding to BASE0, and LANE0 PEEK/POP appear extended to 32 bits when read by processor.

0x000000ac.name: MASK_MSB
0x000000ac.desc:
The most-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive) 
                            Setting MSB < LSB may cause chip to turn inside-out

0x000000ac.name: MASK_LSB
0x000000ac.desc: The least-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)

0x000000ac.name: SHIFT
0x000000ac.desc: Right-rotate applied to accumulator before masking. By appropriately configuring the masks, left and right shifts can be synthesised.

0x000000b0.name: FORCE_MSB
0x000000b0.desc:
ORed into bits 29:28 of the lane result presented to the processor on the bus. 
                            No effect on the internal 32-bit datapath. Handy for using a lane to generate sequence 
                            of pointers into flash or SRAM.

0x000000ec.name: OVERF
0x000000ec.desc: Set if either OVERF0 or OVERF1 is set.

0x000000ec.name: OVERF1
0x000000ec.desc: Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM1 are set.

0x000000ec.desc: Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM0 are set.

0x000000ec.name: CLAMP
0x000000ec.desc:
Only present on INTERP1 on each core. If CLAMP mode is enabled: 
                            - LANE0 result is shifted and masked ACCUM0, clamped by a lower bound of 
                            BASE0 and an upper bound of BASE1. 
                            - Signedness of these comparisons is determined by LANE0_CTRL_SIGNED

0x00000190.name: TMDS
0x00000190.desc: IF 1, detach TMDS encoder (of this core) from the Secure SIO, and attach to the Non-secure SIO.

0x00000190.name: INTERP1
0x00000190.desc: If 1, detach interpolator 1 (of this core) from the Secure SIO, and attach to the Non-secure SIO.

0x00000190.name: INTERP0
0x00000190.desc: If 1, detach interpolator 0 (of this core) from the Secure SIO, and attach to the Non-secure SIO.

0x000001a0.name: CORE1_CLR
0x000001a0.desc: Write 1 to atomically clear the core 1 software interrupt flag. Read to get the status of this flag.

0x000001a0.name: CORE0_CLR
0x000001a0.desc: Write 1 to atomically clear the core 0 software interrupt flag. Read to get the status of this flag.

0x000001a0.name: CORE1_SET
0x000001a0.desc: Write 1 to atomically set the core 1 software interrupt flag. Read to get the status of this flag.

0x000001a0.name: CORE0_SET
0x000001a0.desc: Write 1 to atomically set the core 0 software interrupt flag. Read to get the status of this flag.

0x000001a4.name: DBGPAUSE_CORE1
0x000001a4.desc: If 1, the timer pauses when core 1 is in the debug halt state.

0x000001a4.name: DBGPAUSE_CORE0
0x000001a4.desc: If 1, the timer pauses when core 0 is in the debug halt state.

0x000001a4.name: FULLSPEED
0x000001a4.desc: If 1, increment the timer every cycle (i.e. run directly from the system clock), rather than incrementing on the system-level timer tick input.

0x000001a4.name: EN
0x000001a4.desc: Timer enable bit. When 0, the timer will not increment automatically.

0x000001c0.name: CLEAR_BALANCE
0x000001c0.desc: Clear the running DC balance state of the TMDS encoders. This bit should be written once at the beginning of each scanline.

0x000001c0.name: PIX2_NOSHIFT
0x000001c0.desc:
When encoding two pixels's worth of symbols in one cycle (a read of a PEEK/POP_DOUBLE register), the second encoder sees a shifted version of the colour data register.

                            This control disables that shift, so that both encoder layers see the same pixel data. This is used for pixel doubling.

0x000001c0.name: PIX_SHIFT
0x000001c0.desc:
Shift applied to the colour data register with each read of a POP alias register.

                            Reading from the POP_SINGLE register, or reading from the POP_DOUBLE register with PIX2_NOSHIFT set (for pixel doubling), shifts by the indicated amount.

                            Reading from a POP_DOUBLE register when PIX2_NOSHIFT is clear will shift by double the indicated amount. (Shift by 32 means no shift.)

0x000001c0.name: INTERLEAVE
0x000001c0.desc:
Enable lane interleaving for reads of PEEK_SINGLE/POP_SINGLE.

                            When interleaving is disabled, each of the 3 symbols appears as a contiguous 10-bit field, with lane 0 being the least-significant and starting at bit 0 of the register.

                            When interleaving is enabled, the symbols are packed into 5 chunks of 3 lanes times 2 bits (30 bits total). Each chunk contains two bits of a TMDS symbol per lane, with lane 0 being the least significant.

0x000001c0.name: L2_NBITS
0x000001c0.desc: Number of valid colour MSBs for lane 2 (1-8 bits, encoded as 0 through 7). Remaining LSBs are masked to 0 after the rotate.

0x000001c0.name: L1_NBITS
0x000001c0.desc: Number of valid colour MSBs for lane 1 (1-8 bits, encoded as 0 through 7). Remaining LSBs are masked to 0 after the rotate.

0x000001c0.name: L0_NBITS
0x000001c0.desc: Number of valid colour MSBs for lane 0 (1-8 bits, encoded as 0 through 7). Remaining LSBs are masked to 0 after the rotate.

0x000001c0.name: L2_ROT
0x000001c0.desc:
Right-rotate the 16 LSBs of the colour accumulator by 0-15 bits, in order to get the MSB of the lane 2 (red) colour data aligned with the MSB of the 8-bit encoder input.

                            For example, for RGB565 (red most significant), red is bits 15:11, so should be right-rotated by 8 bits to align with bits 7:3 of the encoder input.

0x000001c0.name: L1_ROT
0x000001c0.desc:
Right-rotate the 16 LSBs of the colour accumulator by 0-15 bits, in order to get the MSB of the lane 1 (green) colour data aligned with the MSB of the 8-bit encoder input.

                            For example, for RGB565, green is bits 10:5, so should be right-rotated by 3 bits to align with bits 7:2 of the encoder input.

0x000001c0.name: L0_ROT
0x000001c0.desc:
Right-rotate the 16 LSBs of the colour accumulator by 0-15 bits, in order to get the MSB of the lane 0 (blue) colour data aligned with the MSB of the 8-bit encoder input.

                            For example, for RGB565 (red most significant), blue is bits 4:0, so should be right-rotated by 13 to align with bits 7:3 of the encoder input.

0x000000b0.desc:
The most-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive) 
                            Setting MSB < LSB may cause chip to turn inside-out

0x000000b0.name: MASK_LSB
0x000000b0.desc: The least-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)

0x000000b0.name: SHIFT
0x000000b0.desc: Right-rotate applied to accumulator before masking. By appropriately configuring the masks, left and right shifts can be synthesised.

0x000000ec.name: OVERF
0x000000ec.desc: Set if either OVERF0 or OVERF1 is set.

0x000000ec.name: OVERF1
0x000000ec.desc: Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM1 are set.

0x000000ec.name: OVERF0
0x000000ec.desc: Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM0 are set.

0x000000ec.name: CLAMP
0x000000ec.desc:
Only present on INTERP1 on each core. If CLAMP mode is enabled: 
                            - LANE0 result is shifted and masked ACCUM0, clamped by a lower bound of 
                            BASE0 and an upper bound of BASE1. 
                            - Signedness of these comparisons is determined by LANE0_CTRL_SIGNED

0x000000ec.name: FORCE_MSB
0x000000ec.desc:
ORed into bits 29:28 of the lane result presented to the processor on the bus. 
                            No effect on the internal 32-bit datapath. Handy for using a lane to generate sequence 
                            of pointers into flash or SRAM.

0x000000ec.name: ADD_RAW
0x000000ec.desc: If 1, mask + shift is bypassed for LANE0 result. This does not affect FULL result.

0x000000ec.name: CROSS_RESULT
0x000000ec.desc: If 1, feed the opposite lane's result into this lane's accumulator on POP.

0x000000ec.name: CROSS_INPUT
0x000000ec.desc:
If 1, feed the opposite lane's accumulator into this lane's shift + mask hardware. 
                            Takes effect even if ADD_RAW is set (the CROSS_INPUT mux is before the shift+mask bypass)

0x000000ec.name: SIGNED
0x000000ec.desc:
If SIGNED is set, the shifted and masked accumulator value is sign-extended to 32 bits 
                            before adding to BASE0, and LANE0 PEEK/POP appear extended to 32 bits when read by processor.

0x000000ec.name: MASK_MSB
0x000000ec.desc:
The most-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive) 
                            Setting MSB < LSB may cause chip to turn inside-out

0x000000ec.name: MASK_LSB
0x000000ec.desc: The least-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)

0x000000ec.name: SHIFT
0x000000ec.desc: Right-rotate applied to accumulator before masking. By appropriately configuring the masks, left and right shifts can be synthesised.

0x000000f0.name: FORCE_MSB
0x000000f0.desc:
ORed into bits 29:28 of the lane result presented to the processor on the bus. 
                            No effect on the internal 32-bit datapath. Handy for using a lane to generate sequence 
                            of pointers into flash or SRAM.

0x000000f0.name: ADD_RAW
0x000000f0.desc: If 1, mask + shift is bypassed for LANE1 result. This does not affect FULL result.

0x000000f0.name: CROSS_RESULT
0x000000f0.desc: If 1, feed the opposite lane's result into this lane's accumulator on POP.

0x000000f0.name: CROSS_INPUT
0x000000f0.desc:
If 1, feed the opposite lane's accumulator into this lane's shift + mask hardware. 
                            Takes effect even if ADD_RAW is set (the CROSS_INPUT mux is before the shift+mask bypass)

0x000000f0.name: SIGNED
0x000000f0.desc:
If SIGNED is set, the shifted and masked accumulator value is sign-extended to 32 bits 
                            before adding to BASE1, and LANE1 PEEK/POP appear extended to 32 bits when read by processor.

0x000000f0.name: MASK_MSB
0x000000f0.desc:
The most-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive) 
                            Setting MSB < LSB may cause chip to turn inside-out

0x000000f0.name: MASK_LSB
0x000000f0.desc: The least-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)

0x000000f0.name: SHIFT
0x000000f0.desc: Right-rotate applied to accumulator before masking. By appropriately configuring the masks, left and right shifts can be synthesised.

0x00000190.name: TMDS
0x00000190.desc: IF 1, detach TMDS encoder (of this core) from the Secure SIO, and attach to the Non-secure SIO.

0x00000190.name: INTERP1
0x00000190.desc: If 1, detach interpolator 1 (of this core) from the Secure SIO, and attach to the Non-secure SIO.

0x00000190.name: INTERP0
0x00000190.desc: If 1, detach interpolator 0 (of this core) from the Secure SIO, and attach to the Non-secure SIO.

0x000001a0.name: CORE1_CLR
0x000001a0.desc: Write 1 to atomically clear the core 1 software interrupt flag. Read to get the status of this flag.

0x000001a0.name: CORE0_CLR
0x000001a0.desc: Write 1 to atomically clear the core 0 software interrupt flag. Read to get the status of this flag.

0x000001a0.name: CORE1_SET
0x000001a0.desc: Write 1 to atomically set the core 1 software interrupt flag. Read to get the status of this flag.

0x000001a0.name: CORE0_SET
0x000001a0.desc: Write 1 to atomically set the core 0 software interrupt flag. Read to get the status of this flag.

0x000001a4.name: DBGPAUSE_CORE1
0x000001a4.desc: If 1, the timer pauses when core 1 is in the debug halt state.

0x000001a4.name: DBGPAUSE_CORE0
0x000001a4.desc: If 1, the timer pauses when core 0 is in the debug halt state.

0x000001a4.name: FULLSPEED
0x000001a4.desc: If 1, increment the timer every cycle (i.e. run directly from the system clock), rather than incrementing on the system-level timer tick input.

0x000001a4.name: EN
0x000001a4.desc: Timer enable bit. When 0, the timer will not increment automatically.

0x000001c0.name: CLEAR_BALANCE
0x000001c0.desc: Clear the running DC balance state of the TMDS encoders. This bit should be written once at the beginning of each scanline.

0x000001c0.name: PIX2_NOSHIFT
0x000001c0.desc:
When encoding two pixels's worth of symbols in one cycle (a read of a PEEK/POP_DOUBLE register), the second encoder sees a shifted version of the colour data register.

                            This control disables that shift, so that both encoder layers see the same pixel data. This is used for pixel doubling.

0x000001c0.name: PIX_SHIFT
0x000001c0.desc:
Shift applied to the colour data register with each read of a POP alias register.

                            Reading from the POP_SINGLE register, or reading from the POP_DOUBLE register with PIX2_NOSHIFT set (for pixel doubling), shifts by the indicated amount.

                            Reading from a POP_DOUBLE register when PIX2_NOSHIFT is clear will shift by double the indicated amount. (Shift by 32 means no shift.)

0x000001c0.name: INTERLEAVE
0x000001c0.desc:
Enable lane interleaving for reads of PEEK_SINGLE/POP_SINGLE.

                            When interleaving is disabled, each of the 3 symbols appears as a contiguous 10-bit field, with lane 0 being the least-significant and starting at bit 0 of the register.

                            When interleaving is enabled, the symbols are packed into 5 chunks of 3 lanes times 2 bits (30 bits total). Each chunk contains two bits of a TMDS symbol per lane, with lane 0 being the least significant.

0x000001c0.name: L2_NBITS
0x000001c0.desc: Number of valid colour MSBs for lane 2 (1-8 bits, encoded as 0 through 7). Remaining LSBs are masked to 0 after the rotate.

0x000001c0.name: L1_NBITS
0x000001c0.desc: Number of valid colour MSBs for lane 1 (1-8 bits, encoded as 0 through 7). Remaining LSBs are masked to 0 after the rotate.

0x000001c0.name: L0_NBITS
0x000001c0.desc: Number of valid colour MSBs for lane 0 (1-8 bits, encoded as 0 through 7). Remaining LSBs are masked to 0 after the rotate.

0x000001c0.name: L2_ROT
0x000001c0.desc:
Right-rotate the 16 LSBs of the colour accumulator by 0-15 bits, in order to get the MSB of the lane 2 (red) colour data aligned with the MSB of the 8-bit encoder input.

                            For example, for RGB565 (red most significant), red is bits 15:11, so should be right-rotated by 8 bits to align with bits 7:3 of the encoder input.

0x000001c0.name: L1_ROT
0x000001c0.desc:
Right-rotate the 16 LSBs of the colour accumulator by 0-15 bits, in order to get the MSB of the lane 1 (green) colour data aligned with the MSB of the 8-bit encoder input.

                            For example, for RGB565, green is bits 10:5, so should be right-rotated by 3 bits to align with bits 7:2 of the encoder input.

0x000001c0.name: L0_ROT
0x000001c0.desc:
Right-rotate the 16 LSBs of the colour accumulator by 0-15 bits, in order to get the MSB of the lane 0 (blue) colour data aligned with the MSB of the 8-bit encoder input.

                            For example, for RGB565 (red most significant), blue is bits 4:0, so should be right-rotated by 13 to align with bits 7:3 of the encoder input.

0x00000000.name: TRACE_CAPTURE_FIFO_OVERFLOW
0x00000000.desc: This status flag is set high when trace data has been dropped due to the FIFO being full at the point trace data was sampled. Write 1 to acknowledge and clear the bit.

0x00000000.name: TRACE_CAPTURE_FIFO_FLUSH
0x00000000.desc:
Set to 1 to continuously hold the trace FIFO in a flushed state and prevent overflow.

                            Before clearing this flag, configure and start a DMA channel with the correct DREQ for the TRACE_CAPTURE_FIFO register.

                            Clear this flag to begin sampling trace data, and set once again once the trace capture buffer is full. You must configure the TPIU in order to generate trace packets to be captured, as well as components like the ETM further upstream to generate the event stream propagated to the TPIU.

0x00000004.name: RDATA
0x00000004.desc:
Read from an 8 x 32-bit FIFO containing trace data captured from the TPIU.

                            Hardware pushes to the FIFO on rising edges of clk_sys, when either of the following is true:

                            * TPIU TRACECTL output is low (normal trace data)

                            * TPIU TRACETCL output is high, and TPIU TRACEDATA0 and TRACEDATA1 are both low (trigger packet)

                            These conditions are in accordance with Arm Coresight Architecture Spec v3.0 section D3.3.3: Decoding requirements for Trace Capture Devices

                            The data captured into the FIFO is the full 32-bit TRACEDATA bus output by the TPIU. Note that the TPIU is a DDR output at half of clk_sys, therefore this interface can capture the full 32-bit TPIU DDR output bandwidth as it samples once per active edge of the TPIU output clock.

0x00000000.name: ENDPOINT
0x00000000.desc: Device endpoint to send data to. Only valid for HOST mode.

0x00000000.name: ADDRESS
0x00000000.desc: In device mode, the address that the device should respond to. Set in response to a SET_ADDR setup packet from the host. In host mode set to the address of the device to communicate with.

0x00000050.name: DATA_SEQ_ERROR
0x00000050.desc:
Data Sequence Error.

                            The device can raise a sequence error in the following conditions:

                            * A SETUP packet is received followed by a DATA1 packet (data phase should always be DATA0) * An OUT packet is received from the host but doesn't match the data pid in the buffer control register read from DPSRAM

                            The host can raise a data sequence error in the following conditions:

                            * An IN packet from the device has the wrong data PID

0x00000050.name: ACK_REC
0x00000050.desc: ACK received. Raised by both host and device.

0x00000050.name: STALL_REC
0x00000050.desc: Host: STALL received

0x00000050.name: NAK_REC
0x00000050.desc: Host: NAK received

0x00000050.name: RX_TIMEOUT
0x00000050.desc: RX timeout is raised by both the host and device if an ACK is not received in the maximum time specified by the USB spec.

0x00000050.name: RX_OVERFLOW
0x00000050.desc: RX overflow is raised by the Serial RX engine if the incoming data is too fast.

0x00000050.name: BIT_STUFF_ERROR
0x00000050.desc: Bit Stuff Error. Raised by the Serial RX engine.

0x00000050.name: CRC_ERROR
0x00000050.desc: CRC Error. Raised by the Serial RX engine.

0x00000050.name: ENDPOINT_ERROR
0x00000050.desc: An endpoint has encountered an error. Read the ep_rx_error and ep_tx_error registers to find out which endpoint had an error.

0x00000050.name: BUS_RESET
0x00000050.desc: Device: bus reset received

0x00000050.name: TRANS_COMPLETE
0x00000050.desc:
Transaction complete.

                            Raised by device if:

                            * An IN or OUT packet is sent with the `LAST_BUFF` bit set in the buffer control register

                            Raised by host if:

                            * A setup packet is sent when no data in or data out transaction follows * An IN packet is received and the `LAST_BUFF` bit is set in the buffer control register * An IN packet is received with zero length * An OUT packet is sent and the `LAST_BUFF` bit is set

0x00000050.name: SETUP_REC
0x00000050.desc: Device: Setup packet received

0x00000050.name: CONNECTED
0x00000050.desc: Device: connected

0x00000050.name: RX_SHORT_PACKET
0x00000050.desc: Device or Host has received a short packet. This is when the data received is less than configured in the buffer control register. Device: If using double buffered mode on device the buffer select will not be toggled after writing status back to the buffer control register. This is to prevent any further transactions on that endpoint until the user has reset the buffer control registers. Host: the current transfer will be stopped early.

0x00000050.name: RESUME
0x00000050.desc: Host: Device has initiated a remote resume. Device: host has initiated a resume.

0x00000050.name: VBUS_OVER_CURR
0x00000050.desc: VBUS over current detected

0x00000050.name: SPEED
0x00000050.desc: Host: device speed. Disconnected = 00, LS = 01, FS = 10

0x00000050.name: SUSPENDED
0x00000050.desc: Bus in suspended state. Valid for device and host. Host and device will go into suspend if neither Keep Alive / SOF frames are enabled.

0x00000050.name: LINE_STATE
0x00000050.desc: USB bus line state

0x00000050.name: VBUS_DETECTED
0x00000050.desc: Device: VBUS Detected

TX_FSSLEW=0: Low speed slew rate

0x0000007c.desc:
Output data. TX_DIFFMODE=1, Ignored 
                            TX_DIFFMODE=0, Drives DPM only. TX_DM_OE=1 to enable drive. DPM=TX_DM

0x0000007c.name: TX_DP
0x0000007c.desc:
Output data. If TX_DIFFMODE=1, Drives DPP/DPM diff pair. TX_DP_OE=1 to enable drive. DPP=TX_DP, DPM=~TX_DP 
                            If TX_DIFFMODE=0, Drives DPP only. TX_DP_OE=1 to enable drive. DPP=TX_DP

0x0000007c.name: TX_DM_OE
0x0000007c.desc:
Output enable. If TX_DIFFMODE=1, Ignored. 
                            If TX_DIFFMODE=0, OE for DPM only. 0 - DPM in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPM driving

0x0000007c.name: TX_DP_OE
0x0000007c.desc:
Output enable. If TX_DIFFMODE=1, OE for DPP/DPM diff pair. 0 - DPP/DPM in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPP/DPM driving 
                            If TX_DIFFMODE=0, OE for DPP only. 0 - DPP in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPP driving

0x0000007c.name: DM_PULLDN_EN
0x0000007c.desc: DM pull down enable

0x0000007c.name: DM_PULLUP_EN
0x0000007c.desc: DM pull up enable

0x0000007c.name: DM_PULLUP_HISEL
0x0000007c.desc: Enable the second DM pull up resistor. 0 - Pull = Rpu2; 1 - Pull = Rpu1 + Rpu2

0x0000007c.name: DP_PULLDN_EN
0x0000007c.desc: DP pull down enable

0x0000007c.name: DP_PULLUP_EN
0x0000007c.desc: DP pull up enable

0x0000007c.name: DP_PULLUP_HISEL
0x0000007c.desc: Enable the second DP pull up resistor. 0 - Pull = Rpu2; 1 - Pull = Rpu1 + Rpu2

0x00000084.name: DM_PULLDN_TRIM
0x00000084.desc:
Value to drive to USB PHY 
                            DM pulldown resistor trim control 
                            Experimental data suggests that the reset value will work, but this register allows adjustment if required

0x00000084.name: DP_PULLDN_TRIM
0x00000084.desc:
Value to drive to USB PHY 
                            DP pulldown resistor trim control 
                            Experimental data suggests that the reset value will work, but this register allows adjustment if required

0x00000088.name: DEV_LS_WAKE_FIX
0x00000088.desc: Device - exit suspend on any non-idle signalling, not qualified with a 1ms timer

0x00000088.name: DEV_RX_ERR_QUIESCE
0x00000088.desc: Device - suppress repeated errors until the device FSM is next in the process of decoding an inbound packet.

0x00000088.name: SIE_RX_CHATTER_SE0_FIX
0x00000088.desc:
RX - when recovering from line chatter or bitstuff errors, treat SE0 as the end of chatter as well as 
                            8 consecutive idle bits.

0x00000088.name: SIE_RX_BITSTUFF_FIX
0x00000088.desc:
RX - when a bitstuff error is signalled by rx_dasm, unconditionally terminate RX decode to 
                            avoid a hang during certain packet phases.

0x00000088.name: DEV_BUFF_CONTROL_DOUBLE_READ_FIX
0x00000088.desc:
Device - the controller FSM performs two reads of the buffer status memory address to 
                            avoid sampling metastable data. An enabled buffer is only used if both reads match.

0x00000088.name: MULTI_HUB_FIX
0x00000088.desc: Host - increase inter-packet and turnaround timeouts to accommodate worst-case hub delays.

0x00000088.name: LINESTATE_DELAY
0x00000088.desc: Device/Host - add an extra 1-bit debounce of linestate sampling.

0x00000088.name: RCV_DELAY
0x00000088.desc: Device - register the received data to account for hub bit dribble before EOP. Only affects certain hubs.

0x00000114.name: RESET
0x00000114.desc: Set to 1 to forcibly reset the device state machine on watchdog expiry

0x00000100.name: RESERVED
0x00000100.desc: RESERVED

0x00000100.name: VN_ERR_INT_MASK
0x00000100.desc: 1'b1-mask interrupt, no interrupt will be generated. See RNG_ISR for an explanation on this interrupt.

0x00000100.name: CRNGT_ERR_INT_MASK
0x00000100.desc: 1'b1-mask interrupt, no interrupt will be generated. See RNG_ISR for an explanation on this interrupt.

0x00000100.name: AUTOCORR_ERR_INT_MASK
0x00000100.desc: 1'b1-mask interrupt, no interrupt will be generated. See RNG_ISR for an explanation on this interrupt.

0x00000100.name: EHR_VALID_INT_MASK
0x00000100.desc: 1'b1-mask interrupt, no interrupt will be generated. See RNG_ISR for an explanation on this interrupt.

0x00000104.name: VN_ERR
0x00000104.desc: 1'b1 indicates Von Neuman error. Error in von Neuman occurs if 32 consecutive collected bits are identical, ZERO or ONE.

0x00000104.name: CRNGT_ERR
0x00000104.desc: 1'b1 indicates CRNGT in the RNG test failed. Failure occurs when two consecutive blocks of 16 collected bits are equal.

0x00000104.name: AUTOCORR_ERR
0x00000104.desc: 1'b1 indicates Autocorrelation test failed four times in a row. When set, RNG cease from functioning until next reset.

0x00000104.name: EHR_VALID
0x00000104.desc: 1'b1 indicates that 192 bits have been collected in the RNG, and are ready to be read.

0x00000108.name: VN_ERR
0x00000108.desc: Write 1'b1 - clear corresponding bit in RNG_ISR.

0x00000108.name: CRNGT_ERR
0x00000108.desc: Write 1'b1 - clear corresponding bit in RNG_ISR.

0x00000108.name: AUTOCORR_ERR
0x00000108.desc: Cannot be cleared by SW! Only RNG reset clears this bit.

0x00000108.name: EHR_VALID
0x00000108.desc: Write 1'b1 - clear corresponding bit in RNG_ISR.

0x0000010c.name: RESERVED
0x0000010c.desc: RESERVED

0x0000010c.name: RND_SRC_SEL
0x0000010c.desc: Selects the number of inverters (out of four possible selections) in the ring oscillator (the entropy source).

0x00000110.name: EHR_VALID
0x00000110.desc: 1'b1 indicates that collection of bits in the RNG is completed, and data can be read from EHR_DATA register.

0x00000114.name: EHR_DATA0
0x00000114.desc: Bits [31:0] of Entropy Holding Register (EHR) - RNG output register

0x00000118.name: EHR_DATA1
0x00000118.desc: Bits [63:32] of Entropy Holding Register (EHR) - RNG output register

0x0000011c.name: EHR_DATA2
0x0000011c.desc: Bits [95:64] of Entropy Holding Register (EHR) - RNG output register

0x00000120.name: EHR_DATA3
0x00000120.desc: Bits [127:96] of Entropy Holding Register (EHR) - RNG output register

0x00000124.name: EHR_DATA4
0x00000124.desc: Bits [159:128] of Entropy Holding Register (EHR) - RNG output register

0x00000128.name: EHR_DATA5
0x00000128.desc: Bits [191:160] of Entropy Holding Register (EHR) - RNG output register

0x0000012c.name: RND_SRC_EN
0x0000012c.desc: * 1'b1 - entropy source is enabled. *1'b0 - entropy source is disabled

0x00000130.name: SAMPLE_CNTR1
0x00000130.desc: Sets the number of rng_clk cycles between two consecutive ring oscillator samples. Note! If the Von-Neuman is bypassed, the minimum value for sample counter must not be less then decimal seventeen

0x00000134.name: AUTOCORR_FAILS
0x00000134.desc: Count each time an autocorrelation test fails. Any write to the register reset the counter. Stop collecting statistic if one of the counters reached the limit.

0x00000134.name: AUTOCORR_TRYS
0x00000134.desc: Count each time an autocorrelation test starts. Any write to the register reset the counter. Stop collecting statistic if one of the counters reached the limit.

0x00000138.name: AUTO_CORRELATE_BYPASS
0x00000138.desc: When set, the autocorrelation test in the TRNG module is bypassed.

0x00000138.name: TRNG_CRNGT_BYPASS
0x00000138.desc: When set, the CRNGT test in the RNG is bypassed.

0x00000138.name: VNC_BYPASS
0x00000138.desc: When set, the Von-Neuman balancer is bypassed (including the 32 consecutive bits test).

0x00000140.name: TRNG_SW_RESET
0x00000140.desc: Writing 1'b1 to this register causes an internal RNG reset.

0x000001b4.name: RNG_DEBUG_EN
0x000001b4.desc: * 1'b1 - debug mode is enabled. *1'b0 - debug mode is disabled

0x000001bc.name: RST_BITS_COUNTER
0x000001bc.desc: Writing any value to this address will reset the bits counter and RNG valid registers. RND_SORCE_ENABLE register must be unset in order for the reset to take place.

0x000001c0.name: RESERVED
0x000001c0.desc: RESERVED

0x000001c0.name: RNG_USE_5_SBOXES
0x000001c0.desc: * 1'b1 - 5 SBOX AES. *1'b0 - 20 SBOX AES

0x000001c0.name: RESEEDING_EXISTS
0x000001c0.desc: * 1'b1 - Exists. *1'b0 - Does not exist

0x000001c0.name: KAT_EXISTS
0x000001c0.desc: * 1'b1 - Exists. *1'b0 - Does not exist

0x000001c0.name: PRNG_EXISTS
0x000001c0.desc: * 1'b1 - Exists. *1'b0 - Does not exist

0x000001c0.name: TRNG_TESTS_BYPASS_EN
0x000001c0.desc: * 1'b1 - Exists. *1'b0 - Does not exist

0x000001c0.name: AUTOCORR_EXISTS
0x000001c0.desc: * 1'b1 - Exists. *1'b0 - Does not exist

0x000001c0.name: CRNGT_EXISTS
0x000001c0.desc: * 1'b1 - Exists. *1'b0 - Does not exist

0x000001c0.name: EHR_WIDTH_192
0x000001c0.desc: * 1'b1 - 192-bit EHR. *1'b0 - 128-bit EHR

0x000001e0.name: ROSC_CNTR_VAL
0x000001e0.desc: Reflects the results of RNG BIST counter.

0x000001e4.name: ROSC_CNTR_VAL
0x000001e4.desc: Reflects the results of RNG BIST counter.

0x000001e8.name: ROSC_CNTR_VAL
0x000001e8.desc: Reflects the results of RNG BIST counter.

0x00000004.name: DISARM
0x00000004.desc:
Forcibly disarm the glitch detectors, if they are armed by OTP. Ignored if ARM is YES.

                            This register is Secure read/write only.

0x00000008.name: DET3_INV
0x00000008.desc: Must be the inverse of DET3, else the default value is used.

0x00000008.name: DET2_INV
0x00000008.desc: Must be the inverse of DET2, else the default value is used.

0x00000008.name: DET1_INV
0x00000008.desc: Must be the inverse of DET1, else the default value is used.

0x00000008.name: DET0_INV
0x00000008.desc: Must be the inverse of DET0, else the default value is used.

0x00000008.name: DET3
0x00000008.desc: Set sensitivity for detector 3. Higher values are more sensitive.

0x00000008.name: DET2
0x00000008.desc: Set sensitivity for detector 2. Higher values are more sensitive.

0x00000008.name: DET1
0x00000008.desc: Set sensitivity for detector 1. Higher values are more sensitive.

0x00000008.name: DET0
0x00000008.desc: Set sensitivity for detector 0. Higher values are more sensitive.

0x0000000c.name: LOCK
0x0000000c.desc: Write any nonzero value to disable writes to ARM, DISARM, SENSITIVITY and LOCK. This register is Secure read/write only.

0x00000000.name: NSEC
0x00000000.desc: Non-secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value.

0x00000000.name: SEC
0x00000000.desc: Secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value. This field is read-only to Non-secure code.

0x00000004.name: NSEC
0x00000004.desc: Non-secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value.

0x00000004.name: SEC
0x00000004.desc: Secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value. This field is read-only to Non-secure code.

0x00000008.name: NSEC
0x00000008.desc: Non-secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value.

0x00000008.name: SEC
0x00000008.desc: Secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value. This field is read-only to Non-secure code.

0x0000000c.name: NSEC
0x0000000c.desc: Non-secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value.

0x0000000c.name: SEC
0x0000000c.desc: Secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value. This field is read-only to Non-secure code.

0x00000010.name: NSEC
0x00000010.desc: Non-secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value.

0x00000010.name: SEC
0x00000010.desc: Secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value. This field is read-only to Non-secure code.

0x00000014.name: NSEC
0x00000014.desc: Non-secure lock status. Writes are OR'd with the current value.

0x00000014.name: SEC
