0x00000100.name: EXEC
0x00000100.desc: Execute instruction

0x00000100.name: IS_WR
0x00000100.desc: Payload type is write

0x00000100.name: HAS_PAYLOAD
0x00000100.desc: Instruction has payload (data to be written or to be read)

0x00000100.name: PAYLOAD_SIZE_M1
0x00000100.desc: Instruction payload size in bytes minus 1

0x00000100.name: TARGET
0x00000100.desc: Instruction target, it can be PMC (0x3a) or DAP (0x02)

0x00000100.name: SHORT_WDATA
0x00000100.desc: wdata to be used only when payload_size_m1=0

0x00000124.name: MISO
0x00000124.desc: SBPI MISO (master in - slave out): response from SBPI

0x00000124.name: FLAG
0x00000124.desc: SBPI flag

0x00000124.name: INSTR_MISS
0x00000124.desc: Last instruction missed (dropped), as the previous has not finished running

0x00000124.name: INSTR_DONE
0x00000124.desc: Last instruction done

0x00000124.name: RDATA_VLD
0x00000124.desc: Read command has returned data

0x00000128.name: PD
0x00000128.desc: Power-down; 1 disables current reference. Must be 0 to read data from the OTP.

0x00000128.name: DCTRL
0x00000128.desc:
1 enables USER interface; 0 disables USER interface (enables SBPI).

                            This bit must be cleared before performing any SBPI access, such as when programming the OTP. The APB data read interface (USER interface) will be inaccessible during this time, and will return a bus error if any read is attempted.

0x0000012c.name: CUSTOMER_RMA_FLAG
0x0000012c.desc: The chip is in RMA mode

0x0000012c.name: ROSC_UP
0x0000012c.desc: Ring oscillator is up and running

0x0000012c.name: ROSC_UP_SEEN
0x0000012c.desc: Ring oscillator was seen up and running

0x00000134.name: CNT_FAIL
0x00000134.desc: Flag if the count of address locations with at least one leaky bit exceeds cnt_max

0x00000134.name: CNT_ENA
0x00000134.desc: Enable the counter before the BIST function is initiated

0x00000134.name: CNT_MAX
0x00000134.desc: The cnt_fail flag will be set if the number of leaky locations exceeds this number

0x00000134.name: CNT
0x00000134.desc: Number of locations that have at least one leaky bit. Note: This count is true only if the BIST was initiated without the fix option.

0x00000150.name: MISC
0x00000150.desc:
Enable other debug components. Specifically, the CTI, and the APB-AP used to access the RISC-V Debug Module.

                            These components are disabled by default if either of the debug disable critical flags is set, or if at least one debug key has been enrolled and the least secure of these enrolled key values has not been provided over SWD.

0x00000150.name: PROC1_SECURE
0x00000150.desc:
Permit core 1's Mem-AP to generate Secure accesses, assuming it is enabled at all. Also enable secure debug of core 1 (SPIDEN and SPNIDEN).

                            Secure debug of core 1 is disabled by default if the secure debug disable critical flag is set, or if at least one debug key has been enrolled and the most secure of these enrolled key values not yet provided over SWD.

0x00000150.name: PROC1
0x00000150.desc:
Enable core 1's Mem-AP if it is currently disabled.

                            The Mem-AP is disabled by default if either of the debug disable critical flags is set, or if at least one debug key has been enrolled and the least secure of these enrolled key values has not been provided over SWD.

0x00000150.name: PROC0_SECURE
0x00000150.desc:
Permit core 0's Mem-AP to generate Secure accesses, assuming it is enabled at all. Also enable secure debug of core 0 (SPIDEN and SPNIDEN).

                            Secure debug of core 0 is disabled by default if the secure debug disable critical flag is set, or if at least one debug key has been enrolled and the most secure of these enrolled key values not yet provided over SWD.

                            Note also that core Mem-APs are unconditionally disabled when a core is switched to RISC-V mode (by setting the ARCHSEL bit and performing a warm reset of the core).

0x00000150.name: PROC0
0x00000150.desc:
Enable core 0's Mem-AP if it is currently disabled.

                            The Mem-AP is disabled by default if either of the debug disable critical flags is set, or if at least one debug key has been enrolled and the least secure of these enrolled key values has not been provided over SWD.

                            Note also that core Mem-APs are unconditionally disabled when a core is switched to RISC-V mode (by setting the ARCHSEL bit and performing a warm reset of the core).

0x00000154.name: MISC
0x00000154.desc: Write 1 to lock the MISC bit of DEBUGEN. Can't be cleared once set.

0x00000154.name: PROC1_SECURE
0x00000154.desc: Write 1 to lock the PROC1_SECURE bit of DEBUGEN. Can't be cleared once set.

0x00000154.name: PROC1
0x00000154.desc: Write 1 to lock the PROC1 bit of DEBUGEN. Can't be cleared once set.

0x00000154.name: PROC0_SECURE
0x00000154.desc: Write 1 to lock the PROC0_SECURE bit of DEBUGEN. Can't be cleared once set.

0x00000154.name: PROC0
0x00000154.desc: Write 1 to lock the PROC0 bit of DEBUGEN. Can't be cleared once set.

0x0000015c.name: CORE1
0x0000015c.desc: Current architecture for core 0. Updated on processor warm reset.

0x0000015c.name: CORE0
0x0000015c.desc: Current architecture for core 0. Updated on processor warm reset.

0x00000160.name: NEXT
0x00000160.desc:
This flag always ORs writes into its current contents. It can be set but not cleared by software.

                            The BOOTDIS_NEXT bit is OR'd into the BOOTDIS_NOW bit when the core is powered down. Simultaneously, the BOOTDIS_NEXT bit is cleared. Setting this bit means that the boot scratch registers will be ignored following the next core power down.

                            This flag should be set by an early boot stage that has soft-locked OTP pages, to prevent later stages from unlocking it via watchdog reset.

0x00000160.name: NOW
0x00000160.desc:
When the core is powered down, the current value of BOOTDIS_NEXT is OR'd into BOOTDIS_NOW, and BOOTDIS_NEXT is cleared.

                            The bootrom checks this flag before reading the boot scratch registers. If it is set, the bootrom clears it, and ignores the BOOT registers. This prevents Secure software from diverting the boot path before a bootloader has had the chance to soft lock OTP pages containing sensitive data.

0x000000a8.name: CS1_SIZE
0x000000a8.desc:
The size of the flash/PSRAM device on chip select 1 (addressable at 0x11000000 through 0x11ffffff).

                            A value of zero is decoded as a size of zero (no device). Nonzero values are decoded as 4kiB << CS1_SIZE. For example, four megabytes is encoded with a CS1_SIZE value of 10, and 16 megabytes is encoded with a CS1_SIZE value of 12.

                            When BOOT_FLAGS0_FLASH_DEVINFO_ENABLE is not set, a default of zero is used.

0x000000a8.name: CS0_SIZE
0x000000a8.desc:
The size of the flash/PSRAM device on chip select 0 (addressable at 0x10000000 through 0x10ffffff).

                            A value of zero is decoded as a size of zero (no device). Nonzero values are decoded as 4kiB << CS0_SIZE. For example, four megabytes is encoded with a CS0_SIZE value of 10, and 16 megabytes is encoded with a CS0_SIZE value of 12.

                            When BOOT_FLAGS0_FLASH_DEVINFO_ENABLE is not set, a default of 12 (16 MiB) is used.

0x000000a8.name: D8H_ERASE_SUPPORTED
0x000000a8.desc:
If true, all attached devices are assumed to support (or ignore, in the case of PSRAM) a block erase command with a command prefix of D8h, an erase size of 64 kiB, and a 24-bit address. Almost all 25-series flash devices support this command.

                            If set, the bootrom will use the D8h erase command where it is able, to accelerate bulk erase operations. This makes flash programming faster.

                            When BOOT_FLAGS0_FLASH_DEVINFO_ENABLE is not set, this field defaults to false.

0x000000a8.name: CS1_GPIO
0x000000a8.desc:
Indicate a GPIO number to be used for the secondary flash chip select (CS1), which selects the external QSPI device mapped at system addresses 0x11000000 through 0x11ffffff. There is no such configuration for CS0, as the primary chip select has a dedicated pin.

                            On RP2350 the permissible GPIO numbers are 0, 8, 19 and 47.

                            Ignored if CS1_size is zero. If CS1_SIZE is nonzero, the bootrom will automatically configure this GPIO as a second chip select upon entering the flash boot path, or entering any other path that may use the QSPI flash interface, such as BOOTSEL mode (nsboot).

0x000000ac.name: ACTIVELOW
0x000000ac.desc: LED is active-low. (Default: active-high.)

0x000000ac.name: PIN
0x000000ac.desc: GPIO index to use for bootloader activity LED.

0x000000ae.name: REFDIV
0x000000ae.desc:
PLL reference divisor, minus one.

                            Programming a value of 0 means a reference divisor of 1. Programming a value of 1 means a reference divisor of 2 (for exceptionally fast XIN inputs)

0x000000ae.name: POSTDIV2
0x000000ae.desc: PLL post-divide 2 divisor, in the range 1..7 inclusive.

0x000000ae.name: POSTDIV1
0x000000ae.desc: PLL post-divide 1 divisor, in the range 1..7 inclusive.

0x000000ae.name: FBDIV
0x000000ae.desc: PLL feedback divisor, in the range 16..320 inclusive.

0x000000b0.name: RANGE
0x000000b0.desc: Value of the XOSC_CTRL_FREQ_RANGE register.

0x000000b0.name: STARTUP
0x000000b0.desc: Value of the XOSC_STARTUP register

0x000000e0.name: RISCV_DISABLE
0x000000e0.desc: Permanently disable RISC-V processors (Hazard3)

0x000000e0.name: ARM_DISABLE
0x000000e0.desc: Permanently disable ARM processors (Cortex-M33)

0x00000100.name: GLITCH_DETECTOR_SENS
0x00000100.desc: Increase the sensitivity of the glitch detectors from their default.

0x00000100.name: GLITCH_DETECTOR_ENABLE
0x00000100.desc: Arm the glitch detectors to reset the system if an abnormal clock/power event is observed.

0x00000100.name: BOOT_ARCH
0x00000100.desc: Set the default boot architecture, 0=ARM 1=RISC-V. Ignored if ARM_DISABLE, RISCV_DISABLE or SECURE_BOOT_ENABLE is set.

0x00000100.name: DEBUG_DISABLE
0x00000100.desc: Disable all debug access

0x00000100.name: SECURE_DEBUG_DISABLE
0x00000100.desc: Disable Secure debug access

0x00000100.name: SECURE_BOOT_ENABLE
0x00000100.desc: Enable boot signature enforcement, and permanently disable the RISC-V cores.

0x00000120.name: DISABLE_XIP_ACCESS_ON_SRAM_ENTRY
0x00000120.desc:
Disable all access to XIP after entering an SRAM binary.

                            Note that this will cause bootrom APIs that access XIP to fail, including APIs that interact with the partition table.

0x00000120.name: ENABLE_OTP_BOOT
0x00000120.desc:
Enable OTP boot. A number of OTP rows specified by OTPBOOT_LEN will be loaded, starting from OTPBOOT_SRC, into the SRAM location specified by OTPBOOT_DST1 and OTPBOOT_DST0.

                            The loaded program image is stored with ECC, 16 bits per row, and must contain a valid IMAGE_DEF. Do not set this bit without first programming an image into OTP and configuring OTPBOOT_LEN, OTPBOOT_SRC, OTPBOOT_DST0 and OTPBOOT_DST1.

                            Note that OTPBOOT_LEN and OTPBOOT_SRC must be even numbers of OTP rows. Equivalently, the image must be a multiple of 32 bits in size, and must start at a 32-bit-aligned address in the ECC read data address window.

0x00000120.name: ROLLBACK_REQUIRED
0x00000120.desc: Require binaries to have a rollback version. Set automatically the first time a binary with a rollback version is booted.

0x00000120.name: HASHED_PARTITION_TABLE
0x00000120.desc: Require a partition table to be hashed (if not signed)

0x00000120.name: SECURE_PARTITION_TABLE
0x00000120.desc: Require a partition table to be signed

0x00000120.name: DISABLE_AUTO_SWITCH_ARCH
0x00000120.desc: Disable auto-switch of CPU architecture on boot when the (only) binary to be booted is for the other Arm/RISC-V architecture and both architectures are enabled

0x00000120.name: SINGLE_FLASH_BINARY
0x00000120.desc: Restrict flash boot path to use of a single binary at the start of flash

0x00000120.name: OVERRIDE_FLASH_PARTITION_SLOT_SIZE
0x00000120.desc:
Override the limit for default flash metadata scanning.

                            The value is specified in FLASH_PARTITION_SLOT_SIZE. Make sure FLASH_PARTITION_SLOT_SIZE is valid before setting this bit

0x00000120.name: FLASH_DEVINFO_ENABLE
0x00000120.desc: Mark FLASH_DEVINFO as containing valid, ECC'd data which describes external flash devices.

0x00000120.name: FAST_SIGCHECK_ROSC_DIV
0x00000120.desc: Enable quartering of ROSC divisor during signature check, to reduce secure boot time

0x00000120.name: FLASH_IO_VOLTAGE_1V8
0x00000120.desc:
If 1, configure the QSPI pads for 1.8 V operation when accessing flash for the first time from the bootrom, using the VOLTAGE_SELECT register for the QSPI pads bank. This slightly improves the input timing of the pads at low voltages, but does not affect their output characteristics.

                            If 0, leave VOLTAGE_SELECT in its reset state (suitable for operation at and above 2.5 V)

0x00000120.name: ENABLE_BOOTSEL_NON_DEFAULT_PLL_XOSC_CFG
0x00000120.desc:
Enable loading of the non-default XOSC and PLL configuration before entering BOOTSEL mode.

                            Ensure that BOOTSEL_XOSC_CFG and BOOTSEL_PLL_CFG are correctly programmed before setting this bit.

                            If this bit is set, user software may use the contents of BOOTSEL_PLL_CFG to calculated the expected XOSC frequency based on the fixed USB boot frequency of 48 MHz.

0x00000120.name: ENABLE_BOOTSEL_LED
0x00000120.desc: Enable bootloader activity LED. If set, bootsel_led_cfg is assumed to be valid

0x0000012c.name: DOUBLE_TAP
0x0000012c.desc:
Enable entering BOOTSEL mode via double-tap of the RUN/RSTn pin. Adds a significant delay to boot time, as configured by DOUBLE_TAP_DELAY.

                            This functions by waiting at startup (i.e. following a reset) to see if a second reset is applied soon afterward. The second reset is detected by the bootrom with help of the POWMAN_CHIP_RESET_DOUBLE_TAP flag, which is not reset by the external reset pin, and the bootrom enters BOOTSEL mode (NSBOOT) to await further instruction over USB or UART.

0x0000012c.name: DOUBLE_TAP_DELAY
0x0000012c.desc:
Adjust how long to wait for a second reset when double tap BOOTSEL mode is enabled via DOUBLE_TAP. The minimum is 50 milliseconds, and each unit of this field adds an additional 50 milliseconds.

                            For example, settings this field to its maximum value of 7 will cause the chip to wait for 400 milliseconds at boot to check for a second reset which requests entry to BOOTSEL mode.

                            200 milliseconds (DOUBLE_TAP_DELAY=3) is a good intermediate value.

0x0000012c.name: KEY_INVALID
0x0000012c.desc:
Mark a boot key as invalid, or prevent it from ever becoming valid. The bootrom will ignore any boot key marked as invalid during secure boot signature checks.

                            Each bit in this field corresponds to one of the four 256-bit boot key hashes that may be stored in page 2 of the OTP.

                            When provisioning boot keys, it's recommended to mark any boot key slots you don't intend to use as KEY_INVALID, so that spurious keys can not be installed at a later time.

0x0000012c.name: KEY_VALID
0x0000012c.desc:
Mark each of the possible boot keys as valid. The bootrom will check signatures against all valid boot keys, and ignore invalid boot keys.

                            Each bit in this field corresponds to one of the four 256-bit boot key hashes that may be stored in page 2 of the OTP.

                            A KEY_VALID bit is ignored if the corresponding KEY_INVALID bit is set. Boot keys are considered valid only when KEY_VALID is set and KEY_INVALID is clear.

                            Do not mark a boot key as KEY_VALID if it does not contain a valid SHA-256 hash of your secp256k1 public key. Verify keys after programming, before setting the KEY_VALID bits -- a boot key with uncorrectable ECC faults will render your device unbootable if secure boot is enabled.

                            Do not enable secure boot without first installing a valid key. This will render your device unbootable.

0x00000150.name: CS1_SIZE
0x00000150.desc:
The size of the flash/PSRAM device on chip select 1 (addressable at 0x11000000 through 0x11ffffff).

                            A value of zero is decoded as a size of zero (no device). Nonzero values are decoded as 4kiB << CS1_SIZE. For example, four megabytes is encoded with a CS1_SIZE value of 10, and 16 megabytes is encoded with a CS1_SIZE value of 12.

                            When BOOT_FLAGS0_FLASH_DEVINFO_ENABLE is not set, a default of zero is used.

0x00000150.name: CS0_SIZE
0x00000150.desc:
The size of the flash/PSRAM device on chip select 0 (addressable at 0x10000000 through 0x10ffffff).

                            A value of zero is decoded as a size of zero (no device). Nonzero values are decoded as 4kiB << CS0_SIZE. For example, four megabytes is encoded with a CS0_SIZE value of 10, and 16 megabytes is encoded with a CS0_SIZE value of 12.

                            When BOOT_FLAGS0_FLASH_DEVINFO_ENABLE is not set, a default of 12 (16 MiB) is used.

0x00000150.name: D8H_ERASE_SUPPORTED
0x00000150.desc:
If true, all attached devices are assumed to support (or ignore, in the case of PSRAM) a block erase command with a command prefix of D8h, an erase size of 64 kiB, and a 24-bit address. Almost all 25-series flash devices support this command.

                            If set, the bootrom will use the D8h erase command where it is able, to accelerate bulk erase operations. This makes flash programming faster.

                            When BOOT_FLAGS0_FLASH_DEVINFO_ENABLE is not set, this field defaults to false.

0x00000150.name: CS1_GPIO
0x00000150.desc:
Indicate a GPIO number to be used for the secondary flash chip select (CS1), which selects the external QSPI device mapped at system addresses 0x11000000 through 0x11ffffff. There is no such configuration for CS0, as the primary chip select has a dedicated pin.

                            On RP2350 the permissible GPIO numbers are 0, 8, 19 and 47.

                            Ignored if CS1_size is zero. If CS1_SIZE is nonzero, the bootrom will automatically configure this GPIO as a second chip select upon entering the flash boot path, or entering any other path that may use the QSPI flash interface, such as BOOTSEL mode (nsboot).

0x00000158.name: PIN
0x00000158.desc: GPIO index to use for bootloader activity LED.

0x0000015c.name: REFDIV
0x0000015c.desc:
PLL reference divisor, minus one.

                            Programming a value of 0 means a reference divisor of 1. Programming a value of 1 means a reference divisor of 2 (for exceptionally fast XIN inputs)

0x0000015c.name: POSTDIV2
0x0000015c.desc: PLL post-divide 2 divisor, in the range 1..7 inclusive.

0x0000015c.name: POSTDIV1
0x0000015c.desc: PLL post-divide 1 divisor, in the range 1..7 inclusive.

0x0000015c.name: FBDIV
0x0000015c.desc: PLL feedback divisor, in the range 16..320 inclusive.

0x00000164.name: DP_DM_SWAP
0x00000164.desc: Swap DM/DP during USB boot, to support board layouts with mirrored USB routing (deliberate or accidental).

0x00003e00.name: NO_KEY_STATE
0x00003e00.desc: State when at least one key is registered for this page and no matching key has been entered.

0x00003e00.name: KEY_R
0x00003e00.desc: Index 1-6 of a hardware key which must be entered to grant read access, or 0 if no such key is required.

0x00003e00.name: KEY_W
0x00003e00.desc: Index 1-6 of a hardware key which must be entered to grant write access, or 0 if no such key is required.

0x00003e04.name: LOCK_BL
0x00003e04.desc: Dummy lock bits reserved for bootloaders (including the RP2350 USB bootloader) to store their own OTP access permissions. No hardware effect, and no corresponding SW_LOCKx registers.

0x00003e04.name: LOCK_NS
0x00003e04.desc:
Lock state for Non-secure accesses to this page. Thermometer-coded, so lock state can be advanced permanently from any state to any less-permissive state by programming OTP. Software can also advance the lock state temporarily (until next OTP reset) using the SW_LOCKx registers.

                            Note that READ_WRITE and READ_ONLY are equivalent in hardware, as the SBPI programming interface is not accessible to Non-secure software. However, Secure software may check these bits to apply write permissions to a Non-secure OTP programming API.

0x00003e04.name: LOCK_S
0x00003e04.desc: Lock state for Secure accesses to this page. Thermometer-coded, so lock state can be advanced permanently from any state to any less-permissive state by programming OTP. Software can also advance the lock state temporarily (until next OTP reset) using the SW_LOCKx registers.

0x00003edc.name: LOCK_BL

0x00003edc.name: LOCK_NS
0x00003edc.desc:
Lock state for Non-secure accesses to this page. Thermometer-coded, so lock state can be advanced permanently from any state to any less-permissive state by programming OTP. Software can also advance the lock state temporarily (until next OTP reset) using the SW_LOCKx registers.

                            Note that READ_WRITE and READ_ONLY are equivalent in hardware, as the SBPI programming interface is not accessible to Non-secure software. However, Secure software may check these bits to apply write permissions to a Non-secure OTP programming API.

0x00003f6c.desc: Dummy lock bits reserved for bootloaders (including the RP2350 USB bootloader) to store their own OTP access permissions. No hardware effect, and no corresponding SW_LOCKx registers.

0x00003f6c.name: LOCK_NS
0x00003f6c.desc:
Lock state for Non-secure accesses to this page. Thermometer-coded, so lock state can be advanced permanently from any state to any less-permissive state by programming OTP. Software can also advance the lock state temporarily (until next OTP reset) using the SW_LOCKx registers.

                            Note that READ_WRITE and READ_ONLY are equivalent in hardware, as the SBPI programming interface is not accessible to Non-secure software. However, Secure software may check these bits to apply write permissions to a Non-secure OTP programming API.

0x00003ff8.name: RMA
0x00003ff8.desc: Decommission for RMA of a suspected faulty device. This re-enables the factory test JTAG interface, and makes pages 3 through 61 of the OTP permanently inaccessible.

0x00003ffc.name: LOCK_BL
0x00003ffc.desc: Dummy lock bits reserved for bootloaders (including the RP2350 USB bootloader) to store their own OTP access permissions. No hardware effect, and no corresponding SW_LOCKx registers.

0x00003ffc.name: LOCK_NS
0x00003ffc.desc:
Lock state for Non-secure accesses to this page. Thermometer-coded, so lock state can be advanced permanently from any state to any less-permissive state by programming OTP. Software can also advance the lock state temporarily (until next OTP reset) using the SW_LOCKx registers.

                            Note that READ_WRITE and READ_ONLY are equivalent in hardware, as the SBPI programming interface is not accessible to Non-secure software. However, Secure software may check these bits to apply write permissions to a Non-secure OTP programming API.

0x00003ffc.name: LOCK_S
0x00003ffc.desc: Lock state for Secure accesses to this page. Thermometer-coded, so lock state can be advanced permanently from any state to any less-permissive state by programming OTP. Software can also advance the lock state temporarily (until next OTP reset) using the SW_LOCKx registers.

0x00000000.name: HDLSIM
0x00000000.desc: Indicates the platform is a simulation

0x00000000.name: FPGA
0x00000000.desc: Indicates the platform is an FPGA

0x00000000.name: ASIC
0x00000000.desc: Indicates the platform is an ASIC

0x00000008.name: ENABLE
0x00000008.desc: Enable this endpoint. The device will not reply to any packets for this endpoint if this bit is not set.

0x00000008.name: DOUBLE_BUFFERED
0x00000008.desc: This endpoint is double buffered.

0x00000008.name: INTERRUPT_PER_BUFF
0x00000008.desc: Trigger an interrupt each time a buffer is done.

0x00000008.name: INTERRUPT_PER_DOUBLE_BUFF
0x00000008.desc: Trigger an interrupt each time both buffers are done. Only valid in double buffered mode.

0x00000008.name: INTERRUPT_ON_STALL
0x00000008.desc: Trigger an interrupt if a STALL is sent. Intended for debug only.

0x00000008.name: INTERRUPT_ON_NAK
0x00000008.desc: Trigger an interrupt if a NAK is sent. Intended for debug only.

0x00000008.name: BUFFER_ADDRESS
0x00000008.desc: 64 byte aligned buffer address for this EP (bits 0-5 are ignored). Relative to the start of the DPRAM.

0x00000080.name: FULL_1
0x00000080.desc: Buffer 1 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

0x00000080.name: LAST_1
0x00000080.desc: Buffer 1 is the last buffer of the transfer.

0x00000080.name: PID_1
0x00000080.desc: The data pid of buffer 1.

0x00000080.name: DOUBLE_BUFFER_ISO_OFFSET
0x00000080.desc:
The number of bytes buffer 1 is offset from buffer 0 in Isochronous mode. Only valid in double buffered mode for an Isochronous endpoint. 
                            For a non Isochronous endpoint the offset is always 64 bytes.

0x00000080.name: AVAILABLE_1
0x00000080.desc: Buffer 1 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when writing the status back.

0x00000080.name: LENGTH_1
0x00000080.desc: The length of the data in buffer 1.

0x00000080.name: FULL_0
0x00000080.desc: Buffer 0 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

0x00000080.name: LAST_0
0x00000080.desc: Buffer 0 is the last buffer of the transfer.

0x00000080.name: PID_0
0x00000080.desc: The data pid of buffer 0.

0x00000080.name: RESET
0x00000080.desc: Reset the buffer selector to buffer 0.

0x00000080.name: STALL
0x00000080.desc: Reply with a stall (valid for both buffers).

0x00000080.name: AVAILABLE_0
0x00000080.desc: Buffer 0 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when writing the status back.

0x00000080.name: LENGTH_0
0x00000080.desc: The length of the data in buffer 1.

0x000000e4.desc: Buffer 1 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

0x000000e4.name: LAST_1
0x000000e4.desc: Buffer 1 is the last buffer of the transfer.

0x000000e4.name: PID_1
0x000000e4.desc: The data pid of buffer 1.

0x000000e4.name: DOUBLE_BUFFER_ISO_OFFSET
0x000000e4.desc:
The number of bytes buffer 1 is offset from buffer 0 in Isochronous mode. Only valid in double buffered mode for an Isochronous endpoint. 
                            For a non Isochronous endpoint the offset is always 64 bytes.

0x000000e4.name: AVAILABLE_1
0x000000e4.desc: Buffer 1 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when writing the status back.

0x000000e4.name: LENGTH_1
0x000000e4.desc: The length of the data in buffer 1.
